PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

airtable.readme_md: # Airtable > Flexible database-spreadsheet hybrid for teams ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Database-10B981) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-26-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/gtalarico/pyairtable?style=social)](https://github.com/gtalarico/pyairtable) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/gtalarico/pyairtable) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/Airtable/airtable.js) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Airtable Inc. | | **Category** | Database | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [airtable.com](https://airtable.com) | | **API Docs** | [airtable.com](https://airtable.com/developers/web/api/introduction) | | **Auth** | Personal Access Token, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 5 requests/second per base | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Team from $20/seat/month | --- ## Overview The Airtable API lets you create, read, update, and delete records in your Airtable bases. Manage tables, fields, views, and webhooks. Build data-driven apps, content calendars, and project trackers backed by Airtable's flexible schema. ## Use Cases - Content calendar management - CRM and lead tracking - Project management - Inventory management - Survey and form data collection ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[airtable]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_AIRTABLE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["airtable"], credentials={"airtable": "your-token"}) # List records from an Airtable table result = kit.execute("airtable_list_records", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "fields": "your-fields", "filter_formula": "your-filter_formula"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["airtable"], credentials={"airtable": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["airtable"], credentials={"airtable": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Personal Access Token 1. Create an account at [Airtable](https://airtable.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://airtable.com/create/tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("airtable_list_records", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `batch_create` Batch create records in an Airtable table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | Table name or ID. | | `records` | `array` | Yes | List of dicts, each with a ``fields`` key. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_batch_create", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "records": "your-records"}) ``` --- ### `create_comment` :warning: Create a comment on a record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | The base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | The table name or ID. | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The record ID. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | The comment text. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_create_comment", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "record_id": "your-record_id", "text": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_field` :warning: Create a field in an Airtable table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | | `table_id` | `string` | Yes | Table ID (e.g. ``tblXXXXXXXXXXXXXX``). | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the new field. | | `type` | `string` | Yes | Airtable field type (e.g. ``singleLineText``, ``number``). | | `options` | `string` | No | Optional type-specific configuration dict. | **Returns:** `AirtableField` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_create_field", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_id": "your-table_id", "name": "my-name", "type": "your-type", "options": "your-options"}) ``` --- ### `create_record` Create a record in an Airtable table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | Table name or ID. | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field name to value for the new record. | **Returns:** `AirtableRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_create_record", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ### `create_table` :warning: Create a table in an Airtable base | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the new table. | | `fields` | `array` | Yes | List of field definition dicts (``name``, ``type``, and optional ``options``). | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional description for the table. | **Returns:** `AirtableTable` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_create_table", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "name": "my-name", "fields": "your-fields", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_webhook` :warning: Create a webhook | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | The base ID. | | `notification_url` | `string` | Yes | URL to receive webhook notifications. | | `specification` | `string` | No | Optional filter specification for which changes to notify about. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_create_webhook", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "notification_url": "your-notification_url", "specification": "your-specification"}) ``` --- ### `delete_comment` :warning: Delete a comment on a record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | The base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | The table name or ID. | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The record ID. | | `comment_id` | `string` | Yes | The comment ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_delete_comment", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "record_id": "your-record_id", "comment_id": "your-comment_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_record` :warning: Delete a record from an Airtable table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | Table name or ID. | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | Record ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_delete_record", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "record_id": "your-record_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_records` :warning: Delete multiple records from a table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | Table name or ID. | | `record_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of record IDs to delete (max 10 per call). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_delete_records", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "record_ids": "your-record_ids"}) ``` --- ### `delete_webhook` :warning: Delete a webhook | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | The base ID. | | `webhook_id` | `string` | Yes | The webhook ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_delete_webhook", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "webhook_id": "your-webhook_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_base_schema` Get the schema of an Airtable base | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID (e.g. ``appXXXXXXXXXXXXXX``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_get_base_schema", {"base_id": "your-base_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_base_tables` Get all tables in an Airtable base | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID (e.g. ``appXXXXXXXXXXXXXX``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_get_base_tables", {"base_id": "your-base_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_record` Get a single record from an Airtable table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | Table name or ID. | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | Record ID (e.g. ``recXXXXXXXXXXXXXX``). | **Returns:** `AirtableRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_get_record", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "record_id": "your-record_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_view` Get a specific view | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | The base ID. | | `table_id` | `string` | Yes | The table ID or name. | | `view_id` | `string` | Yes | The view ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_get_view", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_id": "your-table_id", "view_id": "your-view_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_bases` List all accessible Airtable bases **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_list_bases", {}) ``` --- ### `list_collaborators` List collaborators on an Airtable base | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_list_collaborators", {"base_id": "your-base_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_comments` List comments on a record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | The base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | The table name or ID. | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The record ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_list_comments", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "record_id": "your-record_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_records` List records from an Airtable table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | Table name or ID. | | `fields` | `string` | No | List of field names to include in the response. | | `filter_formula` | `string` | No | Airtable formula for filtering records. | | `sort` | `string` | No | List of sort specs, e.g. ``[{"field": "Name", "direction": "asc"}]``. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum records per page (max 100). (default: `100`) | | `offset` | `string` | No | Pagination offset token from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[AirtableRecord]` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_list_records", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "fields": "your-fields", "filter_formula": "your-filter_formula"}) ``` --- ### `list_views` List views in a table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | The base ID. | | `table_id` | `string` | Yes | The table ID or name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_list_views", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_id": "your-table_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_webhook_payloads` List webhook payloads | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | The base ID. | | `webhook_id` | `string` | Yes | The webhook ID. | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from previous response. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_list_webhook_payloads", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "webhook_id": "your-webhook_id", "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_webhooks` List webhooks for a base | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_list_webhooks", {"base_id": "your-base_id"}) ``` --- ### `update_comment` :warning: Update a comment on a record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | The base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | The table name or ID. | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The record ID. | | `comment_id` | `string` | Yes | The comment ID to update. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | New comment text. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_update_comment", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "record_id": "your-record_id", "comment_id": "your-comment_id", "text": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `update_field` Update a field in an Airtable table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | | `table_id` | `string` | Yes | Table ID. | | `field_id` | `string` | Yes | Field ID (e.g. ``fldXXXXXXXXXXXXXX``). | | `name` | `string` | No | New display name for the field. | | `description` | `string` | No | New description for the field. | **Returns:** `AirtableField` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_update_field", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_id": "your-table_id", "field_id": "your-field_id", "name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `update_record` Update a record in an Airtable table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | Table name or ID. | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | Record ID to update. | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field name to new value. | **Returns:** `AirtableRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_update_record", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "record_id": "your-record_id", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ### `update_records` Update multiple records in a table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | Airtable base ID. | | `table_name` | `string` | Yes | Table name or ID. | | `records` | `array` | Yes | List of dicts with ``id`` and ``fields`` keys. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_update_records", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_name": "your-table_name", "records": "your-records"}) ``` --- ### `update_table` :warning: Update a table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `base_id` | `string` | Yes | The base ID. | | `table_id` | `string` | Yes | The table ID. | | `name` | `string` | No | New table name. | | `description` | `string` | No | New table description. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("airtable_update_table", {"base_id": "your-base_id", "table_id": "your-table_id", "name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use batch operations (`batch_create`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls - Rate limit is 5 requests/second per base — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_comment`, `create_field`, `create_table`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses ## Related Connectors - [Firestore](../firestore/) — Document database - [Mongodb](../mongodb/) — NoSQL database - [Redis](../redis/) — Key-value store - [Supabase](../supabase/) — Postgres as a service --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

anthropic.readme_md: # Anthropic > Claude models for safe, capable AI ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Ai_Ml-7C3AED) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-14-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-python?style=social)](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-python) [![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-SDK-3178C6?logo=github)](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-typescript) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Anthropic | | **Category** | Ai Ml | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [anthropic.com](https://anthropic.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.anthropic.com](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by model and tier | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-token (varies by model) | --- ## Overview The Anthropic API provides access to Claude models for text generation, analysis, and tool use. Build AI assistants, automate reasoning tasks, and integrate Claude's capabilities into your applications. Supports streaming, batching, and multi-turn conversations. ## Use Cases - AI assistants and chatbots - Document analysis - Code generation - Research automation - Content creation ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[anthropic]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_ANTHROPIC_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["anthropic"], credentials={"anthropic": "your-token"}) # List uploaded files result = kit.execute("anthropic_list_files", {"limit": 20}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["anthropic"], credentials={"anthropic": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["anthropic"], credentials={"anthropic": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Console 2. Settings 3. API Keys [Get credentials →](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("anthropic_list_files", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `cancel_batch` Cancel a message batch | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `batch_id` | `string` | Yes | The batch ID to cancel. | **Returns:** `AnthropicBatch` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_cancel_batch", {"batch_id": "your-batch_id"}) ``` --- ### `count_tokens` Count tokens for a message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model ID to count tokens for. | | `messages` | `array` | Yes | List of message dicts with 'role' and 'content' keys. | **Returns:** `TokenCount` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_count_tokens", {"model": "your-model", "messages": "your-messages"}) ``` --- ### `create_batch` :warning: Create a message batch | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `requests` | `array` | Yes | List of batch request dicts, each with ``custom_id`` and ``params`` keys. | **Returns:** `AnthropicBatch` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_create_batch", {"requests": "your-requests"}) ``` --- ### `create_message` Create a message with Claude | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model ID to use (e.g., 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514'). | | `messages` | `array` | Yes | List of message dicts with 'role' and 'content' keys. | | `max_tokens` | `integer` | Yes | Maximum number of tokens to generate. | | `system` | `string` | No | System prompt to set context for the conversation. | | `temperature` | `string` | No | Sampling temperature between 0 and 1. | | `tools` | `string` | No | List of tool definitions for function calling. | **Returns:** `AnthropicMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_create_message", {"model": "your-model", "messages": "your-messages", "max_tokens": 10, "system": "your-system", "temperature": "your-temperature"}) ``` --- ### `delete_file` :warning: Delete an uploaded file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_delete_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_message_batch` :warning: Delete a message batch | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `batch_id` | `string` | Yes | The batch ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_delete_message_batch", {"batch_id": "your-batch_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_batch` Get a message batch by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `batch_id` | `string` | Yes | The batch ID. | **Returns:** `AnthropicBatch` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_get_batch", {"batch_id": "your-batch_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_batch_results` Get message batch results | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `batch_id` | `string` | Yes | The batch ID whose results to retrieve. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_get_batch_results", {"batch_id": "your-batch_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_file` Get a file by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_get_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_model` Get a model by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model_id` | `string` | Yes | The model identifier (e.g., ``'claude-sonnet-4-20250514'``). | **Returns:** `AnthropicModel` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_get_model", {"model_id": "your-model_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_batches` List message batches | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of batches to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_list_batches", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `list_files` List uploaded files | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of files to return. (default: `20`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_list_files", {"limit": 20}) ``` --- ### `list_models` List available Anthropic models **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_list_models", {}) ``` --- ### `upload_file` :warning: Upload a file for use across API calls | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `content` | `string` | Yes | The file content as a string. | | `filename` | `string` | Yes | Name for the uploaded file. | | `media_type` | `string` | No | MIME type (e.g., 'text/plain', 'application/pdf'). (default: `text/plain`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("anthropic_upload_file", {"content": "your-content", "filename": "your-filename", "media_type": "text/plain"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use batch operations (`cancel_batch`, `create_batch`, `delete_message_batch`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls - Rate limit is Varies by model and tier — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_batch`, `delete_file`, `delete_message_batch`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Openai](../openai/) — GPT models - [Pinecone](../pinecone/) — Vector database --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

asana.readme_md: # Asana > Work management and project tracking for teams ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Project_Management-8B5CF6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-38-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/Asana/python-asana?style=social)](https://github.com/Asana/python-asana) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/Asana/python-asana) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/Asana/node-asana) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Asana Inc. | | **Category** | Project Management | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [asana.com](https://asana.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.asana.com](https://developers.asana.com/reference/rest-api-reference) | | **Auth** | Personal Access Token, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 150 requests/minute | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Premium from $10.99/user/month | --- ## Overview The Asana API lets you manage tasks, projects, sections, and teams programmatically. Create workflows, track work status, manage assignments, and build custom integrations with Asana's work management platform. ## Use Cases - Task automation - Project tracking dashboards - Cross-tool workflow sync - Sprint management - Team workload reporting ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[asana]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_ASANA_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["asana"], credentials={"asana": "your-token"}) # List tasks in a project result = kit.execute("asana_list_tasks", {"project_gid": "your-project_gid", "limit": 50, "offset": "your-offset"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["asana"], credentials={"asana": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["asana"], credentials={"asana": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Personal Access Token 1. Create an account at [Asana](https://asana.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://app.asana.com/0/my-apps) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("asana_list_tasks", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_comment` :warning: Add a comment to a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The task GID to comment on. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | The plain-text comment body. | **Returns:** `AsanaComment` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_add_comment", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid", "text": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `add_dependencies` :warning: Add task dependencies | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The task GID. | | `dependencies` | `array` | Yes | List of dependency task GIDs. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_add_dependencies", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid", "dependencies": "your-dependencies"}) ``` --- ### `add_followers` :warning: Add followers to a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The task GID. | | `followers` | `array` | Yes | List of user GIDs to add as followers. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_add_followers", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid", "followers": "your-followers"}) ``` --- ### `add_tag` Add a tag to a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The task GID to tag. | | `tag_gid` | `string` | Yes | The tag GID to add. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_add_tag", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid", "tag_gid": "your-tag_gid"}) ``` --- ### `add_task_to_section` :warning: Add a task to a section | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `section_gid` | `string` | Yes | The section GID to add the task to. | | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The task GID to move. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_add_task_to_section", {"section_gid": "your-section_gid", "task_gid": "your-task_gid"}) ``` --- ### `create_project` :warning: Create a new project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `workspace_gid` | `string` | Yes | The workspace GID to create the project in. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the new project. | | `notes` | `string` | No | Optional description/notes for the project. | **Returns:** `AsanaProject` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_create_project", {"workspace_gid": "your-workspace_gid", "name": "my-name", "notes": "your-notes"}) ``` --- ### `create_section` :warning: Create a section in a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_gid` | `string` | Yes | The project GID to add the section to. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the new section. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_create_section", {"project_gid": "your-project_gid", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `create_subtask` :warning: Create a subtask under a parent task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The parent task GID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name/title of the subtask. | **Returns:** `AsanaTask` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_create_subtask", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `create_tag` :warning: Create a tag | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `workspace_gid` | `string` | Yes | The workspace GID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Tag name. | | `color` | `string` | No | Tag color (e.g., 'dark-pink', 'dark-green'). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_create_tag", {"workspace_gid": "your-workspace_gid", "name": "my-name", "color": "your-color"}) ``` --- ### `create_task` :warning: Create a new task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_gid` | `string` | Yes | The project GID to add the task to. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name/title of the task. | | `notes` | `string` | No | Plain-text description of the task. | | `assignee` | `string` | No | GID of the user to assign (or ``"me"``). | | `due_on` | `string` | No | Due date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | **Returns:** `AsanaTask` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_create_task", {"project_gid": "your-project_gid", "name": "my-name", "notes": "your-notes", "assignee": "your-assignee"}) ``` --- ### `create_webhook` :warning: Create a webhook | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource` | `string` | Yes | The GID of the resource to watch (task, project, etc.). | | `target` | `string` | Yes | The URL to receive webhook notifications. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_create_webhook", {"resource": "your-resource", "target": "your-target"}) ``` --- ### `delete_project` :warning: Delete a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_gid` | `string` | Yes | The project GID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_delete_project", {"project_gid": "your-project_gid"}) ``` --- ### `delete_tag` :warning: Delete a tag | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `tag_gid` | `string` | Yes | The tag GID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_delete_tag", {"tag_gid": "your-tag_gid"}) ``` --- ### `delete_task` :warning: Delete a task by GID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The globally unique identifier for the task. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_delete_task", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid"}) ``` --- ### `delete_webhook` :warning: Delete a webhook | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `webhook_gid` | `string` | Yes | The webhook GID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_delete_webhook", {"webhook_gid": "your-webhook_gid"}) ``` --- ### `duplicate_task` :warning: Duplicate a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The task GID to duplicate. | | `name` | `string` | No | Name for the new task. Defaults to 'Copy of {original}'. | | `include` | `string` | No | What to include: 'notes', 'assignee', 'subtasks', 'attachments', 'tags', 'followers', 'projects', 'dates', 'dependencies', 'parent'. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_duplicate_task", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid", "name": "my-name", "include": "your-include"}) ``` --- ### `get_me` Get the current user **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_get_me", {}) ``` --- ### `get_project` Get a single project by GID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_gid` | `string` | Yes | The globally unique identifier for the project. | **Returns:** `AsanaProject` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_get_project", {"project_gid": "your-project_gid"}) ``` --- ### `get_task` Get a single task by GID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The globally unique identifier for the task. | **Returns:** `AsanaTask` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_get_task", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid"}) ``` --- ### `get_task_count` Get task count for a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_gid` | `string` | Yes | The project GID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_get_task_count", {"project_gid": "your-project_gid"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a user by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_gid` | `string` | Yes | The user GID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_get_user", {"user_gid": "your-user_gid"}) ``` --- ### `get_workspace` Get a workspace by GID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `workspace_gid` | `string` | Yes | The workspace GID. | **Returns:** `AsanaWorkspace` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_get_workspace", {"workspace_gid": "your-workspace_gid"}) ``` --- ### `list_attachments` List attachments on a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The task GID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_attachments", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid"}) ``` --- ### `list_custom_fields` List custom fields in a workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `workspace_gid` | `string` | Yes | The workspace GID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum fields to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_custom_fields", {"workspace_gid": "your-workspace_gid", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_projects` List projects in a workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `workspace_gid` | `string` | Yes | The workspace GID to list projects from. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `50`) | | `offset` | `string` | No | Pagination offset token from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[AsanaProject]` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_projects", {"workspace_gid": "your-workspace_gid", "limit": 50, "offset": "your-offset"}) ``` --- ### `list_sections` List sections in a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_gid` | `string` | Yes | The project GID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_sections", {"project_gid": "your-project_gid"}) ``` --- ### `list_stories` List stories (activity) on a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The task GID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_stories", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid"}) ``` --- ### `list_subtasks` List subtasks of a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The parent task GID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_subtasks", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid"}) ``` --- ### `list_tags` List tags in a workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `workspace_gid` | `string` | Yes | The workspace GID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_tags", {"workspace_gid": "your-workspace_gid"}) ``` --- ### `list_tasks` List tasks in a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_gid` | `string` | Yes | The globally unique identifier for the project. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `50`) | | `offset` | `string` | No | Pagination offset token from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[AsanaTask]` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_tasks", {"project_gid": "your-project_gid", "limit": 50, "offset": "your-offset"}) ``` --- ### `list_teams` List teams in a workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `workspace_gid` | `string` | Yes | The workspace or organization GID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_teams", {"workspace_gid": "your-workspace_gid"}) ``` --- ### `list_users` List users in a workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `workspace_gid` | `string` | Yes | The workspace GID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum users to return. (default: `50`) | | `offset` | `string` | No | Pagination offset token. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[dict[str, Any]]` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_users", {"workspace_gid": "your-workspace_gid", "limit": 50, "offset": "your-offset"}) ``` --- ### `list_webhooks` List webhooks | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `workspace_gid` | `string` | Yes | The workspace GID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_webhooks", {"workspace_gid": "your-workspace_gid"}) ``` --- ### `list_workspaces` List workspaces accessible to the user **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_list_workspaces", {}) ``` --- ### `search_tasks` Search tasks in a workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `workspace_gid` | `string` | Yes | The workspace GID to search in. | | `text` | `string` | No | Text to search in task names and descriptions. | | `assignee` | `string` | No | Filter by assignee GID or 'me'. | | `completed` | `string` | No | Filter by completion status. | | `is_subtask` | `string` | No | Filter subtasks vs top-level tasks. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results to return. (default: `20`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_search_tasks", {"workspace_gid": "your-workspace_gid", "text": "Hello!", "assignee": "your-assignee"}) ``` --- ### `set_parent` :warning: Set parent for a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The task GID to move. | | `parent` | `string` | Yes | Parent task GID, or empty string to make top-level. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_set_parent", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid", "parent": "your-parent"}) ``` --- ### `update_project` :warning: Update a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_gid` | `string` | Yes | The project GID to update. | | `name` | `string` | No | New name for the project. | | `notes` | `string` | No | New description/notes for the project. | | `color` | `string` | No | New colour tag (e.g. ``"dark-green"``). | **Returns:** `AsanaProject` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_update_project", {"project_gid": "your-project_gid", "name": "my-name", "notes": "your-notes"}) ``` --- ### `update_task` Update an existing task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_gid` | `string` | Yes | The task GID to update. | | `name` | `string` | No | New name/title for the task. | | `notes` | `string` | No | New plain-text description. | | `completed` | `string` | No | Whether the task is completed. | | `due_on` | `string` | No | New due date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | **Returns:** `AsanaTask` ```python result = kit.execute("asana_update_task", {"task_gid": "your-task_gid", "name": "my-name", "notes": "your-notes"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_tasks` for filtered queries and `list_attachments` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 150 requests/minute — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_comment`, `add_dependencies`, `add_followers`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 38 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Jira](../jira/) — Issue tracking - [Linear](../linear/) — Modern issue tracker - [Trello](../trello/) — Kanban boards --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

auth0.readme_md: # Auth0 > Identity platform for authentication and authorization ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Security-059669) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-27-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/auth0/auth0-python?style=social)](https://github.com/auth0/auth0-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/auth0/auth0-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/auth0/node-auth0) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Okta (Auth0) | | **Category** | Security | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [auth0.com](https://auth0.com) | | **API Docs** | [auth0.com](https://auth0.com/docs/api/management/v2) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token (Management API Token) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by endpoint (typically 50-100/sec) | | **Pricing** | Free up to 7,500 MAUs, Professional from $240/month | --- ## Overview The Auth0 Management API lets you manage users, connections, roles, and applications in your Auth0 tenant. Configure authentication flows, manage user profiles, assign permissions, and monitor security events. ## Use Cases - User management - Role-based access control - SSO configuration - Passwordless authentication - Security auditing ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[auth0]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_AUTH0_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["auth0"], credentials={"auth0": "your-token"}) # List users in the Auth0 tenant result = kit.execute("auth0_list_users", {"search": "your-search", "limit": 50}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["auth0"], credentials={"auth0": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["auth0"], credentials={"auth0": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token (Management API Token) 1. Create an account at [Auth0](https://auth0.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://manage.auth0.com/#/apis/management/explorer) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("auth0_list_users", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `assign_permissions` :warning: Assign permissions to a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Auth0 ID. | | `permissions` | `array` | Yes | List of permission dicts to assign. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_assign_permissions", {"user_id": "user-123", "permissions": "your-permissions"}) ``` --- ### `assign_role` Assign roles to a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Auth0 ID. | | `role_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of role IDs to assign. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_assign_role", {"user_id": "user-123", "role_ids": "your-role_ids"}) ``` --- ### `block_user` :warning: Block a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Auth0 ID. | **Returns:** `Auth0User` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_block_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `create_connection` :warning: Create a connection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Connection name. | | `strategy` | `string` | Yes | Connection strategy (e.g., 'auth0', 'google-oauth2'). | | `enabled_clients` | `string` | No | Client IDs allowed to use this connection. | **Returns:** `Auth0Connection` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_create_connection", {"name": "my-name", "strategy": "your-strategy", "enabled_clients": "your-enabled_clients"}) ``` --- ### `create_role` :warning: Create a role | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Role name. | | `description` | `string` | No | Role description. | **Returns:** `Auth0Role` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_create_role", {"name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_user` Create a new user in Auth0 | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | Yes | The user's email address. | | `password` | `string` | Yes | The user's password. | | `connection` | `string` | Yes | Database connection name (e.g. ``Username-Password-Authentication``). | **Returns:** `Auth0User` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_create_user", {"email": "user@example.com", "password": "your-password", "connection": "your-connection"}) ``` --- ### `delete_connection` :warning: Delete a connection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `connection_id` | `string` | Yes | The connection ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_delete_connection", {"connection_id": "your-connection_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_role` :warning: Delete a role | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `role_id` | `string` | Yes | The role ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_delete_role", {"role_id": "your-role_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_user` :warning: Delete a user from Auth0 | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Auth0 ID. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_delete_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_client` Get a single client by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `client_id` | `string` | Yes | The Auth0 client/application ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_get_client", {"client_id": "your-client_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_connection` Get a connection by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `connection_id` | `string` | Yes | The connection ID. | **Returns:** `Auth0Connection` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_get_connection", {"connection_id": "your-connection_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_daily_stats` Get daily stats | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date (YYYYMMDD format). | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date (YYYYMMDD format). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_get_daily_stats", {"from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date"}) ``` --- ### `get_log` Get a log event by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `log_id` | `string` | Yes | The log event ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_get_log", {"log_id": "your-log_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_role` Get a role by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `role_id` | `string` | Yes | The role ID. | **Returns:** `Auth0Role` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_get_role", {"role_id": "your-role_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_tenant_settings` Get tenant settings **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_get_tenant_settings", {}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a single user by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Auth0 ID (e.g. ``auth0|abc123``). | **Returns:** `Auth0User` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_get_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_user_permissions` Get permissions assigned to a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Auth0 ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_get_user_permissions", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_clients` List clients (applications) in the tenant | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of clients to return (max 100). (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_list_clients", {"limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_connections` List connections in the Auth0 tenant | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of connections to return (max 100). (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Auth0Connection]` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_list_connections", {"limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_logs` List log events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `per_page` | `integer` | No | Number of log entries per page. (default: `50`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (0-based). (default: `0`) | | `query` | `string` | No | Lucene query string to filter logs. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_list_logs", {"per_page": 50, "page": 0}) ``` --- ### `list_organizations` List organizations in the tenant | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of organizations to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_list_organizations", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `list_role_users` List users assigned to a role | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `role_id` | `string` | Yes | The role ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum users to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_list_role_users", {"role_id": "your-role_id", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_roles` List roles in the Auth0 tenant | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of roles to return (max 100). (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Auth0Role]` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_list_roles", {"limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_user_roles` List roles assigned to a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Auth0 ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_list_user_roles", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_users` List users in the Auth0 tenant | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `search` | `string` | No | Lucene query string for user search. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Number of users per page (max 100). (default: `50`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page index (zero-based). (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Auth0User]` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_list_users", {"search": "your-search", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `unblock_user` Unblock a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Auth0 ID. | **Returns:** `Auth0User` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_unblock_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `update_user` Update an existing user in Auth0 | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Auth0 ID. | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Fields to update (e.g. name, email, blocked, etc.). | **Returns:** `Auth0User` ```python result = kit.execute("auth0_update_user", {"user_id": "user-123", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by endpoint (typically 50-100/sec) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`assign_permissions`, `block_user`, `create_connection`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 27 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Okta](../okta/) — Identity management --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

calendly.readme_md: # Calendly > Scheduling automation for meetings and events ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Productivity-F97316) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-20-purple) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Calendly LLC | | **Category** | Productivity | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [calendly.com](https://calendly.com) | | **API Docs** | [developer.calendly.com](https://developer.calendly.com/api-docs) | | **Auth** | Personal Access Token, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 200 requests/minute | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Standard from $10/seat/month | --- ## Overview The Calendly API provides access to event types, scheduled events, invitees, and scheduling links. Automate meeting scheduling, sync calendar data, manage availability, and build custom booking experiences. ## Use Cases - Meeting scheduling automation - Calendar sync - Event analytics - Custom booking pages - Sales pipeline integration ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[calendly]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_CALENDLY_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["calendly"], credentials={"calendly": "your-token"}) # Get the current authenticated user result = kit.execute("calendly_get_current_user", {}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["calendly"], credentials={"calendly": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["calendly"], credentials={"calendly": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Personal Access Token 1. Create an account at [Calendly](https://calendly.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://calendly.com/integrations/api_webhooks) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("calendly_get_current_user", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `cancel_event` :warning: Cancel a scheduled event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the scheduled event. | | `reason` | `string` | No | Optional cancellation reason. | **Returns:** `CalendlyEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_cancel_event", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid", "reason": "your-reason"}) ``` --- ### `cancel_invitee` :warning: Cancel a scheduled event invitee | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the scheduled event. | | `reason` | `string` | No | Optional cancellation reason. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_cancel_invitee", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid", "reason": "your-reason"}) ``` --- ### `create_invitee` :warning: Schedule a meeting (create invitee) | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The event UUID to schedule for. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | Invitee's email address. | | `first_name` | `string` | No | Invitee's first name. | | `last_name` | `string` | No | Invitee's last name. | | `timezone` | `string` | No | Invitee's timezone (IANA format). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_create_invitee", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid", "email": "user@example.com", "first_name": "your-first_name", "last_name": "your-last_name"}) ``` --- ### `create_webhook` :warning: Create a webhook subscription | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `url` | `string` | Yes | The callback URL for webhook delivery. | | `events` | `array` | Yes | List of event types to subscribe to (e.g. ``["invitee.created", "invitee.canceled"]``). | | `organization_uri` | `string` | Yes | The Calendly organization URI. | | `scope` | `string` | No | Webhook scope (``"organization"`` or ``"user"``). (default: `organization`) | **Returns:** `CalendlyWebhook` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_create_webhook", {"url": "https://example.com", "events": "your-events", "organization_uri": "your-organization_uri", "scope": "organization"}) ``` --- ### `delete_webhook` :warning: Delete a webhook subscription | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `webhook_id` | `string` | Yes | The webhook UUID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_delete_webhook", {"webhook_id": "your-webhook_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_current_user` Get the current authenticated user **Returns:** `CalendlyUser` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_current_user", {}) ``` --- ### `get_event` Get a single scheduled event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the scheduled event. | **Returns:** `CalendlyEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_event", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid"}) ``` --- ### `get_event_type` Get a single event type by UUID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_type_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the event type. | **Returns:** `CalendlyEventType` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_event_type", {"event_type_uuid": "your-event_type_uuid"}) ``` --- ### `get_invitee` Get a single invitee for a scheduled event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the scheduled event. | | `invitee_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the invitee. | **Returns:** `CalendlyInvitee` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_invitee", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid", "invitee_uuid": "your-invitee_uuid"}) ``` --- ### `get_organization` Get organization details **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_organization", {}) ``` --- ### `get_user_busy_times` Get user busy times | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_uri` | `string` | Yes | The user's URI. | | `start_time` | `string` | Yes | Start of range (ISO 8601 format). | | `end_time` | `string` | Yes | End of range (ISO 8601 format). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_user_busy_times", {"user_uri": "your-user_uri", "start_time": "your-start_time", "end_time": "your-end_time"}) ``` --- ### `list_activity_log` List activity log events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `organization_uri` | `string` | Yes | The organization URI. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum entries to return. (default: `20`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[dict[str, Any]]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_activity_log", {"organization_uri": "your-organization_uri", "limit": 20, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_availability_schedules` List user availability schedules | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_uri` | `string` | Yes | The user's URI (e.g., from get_current_user). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_availability_schedules", {"user_uri": "your-user_uri"}) ``` --- ### `list_available_times` List available times for an event type | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_type_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the event type. | | `start` | `string` | No | ISO 8601 start of availability window. | | `end` | `string` | No | ISO 8601 end of availability window. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_available_times", {"event_type_uuid": "your-event_type_uuid", "start": "your-start", "end": "your-end"}) ``` --- ### `list_event_types` List event types for a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_uri` | `string` | Yes | The Calendly user URI to list event types for. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendlyEventType]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_event_types", {"user_uri": "your-user_uri", "limit": 20, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `list_invitees` List invitees for a scheduled event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the scheduled event. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendlyInvitee]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_invitees", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid", "limit": 20, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `list_organization_members` List organization members | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `organization_uri` | `string` | Yes | The Calendly organization URI. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendlyOrganizationMembership]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_organization_members", {"organization_uri": "your-organization_uri", "limit": 20, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `list_routing_forms` List routing forms **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_routing_forms", {}) ``` --- ### `list_scheduled_events` List scheduled events for a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_uri` | `string` | Yes | The Calendly user URI. | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (``"active"`` or ``"canceled"``). | | `min_start_time` | `string` | No | ISO 8601 lower bound on event start time. | | `max_start_time` | `string` | No | ISO 8601 upper bound on event start time. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendlyEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_scheduled_events", {"user_uri": "your-user_uri", "status": "active", "min_start_time": "your-min_start_time"}) ``` --- ### `list_webhooks` List webhook subscriptions | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `organization_uri` | `string` | Yes | The Calendly organization URI. | | `scope` | `string` | No | Webhook scope (``"organization"`` or ``"user"``). (default: `organization`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendlyWebhook]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_webhooks", {"organization_uri": "your-organization_uri", "scope": "organization"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 200 requests/minute — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`cancel_event`, `cancel_invitee`, `create_invitee`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses ## Related Connectors - [Gcalendar](../gcalendar/) — Calendar - [Gdocs](../gdocs/) — Documents - [Gsheets](../gsheets/) — Spreadsheets - [Gtasks](../gtasks/) — Task lists --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

cloudflare.readme_md: # Cloudflare > CDN, DNS, security, and edge computing platform ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Devops-06B6D4) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-23-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/cloudflare/cloudflare-python?style=social)](https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflare-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflare-python) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflare-go) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Cloudflare Inc. | | **Category** | Devops | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [cloudflare.com](https://cloudflare.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.cloudflare.com](https://developers.cloudflare.com/api/) | | **Auth** | API Token, API Key + Email | | **Rate Limit** | 1,200 requests/5 minutes | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Pro from $20/month | --- ## Overview The Cloudflare API provides access to DNS records, zones, firewall rules, Workers, and more. Manage your web infrastructure, configure security policies, deploy serverless functions, and monitor traffic analytics. ## Use Cases - DNS management - CDN configuration - Web security rules - Edge worker deployment - Traffic analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[cloudflare]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_CLOUDFLARE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["cloudflare"], credentials={"cloudflare": "your-token"}) # Get Cloudflare zone analytics result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_analytics", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "since": "your-since"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["cloudflare"], credentials={"cloudflare": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["cloudflare"], credentials={"cloudflare": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Token 1. Create an account at [Cloudflare](https://cloudflare.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_analytics", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_dns_record` :warning: Create a DNS record in a Cloudflare zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `type` | `string` | Yes | DNS record type (``A``, ``AAAA``, ``CNAME``, ``MX``, etc.). | | `name` | `string` | Yes | DNS record name (e.g. ``example.com``). | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Record content (e.g. IP address or target). | | `ttl` | `string` | No | Time to live in seconds. ``1`` means automatic. | **Returns:** `CFDNSRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_create_dns_record", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "type": "your-type", "name": "my-name", "content": "your-content", "ttl": "your-ttl"}) ``` --- ### `create_page_rule` :warning: Create a page rule for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | | `targets` | `array` | Yes | List of target URL pattern dicts. | | `actions` | `array` | Yes | List of action configuration dicts. | **Returns:** `CFPageRule` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_create_page_rule", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "targets": "your-targets", "actions": "your-actions"}) ``` --- ### `create_zone` :warning: Create a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Domain name (e.g., 'example.com'). | | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | Cloudflare account ID. | | `type` | `string` | No | Zone type: 'full' or 'partial'. (default: `full`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_create_zone", {"name": "my-name", "account_id": "your-account_id", "type": "full"}) ``` --- ### `delete_dns_record` :warning: Delete a DNS record from a Cloudflare zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The DNS record ID to delete. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_delete_dns_record", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "record_id": "your-record_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_page_rule` :warning: Delete a page rule | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | | `rule_id` | `string` | Yes | The page rule ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_delete_page_rule", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "rule_id": "your-rule_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_worker` :warning: Delete a worker script | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | Cloudflare account ID. | | `script_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the worker script to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_delete_worker", {"account_id": "your-account_id", "script_name": "your-script_name"}) ``` --- ### `delete_zone` :warning: Delete a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_delete_zone", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `deploy_worker` :warning: Deploy a worker script | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | Cloudflare account ID. | | `script_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the worker script. | | `script_content` | `string` | Yes | The JavaScript worker code. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_deploy_worker", {"account_id": "your-account_id", "script_name": "your-script_name", "script_content": "your-script_content"}) ``` --- ### `get_analytics` Get Cloudflare zone analytics | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `since` | `string` | No | Start of time range. Accepts relative values like ``-1440`` (minutes ago) or ISO 8601 timestamps. | **Returns:** `CFAnalytics` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_analytics", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "since": "your-since"}) ``` --- ### `get_ssl_setting` Get SSL/TLS setting for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_ssl_setting", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_worker` Get a Worker script by name | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | Cloudflare account ID. | | `script_name` | `string` | Yes | The script name. | **Returns:** `CFWorker` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_worker", {"account_id": "your-account_id", "script_name": "your-script_name"}) ``` --- ### `get_zone` Get a single Cloudflare zone by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | **Returns:** `CFZone` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_zone", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_zone_settings` Get zone settings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_zone_settings", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_dns_records` List DNS records for a Cloudflare zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `type` | `string` | No | Filter by record type (``A``, ``AAAA``, ``CNAME``, etc.). | | `name` | `string` | No | Filter by record name. | | `page` | `string` | No | Page number string for pagination. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CFDNSRecord]` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_dns_records", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "type": "your-type", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `list_firewall_rules` List firewall rules for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_firewall_rules", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_page_rules` List page rules for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_page_rules", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_waf_rulesets` List WAF rulesets for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_waf_rulesets", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_workers` List Workers scripts for an account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | Cloudflare account ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_workers", {"account_id": "your-account_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_zones` List Cloudflare zones | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum zones per page (max 50). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Page number string for pagination. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CFZone]` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_zones", {"limit": 20, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `purge_cache` :warning: Purge Cloudflare cache for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `files` | `string` | No | List of specific URLs to purge. If omitted, purges all. | **Returns:** `CFPurgeResult` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_purge_cache", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "files": "your-files"}) ``` --- ### `toggle_development_mode` :warning: Toggle development mode for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `enable` | `boolean` | Yes | True to enable development mode, False to disable. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_toggle_development_mode", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "enable": True}) ``` --- ### `update_dns_record` :warning: Update a DNS record in a Cloudflare zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The DNS record ID to update. | | `type` | `string` | Yes | DNS record type. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | DNS record name. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Updated record content. | **Returns:** `CFDNSRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_update_dns_record", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "record_id": "your-record_id", "type": "your-type", "name": "my-name", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `update_ssl_setting` :warning: Update SSL/TLS setting | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | SSL mode: 'off', 'flexible', 'full', or 'strict'. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_update_ssl_setting", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "value": "my-value"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 1,200 requests/5 minutes — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_dns_record`, `create_page_rule`, `create_zone`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 23 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Datadog](../datadog/) — Monitoring - [Pagerduty](../pagerduty/) — Incident management - [Vercel](../vercel/) — Frontend deployment - [Dockerhub](../dockerhub/) — Container registry --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*
