PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

confluence.readme_md: # Confluence > Team wiki, documentation, and knowledge management ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Knowledge-0EA5E9) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-25-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/atlassian-api/atlassian-python-api?style=social)](https://github.com/atlassian-api/atlassian-python-api) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/atlassian-api/atlassian-python-api) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Atlassian | | **Category** | Knowledge | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [www.atlassian.com/software/confluence](https://www.atlassian.com/software/confluence) | | **API Docs** | [developer.atlassian.com](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/confluence/rest/v2/intro/) | | **Auth** | Basic Auth (email:api_token), OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 100 requests/minute | | **Pricing** | Free up to 10 users, Standard from $6.05/user/month | --- ## Overview The Confluence REST API provides access to spaces, pages, blog posts, comments, and attachments. Create and organize documentation, search content, manage permissions, and build knowledge management workflows. ## Use Cases - Documentation automation - Knowledge base management - Content publishing - Team collaboration - Compliance documentation ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[confluence]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_CONFLUENCE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["confluence"], credentials={"confluence": "your-token"}) # List pages in a space or across all spaces result = kit.execute("confluence_list_pages", {"space_id": "space-123", "limit": 25}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["confluence"], credentials={"confluence": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["confluence"], credentials={"confluence": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Basic Auth (email:api_token) 1. Create an account at [Confluence](https://www.atlassian.com/software/confluence) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("confluence_list_pages", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_comment` :warning: Add a comment to a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID to comment on. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Comment body in Confluence storage format (XHTML). | **Returns:** `ConfluenceComment` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_add_comment", {"page_id": "page-123", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `add_page_label` :warning: Add a label to a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the page to label. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | The label name (e.g., ``"important"``). | **Returns:** `ConfluenceLabel` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_add_page_label", {"page_id": "page-123", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `create_blog_post` :warning: Create a blog post | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | Yes | The space ID. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Blog post title. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Content in storage format (HTML). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_create_blog_post", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "title": "My Title", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_page` Create a new page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the space to create the page in. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Page title. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Page body content in Confluence storage format (XHTML). | | `parent_id` | `string` | No | Optional parent page ID for nested pages. | **Returns:** `ConfluencePage` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_create_page", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "title": "My Title", "body": "Hello!", "parent_id": "your-parent_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_page` :warning: Delete a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the page to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_delete_page", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_blog_post` Get a blog post by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `blog_post_id` | `string` | Yes | The blog post ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_blog_post", {"blog_post_id": "your-blog_post_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_page` Get a single page by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the page. | **Returns:** `ConfluencePage` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_page", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_page_by_title` Get a page by title within a space | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the space to search in. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Exact page title to match. | **Returns:** `ConfluencePage` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_page_by_title", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "title": "My Title"}) ``` --- ### `get_page_history` Get version history for a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_page_history", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_page_labels` Get labels on a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the page to get labels for. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_page_labels", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_page_properties` Get content properties for a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_page_properties", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_space` Get a single space by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the space. | **Returns:** `ConfluenceSpace` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_space", {"space_id": "your-space_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_attachments` List attachments on a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum attachments to return. (default: `25`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_attachments", {"page_id": "page-123", "limit": 25}) ``` --- ### `list_blog_posts` List blog posts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | No | Filter by space ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum posts to return. (default: `25`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_blog_posts", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "limit": 25}) ``` --- ### `list_comments` List comments on a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID to list comments for. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_comments", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_page_ancestors` List ancestor pages of a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the page to get ancestors for. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_page_ancestors", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_page_children` List child pages of a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the parent page. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of child pages to return (max 250). (default: `25`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Cursor token for paginating through children. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ConfluencePage]` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_page_children", {"page_id": "page-123", "limit": 25, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_pages` List pages in a space or across all spaces | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | No | Optional space ID to filter pages by. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of pages per request (max 250). (default: `25`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Cursor token for fetching the next page of results. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ConfluencePage]` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_pages", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "limit": 25}) ``` --- ### `list_space_pages` List pages in a specific space | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the space to list pages from. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum pages per request (max 250). (default: `25`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Cursor token for pagination. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ConfluencePage]` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_space_pages", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "limit": 25, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_spaces` List all spaces | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of spaces per page (max 250). (default: `25`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Cursor token for fetching the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ConfluenceSpace]` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_spaces", {"limit": 25, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `move_page` Move a page to a new parent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID to move. | | `target_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the new parent page. | **Returns:** `ConfluencePage` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_move_page", {"page_id": "page-123", "target_id": "your-target_id"}) ``` --- ### `remove_page_label` :warning: Remove a label from a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID. | | `label_id` | `string` | Yes | The label ID to remove. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_remove_page_label", {"page_id": "page-123", "label_id": "your-label_id"}) ``` --- ### `search` Search for pages using CQL | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query string. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page. (default: `25`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Cursor token for fetching the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ConfluencePage]` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_search", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 25, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `set_page_property` :warning: Set a content property on a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Property key name. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | JSON-serializable value (max 32KB). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_set_page_property", {"page_id": "page-123", "key": "my-key", "value": "my-value"}) ``` --- ### `update_page` Update an existing page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the page to update. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | New page title. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | New page body content in storage format (XHTML). | | `version_number` | `integer` | Yes | New version number (current version + 1). | **Returns:** `ConfluencePage` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_update_page", {"page_id": "page-123", "title": "My Title", "body": "Hello!", "version_number": 10}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search` for filtered queries and `list_attachments` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 100 requests/minute — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_comment`, `add_page_label`, `create_blog_post`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses ## Related Connectors - [Notion](../notion/) — Knowledge workspace --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

datadog.readme_md: # Datadog > Monitoring, APM, and log management platform ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Devops-06B6D4) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-22-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/DataDog/datadog-api-client-python?style=social)](https://github.com/DataDog/datadog-api-client-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/DataDog/datadog-api-client-python) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/DataDog/datadog-api-client-go) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Datadog Inc. | | **Category** | Devops | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [datadoghq.com](https://datadoghq.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.datadoghq.com](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/) | | **Auth** | API Key + Application Key | | **Rate Limit** | 300 requests/hour for some endpoints | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Pro from $15/host/month | --- ## Overview The Datadog API lets you manage dashboards, monitors, metrics, logs, and incidents. Query time-series data, create alerts, manage service catalogs, and build observability automation for your infrastructure. ## Use Cases - Infrastructure monitoring - Alert management - Dashboard automation - Log analysis - Incident response ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[datadog]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_DATADOG_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["datadog"], credentials={"datadog": "your-token"}) # List Datadog events result = kit.execute("datadog_list_events", {"start": 10, "end": 10, "priority": "your-priority"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["datadog"], credentials={"datadog": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["datadog"], credentials={"datadog": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key + Application Key 1. Create an account at [Datadog](https://datadoghq.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://app.datadoghq.com/organization-settings/api-keys) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("datadog_list_events", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `cancel_downtime` :warning: Cancel a scheduled downtime | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `downtime_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric downtime ID to cancel. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_cancel_downtime", {"downtime_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `create_downtime` :warning: Schedule a downtime window | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `scope` | `string` | Yes | Scope of the downtime (e.g. ``"host:myhost"``). | | `start` | `integer` | Yes | POSIX timestamp for the start of the downtime. | | `end` | `string` | No | Optional POSIX timestamp for the end. Omit for indefinite. | **Returns:** `DatadogDowntime` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_create_downtime", {"scope": "your-scope", "start": 10, "end": "your-end"}) ``` --- ### `create_event` :warning: Create a Datadog event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | Event title. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | Event body text. Supports Markdown. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Event priority (``normal`` or ``low``). | | `tags` | `string` | No | List of tags to attach (e.g. ``["env:prod"]``). | **Returns:** `DatadogEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_create_event", {"title": "My Title", "text": "Hello!", "priority": "your-priority", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ### `create_incident` :warning: Create an incident | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | Incident title. | | `severity` | `string` | No | Severity level (SEV-1 through SEV-5). (default: `SEV-3`) | | `customer_impact_scope` | `string` | No | Description of customer impact. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_create_incident", {"title": "My Title", "severity": "SEV-3", "customer_impact_scope": "your-customer_impact_scope"}) ``` --- ### `create_monitor` :warning: Create a Datadog monitor | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the monitor. | | `type` | `string` | Yes | Monitor type (e.g. ``metric alert``, ``service check``). | | `query` | `string` | Yes | The monitor query string. | | `message` | `string` | No | Notification message body. | **Returns:** `DatadogMonitor` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_create_monitor", {"name": "my-name", "type": "your-type", "query": "is:unread", "message": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `delete_dashboard` :warning: Delete a dashboard | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dashboard_id` | `string` | Yes | The dashboard ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_delete_dashboard", {"dashboard_id": "your-dashboard_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_monitor` :warning: Delete a Datadog monitor | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `monitor_id` | `integer` | Yes | The monitor ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_delete_monitor", {"monitor_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_dashboard` Get a dashboard by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dashboard_id` | `string` | Yes | The dashboard ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_get_dashboard", {"dashboard_id": "your-dashboard_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_metric_metadata` Get metadata for a metric | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `metric_name` | `string` | Yes | The fully qualified metric name. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_get_metric_metadata", {"metric_name": "your-metric_name"}) ``` --- ### `get_monitor` Get a single Datadog monitor by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `monitor_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric monitor ID. | **Returns:** `DatadogMonitor` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_get_monitor", {"monitor_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_dashboards` List Datadog dashboards **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DatadogDashboard]` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_dashboards", {}) ``` --- ### `list_downtimes` List scheduled downtimes **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DatadogDowntime]` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_downtimes", {}) ``` --- ### `list_events` List Datadog events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `start` | `integer` | Yes | Start timestamp in epoch seconds. | | `end` | `integer` | Yes | End timestamp in epoch seconds. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Filter by priority (``normal`` or ``low``). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DatadogEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_events", {"start": 10, "end": 10, "priority": "your-priority"}) ``` --- ### `list_hosts` List hosts reporting to Datadog | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `filter` | `string` | No | Filter string (e.g. host name or tag). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_hosts", {"filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `list_incidents` List incidents | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum incidents to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_incidents", {"limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_monitors` List Datadog monitors | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of monitors to return (max 1000). (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DatadogMonitor]` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_monitors", {"limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_slos` List SLOs | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | No | Search query to filter SLOs. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum SLOs to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_slos", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `mute_monitor` :warning: Mute a Datadog monitor | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `monitor_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric monitor ID to mute. | **Returns:** `DatadogMonitor` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_mute_monitor", {"monitor_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `query_metrics` Query Datadog metrics | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Metrics query string (e.g. ``avg:system.cpu.user{*}``). | | `from_ts` | `integer` | Yes | Start timestamp in epoch seconds. | | `to_ts` | `integer` | Yes | End timestamp in epoch seconds. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_query_metrics", {"query": "is:unread", "from_ts": 10, "to_ts": 10}) ``` --- ### `search_logs` Search logs | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Log search query (Datadog log search syntax). | | `time_from` | `string` | Yes | Start time (ISO 8601 or relative like 'now-1h'). | | `time_to` | `string` | Yes | End time (ISO 8601 or 'now'). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum log entries to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_search_logs", {"query": "is:unread", "time_from": "your-time_from", "time_to": "your-time_to", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `search_monitors` Search Datadog monitors by query | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query (e.g. ``"tag:env:prod"`` or monitor name). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of monitors to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DatadogMonitor]` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_search_monitors", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `unmute_monitor` Unmute a Datadog monitor | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `monitor_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric monitor ID to unmute. | **Returns:** `DatadogMonitor` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_unmute_monitor", {"monitor_id": 10}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_logs` for filtered queries and `list_dashboards` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 300 requests/hour for some endpoints — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`cancel_downtime`, `create_downtime`, `create_event`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 22 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Cloudflare](../cloudflare/) — CDN and security - [Pagerduty](../pagerduty/) — Incident management - [Vercel](../vercel/) — Frontend deployment - [Dockerhub](../dockerhub/) — Container registry --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

discord.readme_md: # Discord > Messaging, voice, and community management ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Communication-3B82F6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-25-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/Rapptz/discord.py?style=social)](https://github.com/Rapptz/discord.py) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/Rapptz/discord.py) [![API Spec](https://img.shields.io/badge/API_Spec-SDK-6366F1?logo=github)](https://github.com/discord/discord-api-docs) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Discord Inc. | | **Category** | Communication | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [discord.com](https://discord.com) | | **API Docs** | [discord.com](https://discord.com/developers/docs) | | **Auth** | Bot Token, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 50 requests/second global | | **Pricing** | Free, Nitro from $9.99/month | --- ## Overview The Discord API provides access to Discord servers (guilds), channels, messages, and members. Build bots that moderate communities, send notifications, manage roles, and create interactive experiences. Supports bot tokens and OAuth 2.0. ## Use Cases - Community moderation bots - Server management automation - Notification systems - Gaming integrations - Event management ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[discord]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_DISCORD_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["discord"], credentials={"discord": "your-token"}) # List messages in a channel result = kit.execute("discord_list_messages", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "limit": 50, "before": "your-before"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["discord"], credentials={"discord": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["discord"], credentials={"discord": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bot Token 1. Developer Portal 2. New Application 3. Bot Token [Get credentials →](https://discord.com/developers/applications) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("discord_list_messages", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_member_role` :warning: Add a role to a guild member | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID. | | `role_id` | `string` | Yes | The role snowflake ID to assign. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_add_member_role", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "user_id": "user-123", "role_id": "your-role_id"}) ``` --- ### `add_reaction` Add a reaction to a message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The message snowflake ID. | | `emoji` | `string` | Yes | Unicode emoji (e.g. ``\U0001f44d``) or custom emoji in ``name:id`` format (e.g. ``custom_emoji:123456``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_add_reaction", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id", "emoji": "your-emoji"}) ``` --- ### `ban_member` :warning: Ban a member from a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID to ban. | | `reason` | `string` | No | Optional audit log reason for the ban. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_ban_member", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "user_id": "user-123", "reason": "your-reason"}) ``` --- ### `create_channel` :warning: Create a new channel in a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new channel (2-100 characters, lowercase, no spaces). | | `type` | `integer` | No | Channel type integer. 0 = text, 2 = voice, 4 = category. (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `DiscordChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_create_channel", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "name": "my-name", "type": 0}) ``` --- ### `create_role` :warning: Create a role in a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new role. | | `permissions` | `string` | No | Permission bit set as a string. Defaults to ``@everyone`` permissions of the guild. | | `color` | `string` | No | RGB color value for the role (integer). | **Returns:** `DiscordRole` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_create_role", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "name": "my-name", "permissions": "your-permissions", "color": "your-color"}) ``` --- ### `create_thread` :warning: Create a thread in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The parent channel snowflake ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the thread (1-100 characters). | | `message_id` | `string` | No | Optional message snowflake ID to start the thread from. | **Returns:** `DiscordChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_create_thread", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "name": "my-name", "message_id": "your-message_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_webhook` :warning: Create a webhook for a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the webhook (1-80 characters). | | `avatar` | `string` | No | Optional base64-encoded 128x128 image for the webhook's default avatar. | **Returns:** `DiscordWebhook` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_create_webhook", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "name": "my-name", "avatar": "your-avatar"}) ``` --- ### `delete_channel` :warning: Delete a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_delete_channel", {"channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `delete_message` :warning: Delete a message from a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The message snowflake ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_delete_message", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id"}) ``` --- ### `edit_guild` :warning: Edit guild settings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `name` | `string` | No | Optional new name for the guild (2-100 characters). | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional new description (for Community guilds). | | `icon` | `string` | No | Optional base64-encoded 128x128 image for the guild icon, or ``None`` to remove the icon. | **Returns:** `DiscordGuild` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_edit_guild", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `edit_message` Edit a message in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID containing the message. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The message snowflake ID to edit. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | New message text content (max 2000 characters). | **Returns:** `DiscordMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_edit_message", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `get_channel` Get a single channel by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `DiscordChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_get_channel", {"channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `get_guild` Get guild information | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `DiscordGuild` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_get_guild", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_member` Get a single guild member | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `GuildMember` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_get_member", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a single user by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `DiscordUser` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_get_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `kick_member` :warning: Kick a member from a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID to kick. | | `reason` | `string` | No | Optional audit log reason for the kick. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_kick_member", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "user_id": "user-123", "reason": "your-reason"}) ``` --- ### `list_channels` List channels in a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_list_channels", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_guild_members` List members of a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of members to return (1-1000, default 100). (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_list_guild_members", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "limit": 100}) ``` --- ### `list_messages` List messages in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of messages to return (1-100, default 50). (default: `50`) | | `before` | `string` | No | Get messages before this message snowflake ID. | | `after` | `string` | No | Get messages after this message snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_list_messages", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "limit": 50, "before": "your-before"}) ``` --- ### `list_pinned_messages` List pinned messages in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_list_pinned_messages", {"channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `list_roles` List roles in a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_list_roles", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id"}) ``` --- ### `pin_message` Pin a message in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The message snowflake ID to pin. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_pin_message", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id"}) ``` --- ### `remove_member_role` :warning: Remove a role from a guild member | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID. | | `role_id` | `string` | Yes | The role snowflake ID to remove. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_remove_member_role", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "user_id": "user-123", "role_id": "your-role_id"}) ``` --- ### `send_message` :warning: Send a message to a Discord channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Message text content (max 2000 characters). | | `embeds` | `string` | No | Optional list of embed objects (max 10). Each embed is a dict matching the Discord embed structure. | **Returns:** `DiscordMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_send_message", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "content": "your-content", "embeds": "your-embeds"}) ``` --- ### `unpin_message` Unpin a message from a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The message snowflake ID to unpin. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_unpin_message", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 50 requests/second global — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_member_role`, `ban_member`, `create_channel`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 25 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Gmail](../gmail/) — Email automation - [Slack](../slack/) — Team messaging - [Teams](../teams/) — Microsoft collaboration - [Telegram](../telegram/) — Bot messaging --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

dockerhub.readme_md: # Docker Hub > Container registry and image management ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Devops-06B6D4) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-14-purple) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Docker Inc. | | **Category** | Devops | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [hub.docker.com](https://hub.docker.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.docker.com](https://docs.docker.com/docker-hub/api/latest/) | | **Auth** | Personal Access Token | | **Rate Limit** | 100 pulls/6 hours (anonymous), 200 (authenticated) | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Pro from $5/month | --- ## Overview The Docker Hub API provides access to repositories, images, tags, and organizations. Search for images, manage repository settings, check image vulnerabilities, and automate container image lifecycle management. ## Use Cases - Image registry management - Vulnerability scanning automation - CI/CD image publishing - Repository access control - Image tag management ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[dockerhub]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_DOCKERHUB_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["dockerhub"], credentials={"dockerhub": "your-token"}) # List repositories for a Docker Hub namespace result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_repos", {"namespace": "default", "limit": 25, "page": "your-page"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["dockerhub"], credentials={"dockerhub": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["dockerhub"], credentials={"dockerhub": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Personal Access Token 1. Create an account at [Docker Hub](https://hub.docker.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://hub.docker.com/settings/security) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_repos", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_repo` :warning: Create a Docker Hub repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace (user or org). | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional short description. | | `is_private` | `string` | No | Whether the repository is private. | **Returns:** `DockerRepo` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_create_repo", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "name": "my-name", "description": "your-description", "is_private": "your-is_private"}) ``` --- ### `delete_tag` :warning: Delete a repository tag | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Docker Hub namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `tag` | `string` | Yes | Tag name to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_delete_tag", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo", "tag": "your-tag"}) ``` --- ### `get_build_history` Get build history for a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Docker Hub namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_get_build_history", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `get_org` Get a Docker Hub organisation | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `orgname` | `string` | Yes | Organisation name. | **Returns:** `DockerOrg` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_get_org", {"orgname": "your-orgname"}) ``` --- ### `get_repo` Get a Docker Hub repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `DockerRepo` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_get_repo", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `get_tag` Get a single Docker Hub repository tag | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `tag` | `string` | Yes | Tag name (e.g. ``latest``). | **Returns:** `DockerTag` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_get_tag", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo", "tag": "your-tag"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a Docker Hub user profile | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `username` | `string` | Yes | Docker Hub username. | **Returns:** `DockerUser` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_get_user", {"username": "your-username"}) ``` --- ### `list_build_triggers` List build triggers for a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Docker Hub namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_build_triggers", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `list_collaborators` List collaborators of a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Docker Hub namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_collaborators", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `list_orgs` List Docker Hub organisations for the authenticated user **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_orgs", {}) ``` --- ### `list_repos` List repositories for a Docker Hub namespace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `25`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DockerRepo]` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_repos", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "limit": 25, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `list_tags` List tags for a Docker Hub repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum tags per page (max 100). (default: `25`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DockerTag]` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_tags", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo", "limit": 25, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `search_repos` Search Docker Hub repositories | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query string. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `25`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DockerRepo]` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_search_repos", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 25, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `update_repo` Update a Docker Hub repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `description` | `string` | No | New short description for the repository. | **Returns:** `DockerRepo` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_update_repo", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_repos` for filtered queries and `list_build_triggers` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 100 pulls/6 hours (anonymous), 200 (authenticated) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_repo`, `delete_tag`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Cloudflare](../cloudflare/) — CDN and security - [Datadog](../datadog/) — Monitoring - [Pagerduty](../pagerduty/) — Incident management - [Vercel](../vercel/) — Frontend deployment --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's

figma.readme_md: # Figma > Collaborative design and prototyping tool ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Productivity-F97316) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-22-purple) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Figma Inc. (Adobe) | | **Category** | Productivity | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [figma.com](https://figma.com) | | **API Docs** | [www.figma.com](https://www.figma.com/developers/api) | | **Auth** | Personal Access Token, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 30 requests/minute per token | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Professional from $15/editor/month | --- ## Overview The Figma API provides access to files, components, styles, comments, and project metadata. Extract design tokens, automate asset export, manage team libraries, and build design-to-code workflows. ## Use Cases - Design token extraction - Automated asset export - Design system management - Comment and review automation - Design analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[figma]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_FIGMA_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["figma"], credentials={"figma": "your-token"}) # List projects for a Figma team result = kit.execute("figma_list_projects", {"team_id": "T01234567"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["figma"], credentials={"figma": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["figma"], credentials={"figma": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Personal Access Token 1. Create an account at [Figma](https://figma.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://www.figma.com/developers/api#access-tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("figma_list_projects", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_webhook` :warning: Create a webhook | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The team ID to attach the webhook to. | | `event_type` | `string` | Yes | Event type (e.g., 'FILE_UPDATE', 'FILE_COMMENT', 'FILE_VERSION_UPDATE', 'LIBRARY_PUBLISH'). | | `endpoint` | `string` | Yes | URL to receive webhook notifications. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional description. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_create_webhook", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "event_type": "your-event_type", "endpoint": "your-endpoint", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `delete_comment` :warning: Delete a comment from a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The Figma file key. | | `comment_id` | `string` | Yes | The comment ID to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_delete_comment", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "comment_id": "your-comment_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_webhook` :warning: Delete a webhook | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `webhook_id` | `string` | Yes | The webhook ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_delete_webhook", {"webhook_id": "your-webhook_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_comments` Get comments on a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaComment]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_comments", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_component` Get a specific component from a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | | `component_id` | `string` | Yes | The node ID of the component. | **Returns:** `FigmaComponent` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_component", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "component_id": "your-component_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_component_set` Get component sets from a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The Figma file key. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_component_set", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_file` Get a Figma file by key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | **Returns:** `FigmaFile` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_file", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_file_nodes` Get specific nodes from a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The Figma file key. | | `ids` | `array` | Yes | List of node IDs to retrieve. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_file_nodes", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "ids": "your-ids"}) ``` --- ### `get_file_pages` Get pages in a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_file_pages", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_file_styles` List styles in a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_file_styles", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_image` Export images from a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | | `ids` | `string` | Yes | Comma-separated list of node IDs to render. | | `format` | `string` | No | Image format (``jpg``, ``png``, ``svg``, ``pdf``). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaImage]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_image", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "ids": "your-ids", "format": "your-format"}) ``` --- ### `get_local_variables` Get local variables in a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The Figma file key. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_local_variables", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_published_variables` Get published variables in a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The Figma file key. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_published_variables", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_team_components` List published components in a team library | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The Figma team ID. | | `page_size` | `integer` | No | Number of results per page (max 30). (default: `30`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaComponent]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_team_components", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "page_size": 30, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `get_team_styles` List published styles in a team library | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The Figma team ID. | | `page_size` | `integer` | No | Number of results per page (max 30). (default: `30`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaStyle]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_team_styles", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "page_size": 30, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_components` List components in a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaComponent]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_components", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `list_file_versions` List version history for a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of versions to return. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaVersion]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_file_versions", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `list_project_files` List files in a Figma project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | The Figma project ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaProjectFile]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_project_files", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_projects` List projects for a Figma team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The Figma team ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaProject]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_projects", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_team_projects` List projects in a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The Figma team ID. | | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of projects to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_team_projects", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `list_webhooks` List webhooks | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The team ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_webhooks", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `post_comment` :warning: Post a comment on a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | | `message` | `string` | Yes | The comment message text. | | `client_meta` | `string` | No | Optional positioning data (x, y, node_id, etc.). | **Returns:** `FigmaComment` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_post_comment", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "message": "Hello!", "client_meta": "your-client_meta"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 30 requests/minute per token — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_webhook`, `delete_comment`, `delete_webhook`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses - This connector has 22 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Gcalendar](../gcalendar/) — Calendar - [Gdocs](../gdocs/) — Documents - [Gsheets](../gsheets/) — Spreadsheets - [Gtasks](../gtasks/) — Task lists --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

firestore.readme_md: # Firebase Firestore > Serverless NoSQL document database ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Database-10B981) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-17-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/firebase/firebase-admin-python?style=social)](https://github.com/firebase/firebase-admin-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/firebase/firebase-admin-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/firebase/firebase-admin-node) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/firebase/firebase-admin-go) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Google (Firebase) | | **Category** | Database | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [firebase.google.com/products/firestore](https://firebase.google.com/products/firestore) | | **API Docs** | [firebase.google.com](https://firebase.google.com/docs/firestore/reference/rest) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token (Firebase Auth), Service Account | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan | | **Pricing** | Free tier (Spark), Pay-as-you-go (Blaze) | --- ## Overview The Firestore REST API provides access to documents and collections in your Firebase Firestore database. Query data with filters and ordering, perform batch operations, manage indexes, and build real-time applications. ## Use Cases - Document storage and retrieval - Real-time data sync - Serverless backend - Mobile app data layer - IoT data collection ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[firestore]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_FIRESTORE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["firestore"], credentials={"firestore": "your-token"}) # Get a Firestore document by ID result = kit.execute("firestore_get_document", {"project": "my-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "doc-123"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["firestore"], credentials={"firestore": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["firestore"], credentials={"firestore": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token (Firebase Auth) 1. Create an account at [Firebase Firestore](https://firebase.google.com/products/firestore) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.firebase.google.com/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("firestore_get_document", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `batch_get` Batch get multiple documents | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | Google Cloud project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of document IDs to retrieve. | | `database` | `string` | No | Database ID (default: '(default)'). (default: `(default)`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_batch_get", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_ids": "your-document_ids", "database": "(default)"}) ``` --- ### `batch_write` Execute a batch write in Firestore | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `writes` | `array` | Yes | List of write operation dicts. | **Returns:** `FirestoreBatchWriteResult` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_batch_write", {"project": "your-project", "writes": "your-writes"}) ``` --- ### `count_documents` Count documents in a Firestore collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `filter` | `string` | No | Optional list of field filter dicts (same format as the ``query`` action's ``where`` parameter). | **Returns:** `int` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_count_documents", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `create_collection_group_index` :warning: Create a collection group index in Firestore | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection_group` | `string` | Yes | The collection group ID. | | `fields` | `array` | Yes | List of index field configurations. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_create_collection_group_index", {"project": "your-project", "collection_group": "your-collection_group", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ### `create_document` Create a document in a Firestore collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field names to Python-native values. | | `document_id` | `string` | No | Optional document ID; auto-generated if omitted. | **Returns:** `FirestoreDocument` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_create_document", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "fields": "your-fields", "document_id": "your-document_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_document` :warning: Delete a Firestore document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | Document ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_delete_document", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "your-document_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_index` :warning: Delete a composite index | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | Google Cloud project ID. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database ID (usually '(default)'). | | `index_id` | `string` | Yes | The index ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_delete_index", {"project": "your-project", "database": "your-database", "index_id": "your-index_id"}) ``` --- ### `export_documents` Export documents from a collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | Google Cloud project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | The collection path. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_export_documents", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection"}) ``` --- ### `get_document` Get a Firestore document by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | Document ID. | **Returns:** `FirestoreDocument` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_get_document", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "your-document_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_document_field` Get a single field value from a Firestore document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | Document ID. | | `field_path` | `string` | Yes | Dot-separated field path (e.g. ``"address.city"``). | **Returns:** `Any` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_get_document_field", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "your-document_id", "field_path": "your-field_path"}) ``` --- ### `list_collections` List collections in a Firestore project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_list_collections", {"project": "your-project"}) ``` --- ### `list_documents` List documents in a Firestore collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum documents per page. (default: `100`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token from a previous response for next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FirestoreDocument]` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_list_documents", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "limit": 100, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `list_indexes` List indexes for a collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | Google Cloud project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | The collection group ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_list_indexes", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection"}) ``` --- ### `query` Run a structured query on a Firestore collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `where` | `string` | No | List of field filter dicts. | | `order_by` | `string` | No | List of ``{"field": "name", "direction": "ASCENDING"}`` dicts. | | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of documents to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_query", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "where": "your-where", "order_by": "your-order_by"}) ``` --- ### `run_transaction` Run a transaction with multiple operations | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | Google Cloud project ID. | | `operations` | `array` | Yes | List of write operation dicts. Each should have either ``"update"`` or ``"delete"`` keys. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_run_transaction", {"project": "your-project", "operations": "your-operations"}) ``` --- ### `update_document` Update a Firestore document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | Document ID to update. | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field names to new values. | **Returns:** `FirestoreDocument` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_update_document", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "your-document_id", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ### `update_fields` Update specific fields in a Firestore document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | Document ID to update. | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field names to new Python-native values. | | `update_mask` | `string` | No | Explicit list of field paths to update. Defaults to the keys of ``fields``. | **Returns:** `FirestoreDocument` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_update_fields", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "your-document_id", "fields": "your-fields", "update_mask": "your-update_mask"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use batch operations (`batch_get`, `batch_write`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls - Rate limit is Varies by plan — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_collection_group_index`, `delete_document`, `delete_index`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Airtable](../airtable/) — Spreadsheet database - [Mongodb](../mongodb/) — NoSQL database - [Redis](../redis/) — Key-value store - [Supabase](../supabase/) — Postgres as a service --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.
