freshdesk.readme_md: # Freshdesk > Customer support and helpdesk platform ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Crm-F59E0B) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-23-purple) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Freshworks Inc. | | **Category** | Crm | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [freshdesk.com](https://freshdesk.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.freshdesk.com](https://developers.freshdesk.com/api/) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan (min 50 requests/minute) | | **Pricing** | Free up to 10 agents, Growth from $15/agent/month | --- ## Overview The Freshdesk API provides access to tickets, contacts, agents, companies, and knowledge base articles. Manage support workflows, automate ticket routing, track SLAs, and build custom helpdesk integrations. ## Use Cases - Ticket management - Customer support automation - Knowledge base management - SLA tracking - Multi-channel support ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[freshdesk]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_FRESHDESK_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["freshdesk"], credentials={"freshdesk": "your-token"}) # List contacts result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_contacts", {"limit": 30, "page": 1}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["freshdesk"], credentials={"freshdesk": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["freshdesk"], credentials={"freshdesk": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Create an account at [Freshdesk](https://freshdesk.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://support.freshdesk.com/en/support/solutions/articles/215517) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_contacts", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_note` Add a note to a ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk ticket ID. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Note body (HTML allowed). | **Returns:** `FreshdeskNote` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_add_note", {"ticket_id": 10, "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_company` :warning: Create a new company | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Company name. | | `domains` | `string` | No | List of email domains associated with the company. | | `description` | `string` | No | Company description. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskCompany` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_create_company", {"name": "my-name", "domains": "your-domains", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_contact` :warning: Create a new contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Contact's full name. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | Contact's email address. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskContact` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_create_contact", {"name": "my-name", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `create_ticket` :warning: Create a new support ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Ticket subject line. | | `description` | `string` | Yes | Ticket body (HTML allowed). | | `email` | `string` | Yes | Requester email address. | | `priority` | `integer` | No | Priority (1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High, 4=Urgent). (default: `1`) | | `status` | `integer` | No | Initial status (2=Open, 3=Pending, 4=Resolved, 5=Closed). (default: `2`) | **Returns:** `FreshdeskTicket` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_create_ticket", {"subject": "Hello", "description": "your-description", "email": "user@example.com", "priority": 1, "status": 2}) ``` --- ### `delete_ticket` :warning: Delete a Freshdesk ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk ticket ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_delete_ticket", {"ticket_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_agent` Get a single agent by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `agent_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk agent ID. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskAgent` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_get_agent", {"agent_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_company` Get a single company by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk company ID. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskCompany` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_get_company", {"company_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_contact` Get a single contact by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk contact ID. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskContact` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_get_contact", {"contact_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_ticket` Get a single ticket by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk ticket ID. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskTicket` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_get_ticket", {"ticket_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_agents` List agents in Freshdesk | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of agents to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_agents", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `list_canned_responses` List canned responses **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_canned_responses", {}) ``` --- ### `list_companies` List companies in Freshdesk | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Number of companies per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FreshdeskCompany]` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_companies", {"limit": 30, "page": 1}) ``` --- ### `list_contacts` List contacts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Number of contacts per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FreshdeskContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_contacts", {"limit": 30, "page": 1}) ``` --- ### `list_email_configs` List email configurations **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_email_configs", {}) ``` --- ### `list_groups` List agent groups in Freshdesk **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_groups", {}) ``` --- ### `list_satisfaction_ratings` List satisfaction ratings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Number of ratings per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[dict[str, Any]]` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_satisfaction_ratings", {"limit": 30, "page": 1}) ``` --- ### `list_ticket_fields` List ticket fields in Freshdesk **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_ticket_fields", {}) ``` --- ### `list_tickets` List support tickets | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (2=Open, 3=Pending, 4=Resolved, 5=Closed). | | `priority` | `string` | No | Filter by priority (1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High, 4=Urgent). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Number of tickets per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FreshdeskTicket]` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_tickets", {"status": "active", "priority": "your-priority"}) ``` --- ### `merge_tickets` :warning: Merge multiple tickets into one | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `primary_id` | `integer` | Yes | The ticket ID that will remain open. | | `secondary_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of ticket IDs to merge into the primary. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_merge_tickets", {"primary_id": 10, "secondary_ids": "your-secondary_ids"}) ``` --- ### `reply_to_ticket` :warning: Reply to a ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk ticket ID. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Reply body (HTML allowed). | **Returns:** `FreshdeskReply` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_reply_to_ticket", {"ticket_id": 10, "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `search_tickets` Search tickets using Freshdesk query language | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Freshdesk search query string. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FreshdeskTicket]` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_search_tickets", {"query": "is:unread"}) ``` --- ### `update_contact` Update an existing contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk contact ID. | | `name` | `string` | No | New name for the contact. | | `email` | `string` | No | New email for the contact. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskContact` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_update_contact", {"contact_id": 10, "name": "my-name", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `update_ticket` Update an existing ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk ticket ID. | | `status` | `string` | No | New status value. | | `priority` | `string` | No | New priority value. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskTicket` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_update_ticket", {"ticket_id": 10, "status": "active", "priority": "your-priority"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_tickets` for filtered queries and `list_agents` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is Varies by plan (min 50 requests/minute) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_company`, `create_contact`, `create_ticket`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 23 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Salesforce](../salesforce/) — Enterprise CRM - [Hubspot](../hubspot/) — Marketing & sales CRM - [Intercom](../intercom/) — Customer messaging - [Zendesk](../zendesk/) — Support ticketing --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

gcalendar.readme_md: # Google Calendar > Schedule events, manage calendars, check availability ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Productivity-F97316) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-20-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/googleapis/google-api-python-client?style=social)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Google | | **Category** | Productivity | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [calendar.google.com](https://calendar.google.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.google.com](https://developers.google.com/calendar/api/v3/reference) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0, Service Account | | **Rate Limit** | 500 requests/100 seconds per user | | **Pricing** | Free with Google account | --- ## Overview The Google Calendar API lets you create, modify, and query calendar events. Check free/busy status for scheduling, manage calendar sharing and access control, and build scheduling automation for teams and organizations. ## Use Cases - Meeting scheduling automation - Availability checking - Calendar sync across platforms - Event reminders and notifications - Resource booking systems ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[gcalendar]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_GCALENDAR_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["gcalendar"], credentials={"gcalendar": "your-token"}) # List calendar events result = kit.execute("gcalendar_list_events", {"calendar_id": "primary", "time_min": "your-time_min"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["gcalendar"], credentials={"gcalendar": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["gcalendar"], credentials={"gcalendar": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 1. Google Cloud Console 2. Credentials [Get credentials →](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("gcalendar_list_events", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_calendar_acl` :warning: Add an access-control rule to a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to share. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | The email address of the user to grant access to. | | `role` | `string` | No | The access level: 'none', 'freeBusyReader', 'reader', 'writer', or 'owner'. (default: `reader`) | **Returns:** `CalendarACL` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_add_calendar_acl", {"calendar_id": "primary", "email": "user@example.com", "role": "reader"}) ``` --- ### `clear_calendar` :warning: Clear all events from a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to clear. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_clear_calendar", {"calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `create_calendar` :warning: Create a new calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `summary` | `string` | Yes | Title of the new calendar. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional calendar description. | | `time_zone` | `string` | No | IANA timezone (e.g., 'America/New_York'). Defaults to the authenticated user's timezone. | **Returns:** `Calendar` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_create_calendar", {"summary": "your-summary", "description": "your-description", "time_zone": "your-time_zone"}) ``` --- ### `create_event` :warning: Create a calendar event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `summary` | `string` | Yes | Event title/summary. | | `start` | `string` | Yes | Start time as ISO 8601 string (date or datetime). | | `end` | `string` | Yes | End time as ISO 8601 string (date or datetime). | | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional event description (supports HTML). | | `location` | `string` | No | Optional event location. | | `time_zone` | `string` | No | IANA timezone (e.g., 'America/New_York'). Defaults to the calendar's timezone. | | `attendees` | `string` | No | Optional list of attendee email addresses. | | `send_updates` | `string` | No | Notification policy: 'all', 'externalOnly', or 'none'. (default: `none`) | **Returns:** `CalendarEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_create_event", {"summary": "your-summary", "start": "your-start", "end": "your-end", "calendar_id": "primary", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `delete_calendar` :warning: Delete a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_delete_calendar", {"calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `delete_event` :warning: Delete a calendar event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the event to delete. | | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | | `send_updates` | `string` | No | Notification policy: 'all', 'externalOnly', or 'none'. (default: `none`) | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_delete_event", {"event_id": "your-event_id", "calendar_id": "primary", "send_updates": "none"}) ``` --- ### `get_colors` Get available calendar and event colors **Returns:** `CalendarColors` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_get_colors", {}) ``` --- ### `get_event` Get a single event by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the event to retrieve. | | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | **Returns:** `CalendarEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_get_event", {"event_id": "your-event_id", "calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `get_free_busy` Query free/busy information for calendars | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `time_min` | `string` | Yes | Start of the time range (ISO 8601 datetime). | | `time_max` | `string` | Yes | End of the time range (ISO 8601 datetime). | | `calendar_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of calendar IDs to query. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_get_free_busy", {"time_min": "your-time_min", "time_max": "your-time_max", "calendar_ids": "your-calendar_ids"}) ``` --- ### `list_calendar_acl` List access-control rules for a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_list_calendar_acl", {"calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `list_calendars` List calendars | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page. | | `max_results` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of calendars per page (max 250). (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Calendar]` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_list_calendars", {"page_token": "your-page_token", "max_results": 100}) ``` --- ### `list_event_instances` List instances of a recurring event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | Calendar ID containing the event. | | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The recurring event ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_list_event_instances", {"calendar_id": "primary", "event_id": "your-event_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_events` List calendar events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | | `time_min` | `string` | No | Lower bound (inclusive) as RFC 3339 timestamp. | | `time_max` | `string` | No | Upper bound (exclusive) as RFC 3339 timestamp. | | `query` | `string` | No | Free-text search query across event fields. | | `max_results` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of events per page (max 2500). (default: `25`) | | `single_events` | `boolean` | No | If True, expand recurring events into instances. (default: `True`) | | `order_by` | `string` | No | Sort order: 'startTime' (requires single_events=True) or 'updated'. (default: `startTime`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendarEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_list_events", {"calendar_id": "primary", "time_min": "your-time_min"}) ``` --- ### `move_event` Move an event to a different calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | Source calendar ID. | | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The event ID to move. | | `destination_calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | Destination calendar ID. | **Returns:** `CalendarEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_move_event", {"calendar_id": "primary", "event_id": "your-event_id", "destination_calendar_id": "your-destination_calendar_id"}) ``` --- ### `quick_add_event` :warning: Quick-add an event using natural language | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `text` | `string` | Yes | Natural language description of the event. | | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | **Returns:** `CalendarEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_quick_add_event", {"text": "Hello!", "calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `remove_calendar_acl` :warning: Remove an access-control rule from a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar. | | `rule_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the ACL rule to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_remove_calendar_acl", {"calendar_id": "primary", "rule_id": "your-rule_id"}) ``` --- ### `subscribe_calendar` :warning: Subscribe to an external calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to subscribe to. | **Returns:** `Calendar` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_subscribe_calendar", {"calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `unsubscribe_calendar` :warning: Unsubscribe from a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to unsubscribe from. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_unsubscribe_calendar", {"calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `update_calendar` :warning: Update a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to update. | | `summary` | `string` | No | New calendar title. | | `description` | `string` | No | New calendar description. | | `time_zone` | `string` | No | New IANA timezone. | **Returns:** `Calendar` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_update_calendar", {"calendar_id": "primary", "summary": "your-summary", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `update_event` :warning: Update a calendar event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the event to update. | | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | | `summary` | `string` | No | New event title/summary. | | `start` | `string` | No | New start time as ISO 8601 string. | | `end` | `string` | No | New end time as ISO 8601 string. | | `description` | `string` | No | New event description. | | `location` | `string` | No | New event location. | | `time_zone` | `string` | No | IANA timezone for start/end. | | `attendees` | `string` | No | Replace attendees with this list of email addresses. | | `send_updates` | `string` | No | Notification policy: 'all', 'externalOnly', or 'none'. (default: `none`) | **Returns:** `CalendarEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_update_event", {"event_id": "your-event_id", "calendar_id": "primary", "summary": "your-summary"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 500 requests/100 seconds per user — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_calendar_acl`, `clear_calendar`, `create_calendar`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Gdocs](../gdocs/) — Documents - [Gsheets](../gsheets/) — Spreadsheets - [Gtasks](../gtasks/) — Task lists - [Figma](../figma/) — Design --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

gdocs.readme_md: # Google Docs > Create and edit documents programmatically ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Productivity-F97316) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-5-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/googleapis/google-api-python-client?style=social)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Google | | **Category** | Productivity | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [docs.google.com](https://docs.google.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.google.com](https://developers.google.com/docs/api/reference/rest) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0, Service Account | | **Rate Limit** | 300 requests/minute per project | | **Pricing** | Free with Google account | --- ## Overview The Google Docs API lets you create and modify Google Docs programmatically. Insert text, apply formatting, and extract content. Use it for document generation, template-based reports, and content management workflows. ## Use Cases - Automated document generation - Template-based reporting - Content extraction - Contract and proposal creation ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[gdocs]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_GDOCS_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["gdocs"], credentials={"gdocs": "your-token"}) # Get a document by ID result = kit.execute("gdocs_get_document", {"document_id": "doc-123"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["gdocs"], credentials={"gdocs": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["gdocs"], credentials={"gdocs": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 1. Google Cloud Console 2. Credentials [Get credentials →](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("gdocs_get_document", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `batch_update` :warning: Batch update a document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the document to update. | | `requests` | `array` | Yes | List of Docs API request objects. See `Google Docs API reference `_ for the full request specification. | **Returns:** `BatchUpdateResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("gdocs_batch_update", {"document_id": "your-document_id", "requests": "your-requests"}) ``` --- ### `create_document` :warning: Create a new document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | Title for the new document. | **Returns:** `Document` ```python result = kit.execute("gdocs_create_document", {"title": "My Title"}) ``` --- ### `get_document` Get a document by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the document to retrieve. | **Returns:** `Document` ```python result = kit.execute("gdocs_get_document", {"document_id": "your-document_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_document_text` Extract plain text from a document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the document. | **Returns:** `str` ```python result = kit.execute("gdocs_get_document_text", {"document_id": "your-document_id"}) ``` --- ### `insert_text` :warning: Insert text into a document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the document. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | The text content to insert. | | `index` | `string` | No | The zero-based character index to insert at. If omitted, defaults to index 1 (start of document body, after the implicit newline). | **Returns:** `BatchUpdateResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("gdocs_insert_text", {"document_id": "your-document_id", "text": "Hello!", "index": "your-index"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use batch operations (`batch_update`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls - Rate limit is 300 requests/minute per project — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`batch_update`, `create_document`, `insert_text`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Gcalendar](../gcalendar/) — Calendar - [Gsheets](../gsheets/) — Spreadsheets - [Gtasks](../gtasks/) — Task lists - [Figma](../figma/) — Design --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

gdrive.readme_md: # Google Drive > Store, share, and collaborate on files ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Storage-14B8A6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-22-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/googleapis/google-api-python-client?style=social)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-nodejs-client) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Google | | **Category** | Storage | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [drive.google.com](https://drive.google.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.google.com](https://developers.google.com/drive/api/reference/rest/v3) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0, Service Account | | **Rate Limit** | 12,000 requests/minute per project | | **Pricing** | Free (15GB), Google Workspace plans for more | --- ## Overview The Google Drive API provides programmatic access to Google Drive files and folders. Upload, download, search, and organize files. Manage sharing permissions and collaborate with comments. Export Google Docs/Sheets/Slides to PDF, DOCX, and other formats. ## Use Cases - File backup and sync - Document management systems - Automated report generation - File sharing workflows - Content migration ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[gdrive]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_GDRIVE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["gdrive"], credentials={"gdrive": "your-token"}) # List files in Google Drive result = kit.execute("gdrive_list_files", {"page_size": 20, "order_by": "modifiedTime desc"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["gdrive"], credentials={"gdrive": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["gdrive"], credentials={"gdrive": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 1. Google Cloud Console 2. APIs & Services 3. Credentials [Get credentials →](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("gdrive_list_files", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `copy_file` Copy a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file to copy. | | `name` | `string` | No | Optional name for the copy. Defaults to original name. | **Returns:** `DriveFile` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_copy_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `create_comment` :warning: Create a comment on a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file to comment on. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | The plain-text content of the comment. | **Returns:** `DriveComment` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_create_comment", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `create_folder` Create a folder in Google Drive | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the folder to create. | | `parent_folder_id` | `string` | No | Optional parent folder ID. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional folder description. | **Returns:** `FolderId` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_create_folder", {"name": "my-name", "parent_folder_id": "your-parent_folder_id", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `delete_comment` :warning: Delete a comment from a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file containing the comment. | | `comment_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the comment to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_delete_comment", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "comment_id": "your-comment_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_file` :warning: Delete a file from Google Drive | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file or folder to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_delete_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_permission` :warning: Delete a permission from a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file. | | `permission_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the permission to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_delete_permission", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "permission_id": "your-permission_id"}) ``` --- ### `download_file` Download a file from Google Drive | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file to download. | **Returns:** `FileDownloadResult` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_download_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `empty_trash` :warning: Empty the trash **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_empty_trash", {}) ``` --- ### `export_file` Export a Google Workspace file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the Google Workspace file to export. | | `mime_type` | `string` | Yes | Target MIME type (e.g., 'application/pdf', 'text/csv', 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document'). | **Returns:** `str` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_export_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "mime_type": "your-mime_type"}) ``` --- ### `get_file` Get file metadata by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file to retrieve. | **Returns:** `DriveFile` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_get_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_permission` Get a specific permission on a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file. | | `permission_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the permission entry. | **Returns:** `FilePermission` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_get_permission", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "permission_id": "your-permission_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_revision` Get a specific revision of a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file. | | `revision_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the revision. | **Returns:** `DriveRevision` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_get_revision", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "revision_id": "your-revision_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_storage_quota` Get storage quota information **Returns:** `StorageQuota` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_get_storage_quota", {}) ``` --- ### `list_comments` List comments on a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file. | | `page_size` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of comments per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page of results. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DriveComment]` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_list_comments", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "page_size": 20, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `list_files` List files in Google Drive | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_size` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of files to return per page (max 1000). (default: `20`) | | `order_by` | `string` | No | Sort order (e.g., 'modifiedTime desc', 'name'). (default: `modifiedTime desc`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page of results. | | `folder_id` | `string` | No | If provided, list only files in this folder. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DriveFile]` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_list_files", {"page_size": 20, "order_by": "modifiedTime desc"}) ``` --- ### `list_permissions` List permissions on a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_list_permissions", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_revisions` List revisions of a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file. | | `page_size` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of revisions per page (max 1000). (default: `20`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page of results. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DriveRevision]` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_list_revisions", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "page_size": 20, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `move_file` Move a file to a different folder | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file to move. | | `new_parent_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the destination folder. | **Returns:** `DriveFile` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_move_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "new_parent_id": "your-new_parent_id"}) ``` --- ### `search_files` Search files in Google Drive | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Drive search query string. | | `page_size` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of results per page. (default: `20`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Pagination token for next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DriveFile]` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_search_files", {"query": "is:unread", "page_size": 20, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `share_file` :warning: Share a file with a user or group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file to share. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | Email address of the user or group to share with. | | `role` | `string` | No | Permission role: 'reader', 'commenter', 'writer', or 'organizer'. (default: `reader`) | | `type` | `string` | No | Permission type: 'user', 'group', 'domain', or 'anyone'. (default: `user`) | | `send_notification` | `boolean` | No | Whether to send an email notification. (default: `True`) | | `message` | `string` | No | Optional message to include in the notification email. | **Returns:** `FilePermission` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_share_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "email": "user@example.com", "role": "reader", "type": "user"}) ``` --- ### `update_file` Update file metadata | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the file to update. | | `name` | `string` | No | New filename. | | `description` | `string` | No | New file description. | | `starred` | `string` | No | Whether to star / unstar the file. | **Returns:** `DriveFile` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_update_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `upload_file` Upload a file to Google Drive | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | The filename for the uploaded file. | | `content_base64` | `string` | Yes | Base64-encoded file content. | | `mime_type` | `string` | No | MIME type of the file. (default: `application/octet-stream`) | | `parent_folder_id` | `string` | No | Optional parent folder ID. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional file description. | **Returns:** `FileUploadResult` ```python result = kit.execute("gdrive_upload_file", {"name": "my-name", "content_base64": "your-content_base64", "mime_type": "application/octet-stream", "parent_folder_id": "your-parent_folder_id"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_files` for filtered queries and `list_comments` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 12,000 requests/minute per project — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_comment`, `delete_comment`, `delete_file`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 22 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [S3](../s3/) — AWS storage --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.
PageState.title: PageState

github.readme_md: # GitHub > Code hosting, issues, PRs, and CI/CD ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Code_Platform-1E293B) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-37-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/PyGithub/PyGithub?style=social)](https://github.com/PyGithub/PyGithub) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/PyGithub/PyGithub) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/octokit/octokit.js) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/google/go-github) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Microsoft (GitHub Inc.) | | **Category** | Code Platform | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [github.com](https://github.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.github.com](https://docs.github.com/en/rest) | | **Auth** | Personal Access Token, OAuth 2.0, GitHub App | | **Rate Limit** | 5,000 requests/hour authenticated | | **Pricing** | Free for public repos, Teams from $4/user/month | --- ## Overview The GitHub REST API provides access to repositories, issues, pull requests, commits, releases, and more. Automate code reviews, manage CI/CD workflows, track issues, and build developer tools that integrate with the GitHub ecosystem. ## Use Cases - CI/CD automation - Issue tracking and triage - Code review workflows - Release management - Developer analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[github]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_GITHUB_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["github"], credentials={"github": "your-token"}) # List repositories for a user or organisation result = kit.execute("github_list_repos", {"org": "your-org", "user": "your-user"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["github"], credentials={"github": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["github"], credentials={"github": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Personal Access Token 1. Settings 2. Developer Settings 3. Personal Access Tokens [Get credentials →](https://github.com/settings/tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("github_list_repos", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_labels` Add labels to an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `issue_number` | `integer` | Yes | The issue number. | | `labels` | `array` | Yes | List of label names to add. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("github_add_labels", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "issue_number": 10, "labels": "your-labels"}) ``` --- ### `create_comment` :warning: Create a comment on an issue or pull request | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `issue_number` | `integer` | Yes | The issue (or PR) number to comment on. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Comment body in Markdown. | **Returns:** `Comment` ```python result = kit.execute("github_create_comment", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "issue_number": 10, "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_gist` :warning: Create a gist | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `files` | `object` | Yes | Mapping of filename to file content. E.g. ``{"hello.py": "print('hello')"}``. | | `description` | `string` | No | Gist description. | | `public` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, create a public gist. (default: `True`) | **Returns:** `GitHubGist` ```python result = kit.execute("github_create_gist", {"files": "your-files", "description": "your-description", "public": True}) ``` --- ### `create_issue` :warning: Create an issue in a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Issue title. | | `body` | `string` | No | Issue body in Markdown. | | `labels` | `string` | No | List of label names to apply. | | `assignees` | `string` | No | List of usernames to assign. | **Returns:** `Issue` ```python result = kit.execute("github_create_issue", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "title": "My Title", "body": "Hello!", "labels": "your-labels"}) ``` --- ### `create_or_update_file` :warning: Create or update a file in a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `path` | `string` | Yes | Path for the file (e.g. ``docs/README.md``). | | `content` | `string` | Yes | File content, **base64-encoded**. | | `message` | `string` | Yes | Commit message. | | `sha` | `string` | No | Current blob SHA of the file (required for updates). | | `branch` | `string` | No | Branch to commit to (defaults to default branch). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("github_create_or_update_file", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "path": "/path/to/file", "content": "your-content", "message": "Hello!", "sha": "your-sha", "branch": "your-branch"}) ``` --- ### `create_pull_request` :warning: Create a pull request | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | PR title. | | `head` | `string` | Yes | Branch (or ``user:branch``) containing your changes. | | `base` | `string` | Yes | Branch you want to merge into (e.g. ``main``). | | `body` | `string` | No | PR description in Markdown. | | `draft` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, create as a draft PR. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `PullRequest` ```python result = kit.execute("github_create_pull_request", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "title": "My Title", "head": "your-head", "base": "your-base", "body": "Hello!", "draft": False}) ``` --- ### `create_release` :warning: Create a release | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `tag_name` | `string` | Yes | Git tag for the release. | | `name` | `string` | No | Release title. | | `body` | `string` | No | Release notes in Markdown. | | `draft` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, create as a draft. (default: `False`) | | `prerelease` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, mark as pre-release. (default: `False`) | | `target_commitish` | `string` | No | Branch or commit SHA to tag (defaults to default branch). | **Returns:** `Release` ```python result = kit.execute("github_create_release", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "tag_name": "your-tag_name", "name": "my-name", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_repo` :warning: Create a new repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `description` | `string` | No | Short description. | | `private` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, create a private repository. (default: `False`) | | `auto_init` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, initialize with a README. (default: `False`) | | `org` | `string` | No | Create under this organisation instead of the user. | **Returns:** `Repository` ```python result = kit.execute("github_create_repo", {"name": "my-name", "description": "your-description", "private": False}) ``` --- ### `delete_file` :warning: Delete a file from a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `path` | `string` | Yes | Path to the file to delete. | | `sha` | `string` | Yes | Current blob SHA of the file (required). | | `message` | `string` | Yes | Commit message. | | `branch` | `string` | No | Branch to commit to. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("github_delete_file", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "path": "/path/to/file", "sha": "your-sha", "message": "Hello!", "branch": "your-branch"}) ``` --- ### `fork_repo` :warning: Fork a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Source repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Source repository name. | | `organization` | `string` | No | Optional org to fork into. | **Returns:** `Repository` ```python result = kit.execute("github_fork_repo", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "organization": "your-organization"}) ``` --- ### `get_authenticated_user` Get the authenticated user's profile **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("github_get_authenticated_user", {}) ``` --- ### `get_branch` Get a single branch | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `branch` | `string` | Yes | Branch name. | **Returns:** `Branch` ```python result = kit.execute("github_get_branch", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "branch": "your-branch"}) ``` --- ### `get_content` Get file or directory contents from a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `path` | `string` | Yes | Path to file or directory (e.g. ``src/main.py``). | | `ref` | `string` | No | Branch, tag, or commit SHA (defaults to default branch). | **Returns:** `FileContent` ```python result = kit.execute("github_get_content", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "path": "/path/to/file", "ref": "your-ref"}) ``` --- ### `get_issue` Get a single issue by number | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `issue_number` | `integer` | Yes | The issue number. | **Returns:** `Issue` ```python result = kit.execute("github_get_issue", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "issue_number": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_latest_release` Get the latest release | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `Release` ```python result = kit.execute("github_get_latest_release", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `get_pull_request` Get a single pull request by number | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `pr_number` | `integer` | Yes | The pull request number. | **Returns:** `PullRequest` ```python result = kit.execute("github_get_pull_request", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "pr_number": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_rate_limit` Get the current rate limit status **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("github_get_rate_limit", {}) ``` --- ### `get_repo` Get a single repository by owner and name | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner (user or org login). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `Repository` ```python result = kit.execute("github_get_repo", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `list_branches` List branches in a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum branches per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Branch]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_list_branches", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "limit": 30, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `list_comments` List comments on an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `issue_number` | `integer` | Yes | The issue (or PR) number. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum comments per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Comment]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_list_comments", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "issue_number": 10, "limit": 30, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `list_commits` List commits for a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `sha` | `string` | No | Branch name or commit SHA to start listing from. | | `path` | `string` | No | Only commits containing this file path. | | `author` | `string` | No | GitHub login or email to filter by. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum commits per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Commit]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_list_commits", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "sha": "your-sha", "path": "/path/to/file"}) ``` --- ### `list_gists` List gists for the authenticated user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum gists per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[GitHubGist]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_list_gists", {"limit": 30, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `list_issues` List issues for a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `state` | `string` | No | Filter by state: ``open``, ``closed``, or ``all``. | | `labels` | `string` | No | Comma-separated list of label names to filter by. | | `assignee` | `string` | No | Filter by assignee username, or ``none`` / ``*``. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum issues per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Issue]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_list_issues", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "state": "open", "labels": "your-labels"}) ``` --- ### `list_pull_requests` List pull requests for a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `state` | `string` | No | Filter by state: ``open``, ``closed``, or ``all``. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum PRs per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PullRequest]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_list_pull_requests", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "state": "open", "limit": 30}) ``` --- ### `list_releases` List releases for a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum releases per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Release]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_list_releases", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "limit": 30, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `list_repos` List repositories for a user or organisation | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `org` | `string` | No | Organisation login name. Lists org repos when provided. | | `user` | `string` | No | Username. Lists that user's repos when provided. If neither is set, lists the authenticated user's repos. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum repositories per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page (from a previous response). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Repository]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_list_repos", {"org": "your-org", "user": "your-user"}) ``` --- ### `list_workflow_runs` List workflow runs for a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `workflow_id` | `string` | No | Filter to a specific workflow. If omitted, returns runs for all workflows. | | `branch` | `string` | No | Filter by branch name. | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status: ``queued``, ``in_progress``, ``completed``, ``success``, ``failure``, etc. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum runs per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[WorkflowRun]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_list_workflow_runs", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "workflow_id": "your-workflow_id", "branch": "your-branch"}) ``` --- ### `list_workflows` List workflows in a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum workflows per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Workflow]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_list_workflows", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "limit": 30, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `merge_pull_request` :warning: Merge a pull request | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `pr_number` | `integer` | Yes | The pull request number. | | `merge_method` | `string` | No | ``merge``, ``squash``, or ``rebase``. (default: `merge`) | | `commit_title` | `string` | No | Custom merge commit title. | | `commit_message` | `string` | No | Custom merge commit message. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("github_merge_pull_request", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "pr_number": 10, "merge_method": "merge", "commit_title": "your-commit_title"}) ``` --- ### `remove_label` :warning: Remove a label from an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `issue_number` | `integer` | Yes | The issue number. | | `label_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the label to remove. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("github_remove_label", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "issue_number": 10, "label_name": "your-label_name"}) ``` --- ### `search_code` Search code across GitHub repositories | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | GitHub code search query (e.g. ``"addClass in:file language:js repo:jquery/jquery"``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CodeSearchResult]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_search_code", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 30, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `search_issues` Search issues and pull requests on GitHub | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query (e.g. ``"is:issue is:open label:bug"``). | | `sort` | `string` | No | Sort field: ``comments``, ``reactions``, ``created``, or ``updated``. Defaults to best match. | | `order` | `string` | No | Sort order: ``asc`` or ``desc``. (default: `desc`) | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Issue]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_search_issues", {"query": "is:unread", "sort": "your-sort", "order": "desc"}) ``` --- ### `search_repos` Search repositories on GitHub | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query (e.g. ``"language:python stars:>1000"``). | | `sort` | `string` | No | Sort field: ``stars``, ``forks``, ``help-wanted-issues``, or ``updated``. Defaults to best match. | | `order` | `string` | No | Sort order: ``asc`` or ``desc``. (default: `desc`) | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Repository]` ```python result = kit.execute("github_search_repos", {"query": "is:unread", "sort": "your-sort", "order": "desc"}) ``` --- ### `star_repo` :warning: Star a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("github_star_repo", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `trigger_workflow` :warning: Trigger a workflow dispatch | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `workflow_id` | `integer` | Yes | Workflow ID or filename (e.g. ``ci.yml``). | | `ref` | `string` | No | Git ref (branch or tag) to run the workflow on. (default: `main`) | | `inputs` | `string` | No | Optional workflow input parameters. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("github_trigger_workflow", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "workflow_id": 10, "ref": "main", "inputs": "your-inputs"}) ``` --- ### `unstar_repo` :warning: Unstar a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("github_unstar_repo", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `update_issue` Update an existing issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owner` | `string` | Yes | Repository owner. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `issue_number` | `integer` | Yes | The issue number. | | `title` | `string` | No | New title. | | `body` | `string` | No | New body in Markdown. | | `state` | `string` | No | New state: ``open`` or ``closed``. | | `labels` | `string` | No | Replace all labels with this list. | | `assignees` | `string` | No | Replace all assignees with this list. | **Returns:** `Issue` ```python result = kit.execute("github_update_issue", {"owner": "owner", "repo": "owner/repo", "issue_number": 10, "title": "My Title", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_code` for filtered queries and `list_branches` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 5,000 requests/hour authenticated — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_comment`, `create_gist`, `create_issue`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 37 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Gitlab](../gitlab/) — DevOps platform --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
