gitlab.readme_md: # GitLab > DevOps platform with Git, CI/CD, and issue tracking ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Code_Platform-1E293B) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-21-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/python-gitlab/python-gitlab?style=social)](https://github.com/python-gitlab/python-gitlab) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/python-gitlab/python-gitlab) [![Ruby (source)](https://img.shields.io/badge/Ruby_(source)-SDK-CC342D?logo=github)](https://github.com/gitlabhq/gitlabhq) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | GitLab Inc. | | **Category** | Code Platform | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [gitlab.com](https://gitlab.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.gitlab.com](https://docs.gitlab.com/api/rest/) | | **Auth** | Personal Access Token, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 2,000 requests/minute authenticated | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Premium from $29/user/month | --- ## Overview The GitLab REST API provides access to projects, merge requests, issues, pipelines, and CI/CD. Manage the full DevOps lifecycle from a single platform. Supports both GitLab.com and self-hosted instances. ## Use Cases - CI/CD pipeline management - Merge request automation - Issue tracking - Release management - Self-hosted DevOps ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[gitlab]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_GITLAB_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["gitlab"], credentials={"gitlab": "your-token"}) # List issues for a GitLab project result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_issues", {"project_id": "proj-123", "state": "open", "labels": "your-labels"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["gitlab"], credentials={"gitlab": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["gitlab"], credentials={"gitlab": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Personal Access Token 1. Preferences 2. Access Tokens 3. Create [Get credentials →](https://gitlab.com/-/user_settings/personal_access_tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_issues", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_branch` :warning: Create a new branch | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `branch_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new branch. | | `ref` | `string` | Yes | The branch name or commit SHA to branch from. | **Returns:** `GitLabBranch` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_create_branch", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "branch_name": "your-branch_name", "ref": "your-ref"}) ``` --- ### `create_comment` :warning: Create a comment on an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `issue_iid` | `integer` | Yes | The issue internal ID (IID). | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Comment text in Markdown format. | **Returns:** `GitLabComment` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_create_comment", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "issue_iid": 10, "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_issue` :warning: Create an issue in a GitLab project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Issue title. | | `description` | `string` | No | Issue description in Markdown. | | `labels` | `string` | No | Comma-separated list of label names to apply. | **Returns:** `GitLabIssue` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_create_issue", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "title": "My Title", "description": "your-description", "labels": "your-labels"}) ``` --- ### `create_label` :warning: Create a label in a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Label name. | | `color` | `string` | Yes | Label color in hex format (e.g. ``"#FF0000"``). | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional label description. | **Returns:** `GitLabLabel` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_create_label", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "name": "my-name", "color": "your-color", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_merge_request` :warning: Create a merge request in a GitLab project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `source_branch` | `string` | Yes | The source branch for the MR. | | `target_branch` | `string` | Yes | The target branch for the MR. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Merge request title. | | `description` | `string` | No | Merge request description in Markdown. | **Returns:** `MergeRequest` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_create_merge_request", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "source_branch": "your-source_branch", "target_branch": "your-target_branch", "title": "My Title", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_milestone` :warning: Create a milestone in a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Milestone title. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional milestone description. | | `due_date` | `string` | No | Due date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | | `start_date` | `string` | No | Start date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | **Returns:** `GitLabMilestone` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_create_milestone", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "title": "My Title", "description": "your-description", "due_date": "your-due_date"}) ``` --- ### `delete_branch` :warning: Delete a branch from a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `branch_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the branch to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_delete_branch", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "branch_name": "your-branch_name"}) ``` --- ### `get_merge_request` Get a single merge request by IID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `mr_iid` | `integer` | Yes | The merge request internal ID (IID). | **Returns:** `MergeRequest` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_get_merge_request", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "mr_iid": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_pipeline` Get a single pipeline by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `pipeline_id` | `integer` | Yes | The pipeline ID. | **Returns:** `Pipeline` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_get_pipeline", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "pipeline_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_project` Get a single project by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or URL-encoded ``namespace/project`` path. | **Returns:** `Project` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_get_project", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_branches` List branches for a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `search` | `string` | No | Optional search string to filter branch names. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum branches per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_branches", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "search": "your-search", "limit": 20}) ``` --- ### `list_issues` List issues for a GitLab project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `state` | `string` | No | Filter by state: ``opened``, ``closed``, or ``all``. | | `labels` | `string` | No | Comma-separated list of label names to filter by. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum issues per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[GitLabIssue]` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_issues", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "state": "open", "labels": "your-labels"}) ``` --- ### `list_jobs` List jobs for a pipeline | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `pipeline_id` | `integer` | Yes | The pipeline ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_jobs", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "pipeline_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_labels` List labels for a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `search` | `string` | No | Optional search string to filter labels by name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum labels per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_labels", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "search": "your-search", "limit": 20}) ``` --- ### `list_merge_requests` List merge requests for a GitLab project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `state` | `string` | No | Filter by state: ``opened``, ``closed``, ``merged``, or ``all``. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum MRs per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[MergeRequest]` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_merge_requests", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "state": "open", "limit": 20}) ``` --- ### `list_milestones` List milestones for a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `state` | `string` | No | Filter by state: ``active`` or ``closed``. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum milestones per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_milestones", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "state": "open", "limit": 20}) ``` --- ### `list_pipelines` List CI/CD pipelines for a GitLab project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status: ``running``, ``pending``, ``success``, ``failed``, ``canceled``, ``skipped``, ``created``, ``manual``. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum pipelines per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Pipeline]` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_pipelines", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "status": "active", "limit": 20}) ``` --- ### `list_project_members` List project members | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `query` | `string` | No | Optional search query to filter members by name/username. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum members per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[GitLabMember]` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_project_members", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "query": "is:unread", "limit": 20}) ``` --- ### `list_projects` List GitLab projects | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `owned` | `string` | No | If true, only return projects owned by the current user. | | `search` | `string` | No | Search projects by name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum projects per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Project]` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_projects", {"owned": "your-owned", "search": "your-search"}) ``` --- ### `list_tags` List tags for a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `search` | `string` | No | Optional search string to filter tag names. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum tags per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_list_tags", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "search": "your-search", "limit": 20}) ``` --- ### `retry_pipeline` :warning: Retry a failed pipeline | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | Numeric project ID or ``namespace/project`` path. | | `pipeline_id` | `integer` | Yes | The pipeline ID to retry. | **Returns:** `Pipeline` ```python result = kit.execute("gitlab_retry_pipeline", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "pipeline_id": 10}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 2,000 requests/minute authenticated — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_branch`, `create_comment`, `create_issue`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 21 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Github](../github/) — Code hosting --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's

    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.

gmail.readme_md:
# Gmail > Read, send, and manage emails programmatically ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Communication-3B82F6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-38-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/googleapis/google-api-python-client?style=social)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-nodejs-client) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-go-client) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Google | | **Category** | Communication | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Base URL** | `https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1` | | **Website** | [mail.google.com](https://mail.google.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.google.com](https://developers.google.com/workspace/gmail/api/reference/rest) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0, Service Account | | **Rate Limit** | 250 requests/second per user | | **Pricing** | Free (Google Workspace required for some features) | --- ## Overview The Gmail API lets you read, compose, send, and organize email messages and threads. Use it to build email automation, customer communication workflows, notification systems, and email analytics pipelines. Supports OAuth 2.0 for user-level access and Service Accounts for domain-wide access in Google Workspace. ## Use Cases - Email automation and scheduling - Customer support ticket creation from emails - Email analytics and reporting - Newsletter management - Automated replies and forwarding rules - Email migration between accounts - Audit and compliance monitoring ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[gmail]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_GMAIL_CREDENTIALS=your-oauth-access-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["gmail"], credentials={"gmail": "your-token"}) # List unread emails result = kit.execute("gmail_list_emails", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 5}) print(result) # Send an email result = kit.execute("gmail_send_email", { "to": "recipient@example.com", "subject": "Hello from ToolsConnector", "body": "

Hello!

Sent via Gmail connector.

" }) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["gmail"], credentials={"gmail": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["gmail"], credentials={"gmail": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 (recommended for user accounts) 1. Go to [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) 2. Create a new project (or select existing) 3. Enable the **Gmail API** under APIs & Services 4. Create **OAuth 2.0 Client ID** credentials 5. Set authorized redirect URIs for your application 6. Use the obtained access token as your credential ### Service Account (for domain-wide access) 1. Create a service account in Google Cloud Console 2. Enable domain-wide delegation in Google Workspace Admin 3. Download the JSON key file 4. Use the access token obtained via the service account ### Scopes | Scope | Access Level | |---|---| | `gmail.readonly` | Read emails, threads, labels | | `gmail.send` | Send emails and drafts | | `gmail.modify` | Full read/write access | | `gmail.labels` | Manage labels only | ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError, NotFoundError try: result = kit.execute("gmail_list_emails", {"query": "is:unread"}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") except NotFoundError as e: print(f"Email not found: {e.message}") ``` ## Actions ### `batch_delete` :warning: Batch delete messages permanently | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of message IDs to delete (max 1000). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_batch_delete", {"email_ids": "your-email_ids"}) ``` --- ### `batch_modify` Batch modify labels on multiple emails | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of message IDs to modify (max 1000). | | `add_labels` | `string` | No | Label IDs to add to all specified messages. | | `remove_labels` | `string` | No | Label IDs to remove from all specified messages. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_batch_modify", {"email_ids": "your-email_ids", "add_labels": "your-add_labels", "remove_labels": "your-remove_labels"}) ``` --- ### `create_draft` Create a draft email | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `string` | Yes | Recipient email address. | | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Email subject line. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Email body (supports HTML). | | `cc` | `string` | No | Optional CC recipients. | **Returns:** `DraftId` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_create_draft", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "subject": "Hello", "body": "Hello!", "cc": "your-cc"}) ``` --- ### `create_label` Create a new label | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the label. | | `label_color` | `string` | No | Optional color specification with text_color and background_color hex values. | **Returns:** `Label` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_create_label", {"name": "my-name", "label_color": "your-label_color"}) ``` --- ### `delete_draft` :warning: Delete a draft | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `draft_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the draft to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_delete_draft", {"draft_id": "your-draft_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_email` :warning: Delete an email | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the email to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_delete_email", {"email_id": "your-email_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_label` :warning: Delete a label | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `label_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the label to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_delete_label", {"label_id": "your-label_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_thread` :warning: Permanently delete a thread | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `thread_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the thread to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_delete_thread", {"thread_id": "your-thread_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_attachment` Download an email attachment | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the email containing the attachment. | | `attachment_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the attachment to download. | **Returns:** `Attachment` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_get_attachment", {"email_id": "your-email_id", "attachment_id": "your-attachment_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_draft` Get a single draft by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `draft_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the draft to retrieve. | | `format` | `string` | No | Response format: 'full', 'metadata', or 'minimal'. (default: `full`) | **Returns:** `Draft` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_get_draft", {"draft_id": "your-draft_id", "format": "full"}) ``` --- ### `get_email` Get a single email by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the email message to retrieve. | | `format` | `string` | No | Response format: 'full', 'metadata', 'minimal', or 'raw'. (default: `full`) | **Returns:** `Email` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_get_email", {"email_id": "your-email_id", "format": "full"}) ``` --- ### `get_label` Get a single label by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `label_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the label to retrieve. | **Returns:** `Label` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_get_label", {"label_id": "your-label_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_profile` Get user profile **Returns:** `UserProfile` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_get_profile", {}) ``` --- ### `get_thread` Get a single thread by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `thread_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the thread to retrieve. | | `format` | `string` | No | Response format: 'full', 'metadata', or 'minimal'. (default: `metadata`) | **Returns:** `Thread` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_get_thread", {"thread_id": "your-thread_id", "format": "metadata"}) ``` --- ### `get_vacation_settings` Get vacation auto-reply settings **Returns:** `VacationSettings` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_get_vacation_settings", {}) ``` --- ### `import_message` :warning: Import an email message into the mailbox | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `raw_rfc2822` | `string` | Yes | The complete RFC 2822 formatted email as a string. | | `label_ids` | `string` | No | Labels to apply (e.g., ["INBOX", "UNREAD"]). | | `internal_date_source` | `string` | No | How to determine the internal date. "dateHeader" uses the Date header, "receivedTime" uses the import time. (default: `dateHeader`) | **Returns:** `MessageId` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_import_message", {"raw_rfc2822": "your-raw_rfc2822", "label_ids": "your-label_ids", "internal_date_source": "dateHeader"}) ``` --- ### `insert_message` :warning: Insert an email message directly into the mailbox | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `raw_rfc2822` | `string` | Yes | The complete RFC 2822 formatted email as a string. | | `label_ids` | `string` | No | Labels to apply. | | `internal_date_source` | `string` | No | "receivedTime" (default) or "dateHeader". (default: `receivedTime`) | **Returns:** `MessageId` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_insert_message", {"raw_rfc2822": "your-raw_rfc2822", "label_ids": "your-label_ids", "internal_date_source": "receivedTime"}) ``` --- ### `list_drafts` List drafts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of drafts to return per page. (default: `10`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page of results. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Draft]` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_list_drafts", {"limit": 10, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `list_emails` List emails matching a query | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | No | Gmail search query (same syntax as Gmail search bar). (default: `is:unread`) | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of emails to return per page. (default: `10`) | | `labels` | `string` | No | Filter by label IDs (e.g., ["INBOX", "IMPORTANT"]). | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page of results. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Email]` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_list_emails", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_history` List mailbox history | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `start_history_id` | `string` | Yes | History ID to start listing from. Only changes after this ID are returned. | | `label_id` | `string` | No | Only return history for this label ID. | | `history_types` | `string` | No | Filter by change type. Valid values: ``messageAdded``, ``messageDeleted``, ``labelAdded``, ``labelRemoved``. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of history records per page. (default: `100`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page of results. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[HistoryRecord]` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_list_history", {"start_history_id": "your-start_history_id", "label_id": "your-label_id", "history_types": "your-history_types"}) ``` --- ### `list_labels` List all labels **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_list_labels", {}) ``` --- ### `list_threads` List email threads | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | No | Gmail search query to filter threads (same syntax as the Gmail search bar). Omit to list all threads. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of threads to return per page. (default: `10`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page of results. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Thread]` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_list_threads", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `mark_as_read` Mark an email as read | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the email to mark as read. | **Returns:** `Email` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_mark_as_read", {"email_id": "your-email_id"}) ``` --- ### `mark_as_unread` Mark an email as unread | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the email to mark as unread. | **Returns:** `Email` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_mark_as_unread", {"email_id": "your-email_id"}) ``` --- ### `modify_labels` Modify email labels | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the email to modify. | | `add_labels` | `string` | No | Labels to add (e.g., ["STARRED", "IMPORTANT"]). | | `remove_labels` | `string` | No | Labels to remove (e.g., ["UNREAD"]). | **Returns:** `Email` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_modify_labels", {"email_id": "your-email_id", "add_labels": "your-add_labels", "remove_labels": "your-remove_labels"}) ``` --- ### `modify_thread` Modify thread labels | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `thread_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the thread to modify. | | `add_labels` | `string` | No | Label IDs to add (e.g., ["STARRED", "IMPORTANT"]). | | `remove_labels` | `string` | No | Label IDs to remove (e.g., ["UNREAD"]). | **Returns:** `Thread` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_modify_thread", {"thread_id": "your-thread_id", "add_labels": "your-add_labels", "remove_labels": "your-remove_labels"}) ``` --- ### `search_emails` Search emails with advanced query | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Gmail search query (e.g., "from:boss has:attachment after:2024/01/01"). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page. (default: `25`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Pagination token for next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Email]` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_search_emails", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 25, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `send_draft` :warning: Send a draft | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `draft_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the draft to send. | **Returns:** `MessageId` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_send_draft", {"draft_id": "your-draft_id"}) ``` --- ### `send_email` :warning: Send an email | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `string` | Yes | Recipient email address. | | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Email subject line. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Email body (supports HTML). | | `cc` | `string` | No | Optional CC recipients. | | `bcc` | `string` | No | Optional BCC recipients. | | `reply_to` | `string` | No | Optional reply-to address. | **Returns:** `MessageId` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_send_email", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "subject": "Hello", "body": "Hello!", "cc": "your-cc", "bcc": "your-bcc"}) ``` --- ### `star_email` Star an email | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the email to star. | **Returns:** `Email` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_star_email", {"email_id": "your-email_id"}) ``` --- ### `trash_email` Move an email to trash | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the email to trash. | **Returns:** `Email` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_trash_email", {"email_id": "your-email_id"}) ``` --- ### `trash_thread` Move a thread to trash | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `thread_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the thread to trash. | **Returns:** `Thread` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_trash_thread", {"thread_id": "your-thread_id"}) ``` --- ### `unstar_email` Remove star from an email | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the email to unstar. | **Returns:** `Email` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_unstar_email", {"email_id": "your-email_id"}) ``` --- ### `untrash_email` Remove an email from trash | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the email to untrash. | **Returns:** `Email` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_untrash_email", {"email_id": "your-email_id"}) ``` --- ### `untrash_thread` Remove a thread from trash | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `thread_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the thread to untrash. | **Returns:** `Thread` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_untrash_thread", {"thread_id": "your-thread_id"}) ``` --- ### `update_draft` Update a draft | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `draft_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the draft to update. | | `to` | `string` | Yes | Recipient email address. | | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Email subject line. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Email body (supports HTML). | | `cc` | `string` | No | Optional CC recipients. | **Returns:** `Draft` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_update_draft", {"draft_id": "your-draft_id", "to": "recipient@example.com", "subject": "Hello", "body": "Hello!", "cc": "your-cc"}) ``` --- ### `update_label` Update a label | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `label_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the label to update. | | `name` | `string` | No | New display name for the label. | | `label_color` | `string` | No | Optional new color specification with text_color and background_color hex values. | **Returns:** `Label` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_update_label", {"label_id": "your-label_id", "name": "my-name", "label_color": "your-label_color"}) ``` --- ### `update_vacation_settings` :warning: Update vacation auto-reply settings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `enable` | `boolean` | Yes | Whether to enable the vacation auto-reply. | | `response_subject` | `string` | No | Subject line for the auto-reply message. | | `response_body` | `string` | No | Body of the auto-reply (supports HTML). | | `start_time` | `string` | No | Start time as epoch milliseconds string. Required when enabling auto-reply. | | `end_time` | `string` | No | End time as epoch milliseconds string. Required when enabling auto-reply. | **Returns:** `VacationSettings` ```python result = kit.execute("gmail_update_vacation_settings", {"enable": True, "response_subject": "your-response_subject", "response_body": "your-response_body"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use Gmail search syntax in the `query` parameter: `from:boss@company.com has:attachment after:2024/01/01` - For bulk operations, use `batch_modify` and `batch_delete` instead of individual calls - Thread operations (`list_threads`, `get_thread`) group related messages together - Use `list_history` with a `startHistoryId` for efficient incremental sync - Labels are case-sensitive: `"INBOX"`, `"UNREAD"`, `"STARRED"` - The `trash_email` action is reversible (use `untrash_email`), but `delete_email` is permanent ## Related Connectors - [Google Drive](../gdrive/) — File storage and sharing - [Google Calendar](../gcalendar/) — Event scheduling - [Google Sheets](../gsheets/) — Spreadsheet data - [Microsoft Outlook](../outlook/) — Alternative email connector - [SendGrid](../sendgrid/) — Transactional email sending --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

gsheets.readme_md: # Google Sheets > Read, write, and format spreadsheet data ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Productivity-F97316) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-16-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/googleapis/google-api-python-client?style=social)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Google | | **Category** | Productivity | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [sheets.google.com](https://sheets.google.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.google.com](https://developers.google.com/sheets/api/reference/rest) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0, Service Account | | **Rate Limit** | 300 requests/minute per project | | **Pricing** | Free with Google account | --- ## Overview The Google Sheets API provides full access to spreadsheet data and structure. Read and write cell values, create and format sheets, apply batch operations, and use Sheets as a lightweight database or reporting layer for your applications. ## Use Cases - Data entry and extraction - Automated reporting - Spreadsheet as database - Data pipelines and ETL - Budget and inventory tracking ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[gsheets]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_GSHEETS_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["gsheets"], credentials={"gsheets": "your-token"}) # Get values from a range result = kit.execute("gsheets_get_values", {"spreadsheet_id": "sheet-123", "range": "your-range"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["gsheets"], credentials={"gsheets": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["gsheets"], credentials={"gsheets": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 1. Google Cloud Console 2. Credentials [Get credentials →](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("gsheets_get_values", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_sheet` :warning: Add a new sheet tab | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the spreadsheet. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new sheet tab. | | `row_count` | `string` | No | Optional initial row count. | | `column_count` | `string` | No | Optional initial column count. | **Returns:** `Sheet` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_add_sheet", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "title": "My Title", "row_count": "your-row_count", "column_count": "your-column_count"}) ``` --- ### `append_values` :warning: Append values after a range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the spreadsheet. | | `range` | `string` | Yes | A1 notation range that defines the table to append to (e.g., ``'Sheet1!A1:D1'``). | | `values` | `array` | Yes | 2D list of cell values to append. | | `input_option` | `string` | No | How to interpret the input data (``'RAW'`` or ``'USER_ENTERED'``). (default: `USER_ENTERED`) | **Returns:** `AppendResult` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_append_values", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "range": "your-range", "values": "your-values", "input_option": "USER_ENTERED"}) ``` --- ### `auto_resize_columns` Auto-resize columns to fit content | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The spreadsheet ID. | | `sheet_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric sheet ID. | | `start_column` | `integer` | No | Start column index (0-indexed, default 0 = A). (default: `0`) | | `end_column` | `integer` | No | End column index (exclusive, default 26 = Z). (default: `26`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_auto_resize_columns", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "sheet_id": 10, "start_column": 0, "end_column": 26}) ``` --- ### `batch_get_values` Batch get values from multiple ranges | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the spreadsheet. | | `ranges` | `array` | Yes | List of A1 notation ranges to read. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_batch_get_values", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "ranges": "your-ranges"}) ``` --- ### `batch_update_spreadsheet` :warning: Apply structural changes to a spreadsheet | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The spreadsheet ID. | | `requests` | `array` | Yes | List of request dicts. Each dict should contain exactly one request type as its key. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_batch_update_spreadsheet", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "requests": "your-requests"}) ``` --- ### `batch_update_values` :warning: Batch update values across multiple ranges | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the spreadsheet. | | `data` | `array` | Yes | List of dicts, each with ``range`` (str) and ``values`` (2D list). Example:: [ {"range": "Sheet1!A1:B2", "values": [[1, 2], [3, 4]]}, {"range": "Sheet1!D1:E2", "values": [["a", "b"]]}, ] | | `input_option` | `string` | No | How to interpret the input data (``'RAW'`` or ``'USER_ENTERED'``). (default: `USER_ENTERED`) | **Returns:** `BatchUpdateResult` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_batch_update_values", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "data": "your-data", "input_option": "USER_ENTERED"}) ``` --- ### `clear_values` :warning: Clear values from a range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the spreadsheet. | | `range` | `string` | Yes | A1 notation range to clear (e.g., ``'Sheet1!A1:D10'``). | **Returns:** `ClearResult` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_clear_values", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "range": "your-range"}) ``` --- ### `copy_sheet` Copy a sheet to another spreadsheet | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The source spreadsheet ID. | | `sheet_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric sheet ID to copy. | | `destination_spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The target spreadsheet ID. | **Returns:** `Sheet` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_copy_sheet", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "sheet_id": 10, "destination_spreadsheet_id": "your-destination_spreadsheet_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_spreadsheet` :warning: Create a new spreadsheet | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | Title for the new spreadsheet. | | `sheet_names` | `string` | No | Optional list of sheet tab names to create. If omitted, Google creates a single default sheet. | **Returns:** `Spreadsheet` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_create_spreadsheet", {"title": "My Title", "sheet_names": "your-sheet_names"}) ``` --- ### `delete_sheet` :warning: Delete a sheet tab | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the spreadsheet. | | `sheet_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric sheet ID to delete (not the tab name). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_delete_sheet", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "sheet_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_sheet_metadata` Get sheet metadata for all tabs | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the spreadsheet. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_get_sheet_metadata", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_spreadsheet` Get spreadsheet metadata and sheets | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve. | **Returns:** `Spreadsheet` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_get_spreadsheet", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_values` Get values from a range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the spreadsheet. | | `range` | `string` | Yes | A1 notation range (e.g., ``'Sheet1!A1:D10'``). | **Returns:** `SheetValues` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_get_values", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "range": "your-range"}) ``` --- ### `merge_cells` :warning: Merge cells in a range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The spreadsheet ID. | | `sheet_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric sheet ID. | | `start_row` | `integer` | Yes | Start row (0-indexed). | | `end_row` | `integer` | Yes | End row (exclusive). | | `start_column` | `integer` | Yes | Start column (0-indexed). | | `end_column` | `integer` | Yes | End column (exclusive). | | `merge_type` | `string` | No | "MERGE_ALL", "MERGE_COLUMNS", or "MERGE_ROWS". (default: `MERGE_ALL`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_merge_cells", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "sheet_id": 10, "start_row": 10, "end_row": 10, "start_column": 10, "end_column": 10, "merge_type": "MERGE_ALL"}) ``` --- ### `rename_sheet` :warning: Rename a sheet tab within a spreadsheet | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The spreadsheet ID. | | `sheet_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric sheet ID (not the sheet name). | | `new_title` | `string` | Yes | The new name for the sheet tab. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_rename_sheet", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "sheet_id": 10, "new_title": "your-new_title"}) ``` --- ### `update_values` :warning: Update values in a range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `spreadsheet_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the spreadsheet. | | `range` | `string` | Yes | A1 notation range to write to (e.g., ``'Sheet1!A1:D10'``). | | `values` | `array` | Yes | 2D list of cell values to write. | | `input_option` | `string` | No | How to interpret the input data. Either ``'RAW'`` (no parsing) or ``'USER_ENTERED'`` (parsed as if typed into the UI). (default: `USER_ENTERED`) | **Returns:** `UpdateResult` ```python result = kit.execute("gsheets_update_values", {"spreadsheet_id": "your-spreadsheet_id", "range": "your-range", "values": "your-values", "input_option": "USER_ENTERED"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use batch operations (`batch_get_values`, `batch_update_spreadsheet`, `batch_update_values`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls - Rate limit is 300 requests/minute per project — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_sheet`, `append_values`, `batch_update_spreadsheet`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Gcalendar](../gcalendar/) — Calendar - [Gdocs](../gdocs/) — Documents - [Gtasks](../gtasks/) — Task lists - [Figma](../figma/) — Design --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

gtasks.readme_md: # Google Tasks > Manage task lists and to-do items ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Productivity-F97316) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-13-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/googleapis/google-api-python-client?style=social)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Google | | **Category** | Productivity | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [tasks.google.com](https://tasks.google.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.google.com](https://developers.google.com/tasks/reference/rest) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 300 requests/minute per project | | **Pricing** | Free with Google account | --- ## Overview The Google Tasks API provides access to task lists and individual tasks. Create, update, complete, and organize tasks programmatically. Integrates naturally with Google Calendar and Gmail for productivity workflows. ## Use Cases - Task automation - Project tracking - To-do list sync - Workflow triggers on task completion ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[gtasks]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_GTASKS_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["gtasks"], credentials={"gtasks": "your-token"}) # List tasks in a task list result = kit.execute("gtasks_list_tasks", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id", "completed": "your-completed", "due_min": "your-due_min"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["gtasks"], credentials={"gtasks": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["gtasks"], credentials={"gtasks": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 1. Google Cloud Console 2. Credentials [Get credentials →](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("gtasks_list_tasks", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `clear_completed` :warning: Clear completed tasks from a list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_list_id` | `string` | Yes | The task list ID. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_clear_completed", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id"}) ``` --- ### `complete_task` :warning: Complete a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_list_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task list containing the task. | | `task_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task to complete. | **Returns:** `GoogleTask` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_complete_task", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id", "task_id": "task-123"}) ``` --- ### `create_task` :warning: Create a new task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_list_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task list to add the task to. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Title for the new task. | | `notes` | `string` | No | Optional notes/description for the task. | | `due` | `string` | No | Optional due date as RFC 3339 timestamp. | **Returns:** `GoogleTask` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_create_task", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id", "title": "My Title", "notes": "your-notes", "due": "your-due"}) ``` --- ### `create_task_list` :warning: Create a new task list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | Title for the new task list. | **Returns:** `TaskList` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_create_task_list", {"title": "My Title"}) ``` --- ### `delete_task` :warning: Delete a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_list_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task list containing the task. | | `task_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_delete_task", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id", "task_id": "task-123"}) ``` --- ### `delete_task_list` :warning: Delete a task list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_list_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task list to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_delete_task_list", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_task` Get a task by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_list_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task list containing the task. | | `task_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task. | **Returns:** `GoogleTask` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_get_task", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id", "task_id": "task-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_task_list` Get a task list by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_list_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task list. | **Returns:** `TaskList` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_get_task_list", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_task_lists` List all task lists | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of task lists to return (max 100). | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page of results. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[TaskList]` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_list_task_lists", {"limit": "your-limit", "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `list_tasks` List tasks in a task list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_list_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task list. | | `completed` | `string` | No | If True, include only completed tasks. If False, include only incomplete tasks. If None, include all. | | `due_min` | `string` | No | Lower bound for task due date (RFC 3339 timestamp). | | `due_max` | `string` | No | Upper bound for task due date (RFC 3339 timestamp). | | `show_hidden` | `string` | No | Whether to show hidden and deleted tasks. | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[GoogleTask]` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_list_tasks", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id", "completed": "your-completed", "due_min": "your-due_min"}) ``` --- ### `move_task` Move a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_list_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task list. | | `task_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task to move. | | `parent` | `string` | No | Optional parent task ID to nest under (makes it a subtask). Pass ``None`` to move to the top level. | | `previous` | `string` | No | Optional ID of the task to position after. If omitted, the task is moved to the first position. | **Returns:** `GoogleTask` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_move_task", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id", "task_id": "task-123", "parent": "your-parent", "previous": "your-previous"}) ``` --- ### `update_task` :warning: Update a task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_list_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task list containing the task. | | `task_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the task to update. | | `title` | `string` | No | New title for the task. | | `notes` | `string` | No | New notes/description for the task. | | `status` | `string` | No | New status (``'needsAction'`` or ``'completed'``). | | `due` | `string` | No | New due date as RFC 3339 timestamp. | **Returns:** `GoogleTask` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_update_task", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id", "task_id": "task-123", "title": "My Title", "notes": "your-notes"}) ``` --- ### `update_task_list` :warning: Update a task list title | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_list_id` | `string` | Yes | The task list ID. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | New title for the task list. | **Returns:** `TaskList` ```python result = kit.execute("gtasks_update_task_list", {"task_list_id": "your-task_list_id", "title": "My Title"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 300 requests/minute per project — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`clear_completed`, `complete_task`, `create_task`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Gcalendar](../gcalendar/) — Calendar - [Gdocs](../gdocs/) — Documents - [Gsheets](../gsheets/) — Spreadsheets - [Figma](../figma/) — Design --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

hubspot.readme_md: # HubSpot > CRM, marketing, sales, and service hub ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Crm-F59E0B) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-19-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/HubSpot/hubspot-api-python?style=social)](https://github.com/HubSpot/hubspot-api-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/HubSpot/hubspot-api-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/HubSpot/hubspot-api-nodejs) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | HubSpot Inc. | | **Category** | Crm | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [hubspot.com](https://hubspot.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.hubspot.com](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/api/crm) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token (Private App), OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 100 requests/10 seconds (private apps) | | **Pricing** | Free CRM, Starter from $20/month | --- ## Overview The HubSpot CRM API provides access to contacts, companies, deals, tickets, and pipelines. Search and filter records, manage sales pipelines, and build marketing automation workflows on HubSpot's platform. ## Use Cases - Contact management - Deal pipeline tracking - Marketing automation - Customer support ticketing - Sales analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[hubspot]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_HUBSPOT_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["hubspot"], credentials={"hubspot": "your-token"}) # List contacts result = kit.execute("hubspot_list_contacts", {"limit": 100, "after": "your-after"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["hubspot"], credentials={"hubspot": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["hubspot"], credentials={"hubspot": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token (Private App) 1. Settings 2. Integrations 3. Private Apps 4. Create [Get credentials →](https://app.hubspot.com/settings) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("hubspot_list_contacts", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_company` :warning: Create a new company | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Company name (required). | | `domain` | `string` | No | Company domain / website. | | `properties` | `string` | No | Additional property key-value pairs. | **Returns:** `HubSpotCompany` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_create_company", {"name": "my-name", "domain": "your-domain", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ### `create_contact` :warning: Create a new contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | Yes | Email address (required). | | `firstname` | `string` | No | First name. | | `lastname` | `string` | No | Last name. | | `phone` | `string` | No | Phone number. | | `company` | `string` | No | Company name. | **Returns:** `HubSpotContact` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_create_contact", {"email": "user@example.com", "firstname": "your-firstname", "lastname": "your-lastname"}) ``` --- ### `create_deal` :warning: Create a new deal | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dealname` | `string` | Yes | Name of the deal (required). | | `pipeline` | `string` | No | Pipeline ID (defaults to the default pipeline). | | `dealstage` | `string` | No | Deal stage ID within the pipeline. | | `amount` | `string` | No | Deal monetary amount. | **Returns:** `HubSpotDeal` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_create_deal", {"dealname": "your-dealname", "pipeline": "your-pipeline", "dealstage": "your-dealstage"}) ``` --- ### `create_ticket` :warning: Create a new ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Ticket subject (required). | | `pipeline` | `string` | No | Pipeline ID for the ticket. | | `stage` | `string` | No | Pipeline stage ID. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Ticket priority (e.g., ``"HIGH"``). | **Returns:** `HubSpotTicket` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_create_ticket", {"subject": "Hello", "pipeline": "your-pipeline", "stage": "your-stage"}) ``` --- ### `delete_contact` :warning: Delete a contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The HubSpot contact ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_delete_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_deal` :warning: Delete a deal | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `deal_id` | `string` | Yes | The HubSpot deal ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_delete_deal", {"deal_id": "your-deal_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_company` Get a single company by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company_id` | `string` | Yes | The HubSpot company ID. | **Returns:** `HubSpotCompany` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_get_company", {"company_id": "your-company_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_contact` Get a single contact by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The HubSpot contact ID. | **Returns:** `HubSpotContact` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_get_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_deal` Get a single deal by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `deal_id` | `string` | Yes | The HubSpot deal ID. | **Returns:** `HubSpotDeal` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_get_deal", {"deal_id": "your-deal_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_ticket` Get a single ticket by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `string` | Yes | The HubSpot ticket ID. | **Returns:** `HubSpotTicket` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_get_ticket", {"ticket_id": "ticket-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_companies` List companies | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `100`) | | `after` | `string` | No | Cursor token from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[HubSpotCompany]` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_list_companies", {"limit": 100, "after": "your-after"}) ``` --- ### `list_contacts` List contacts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `100`) | | `after` | `string` | No | Cursor token from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[HubSpotContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_list_contacts", {"limit": 100, "after": "your-after"}) ``` --- ### `list_deals` List deals | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `100`) | | `after` | `string` | No | Cursor token from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[HubSpotDeal]` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_list_deals", {"limit": 100, "after": "your-after"}) ``` --- ### `list_pipelines` List pipelines for an object type | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `object_type` | `string` | No | The CRM object type (e.g., ``"deals"``, ``"tickets"``). Defaults to ``"deals"``. (default: `deals`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_list_pipelines", {"object_type": "deals"}) ``` --- ### `list_tickets` List tickets | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `100`) | | `after` | `string` | No | Cursor token from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[HubSpotTicket]` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_list_tickets", {"limit": 100, "after": "your-after"}) ``` --- ### `search_contacts` Search contacts by query | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query string. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results to return (max 100). (default: `10`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[HubSpotContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_search_contacts", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `update_contact` Update an existing contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The HubSpot contact ID. | | `properties` | `object` | Yes | Dict of property names to new values. | **Returns:** `HubSpotContact` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_update_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ### `update_deal` Update an existing deal | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `deal_id` | `string` | Yes | The HubSpot deal ID. | | `properties` | `object` | Yes | Dict of property names to new values. | **Returns:** `HubSpotDeal` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_update_deal", {"deal_id": "your-deal_id", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ### `update_ticket` Update an existing ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `string` | Yes | The HubSpot ticket ID. | | `properties` | `object` | Yes | Dict of property names to new values. | **Returns:** `HubSpotTicket` ```python result = kit.execute("hubspot_update_ticket", {"ticket_id": "ticket-123", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_contacts` for filtered queries and `list_companies` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 100 requests/10 seconds (private apps) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_company`, `create_contact`, `create_deal`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Salesforce](../salesforce/) — Enterprise CRM - [Freshdesk](../freshdesk/) — Helpdesk - [Intercom](../intercom/) — Customer messaging - [Zendesk](../zendesk/) — Support ticketing --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

intercom.readme_md: # Intercom > Customer messaging and engagement platform ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Crm-F59E0B) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-16-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/intercom/python-intercom?style=social)](https://github.com/intercom/python-intercom) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/intercom/python-intercom) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Intercom Inc. | | **Category** | Crm | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [intercom.com](https://intercom.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.intercom.com](https://developers.intercom.com/docs/references/rest-api/api.intercom.io/) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token (Access Token) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan and endpoint | | **Pricing** | From $39/seat/month | --- ## Overview The Intercom API provides access to contacts, conversations, articles, teams, and tags. Manage customer communication, automate messaging workflows, build help center content, and integrate with your CRM. ## Use Cases - Customer communication - In-app messaging - Help center management - Lead qualification - User engagement tracking ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[intercom]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_INTERCOM_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["intercom"], credentials={"intercom": "your-token"}) # List contacts result = kit.execute("intercom_list_contacts", {"limit": 50, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["intercom"], credentials={"intercom": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["intercom"], credentials={"intercom": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token (Access Token) 1. Create an account at [Intercom](https://intercom.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://app.intercom.com/a/apps/_/developer-hub) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("intercom_list_contacts", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `close_conversation` Close a conversation | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `conversation_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom conversation ID. | **Returns:** `IntercomConversation` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_close_conversation", {"conversation_id": "your-conversation_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_contact` :warning: Create a new contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `role` | `string` | No | Contact role: ``"user"`` or ``"lead"``. (default: `user`) | | `email` | `string` | No | Email address. | | `name` | `string` | No | Full name. | | `phone` | `string` | No | Phone number. | **Returns:** `IntercomContact` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_create_contact", {"role": "user", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `create_message` :warning: Create and send a new message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `from_email` | `string` | Yes | Admin email sending the message. | | `to_email` | `string` | Yes | Recipient contact email address. | | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Message subject line. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Message body (HTML allowed). | **Returns:** `IntercomMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_create_message", {"from_email": "your-from_email", "to_email": "your-to_email", "subject": "Hello", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_note` :warning: Create a note on a contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom contact ID. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Note body text (HTML allowed). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_create_note", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `get_admin` Get a single admin by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `admin_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom admin ID. | **Returns:** `IntercomAdmin` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_get_admin", {"admin_id": "your-admin_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_contact` Get a single contact by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom contact ID. | **Returns:** `IntercomContact` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_get_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_conversation` Get a single conversation by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `conversation_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom conversation ID. | **Returns:** `IntercomConversation` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_get_conversation", {"conversation_id": "your-conversation_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_admins` List all admins in the workspace **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_list_admins", {}) ``` --- ### `list_contacts` List contacts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 150). (default: `50`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[IntercomContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_list_contacts", {"limit": 50, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_conversations` List conversations | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 150). (default: `20`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[IntercomConversation]` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_list_conversations", {"limit": 20, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_notes` List notes on a contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom contact ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_list_notes", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_tags` List all tags in the workspace **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_list_tags", {}) ``` --- ### `reply_to_conversation` :warning: Reply to a conversation | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `conversation_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom conversation ID. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Reply body text. | | `message_type` | `string` | No | Type of reply: ``"comment"`` or ``"note"``. (default: `comment`) | **Returns:** `IntercomConversation` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_reply_to_conversation", {"conversation_id": "your-conversation_id", "body": "Hello!", "message_type": "comment"}) ``` --- ### `search_contacts` Search contacts using filters | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | The value to search for. | | `field` | `string` | No | The contact field to search on (e.g. ``"email"``, ``"name"``, ``"phone"``). (default: `email`) | | `operator` | `string` | No | Comparison operator (``"="``, ``"!="``, ``"~"``, ``"contains"``, ``"starts_with"``). (default: `=`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[IntercomContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_search_contacts", {"query": "is:unread", "field": "email", "operator": "="}) ``` --- ### `tag_contact` :warning: Tag a contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom contact ID. | | `tag_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the tag to apply. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_tag_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id", "tag_name": "your-tag_name"}) ``` --- ### `update_contact` Update an existing contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom contact ID. | | `name` | `string` | No | New full name. | | `email` | `string` | No | New email address. | **Returns:** `IntercomContact` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_update_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id", "name": "my-name", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_contacts` for filtered queries and `list_admins` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is Varies by plan and endpoint — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_contact`, `create_message`, `create_note`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Salesforce](../salesforce/) — Enterprise CRM - [Hubspot](../hubspot/) — Marketing & sales CRM - [Freshdesk](../freshdesk/) — Helpdesk - [Zendesk](../zendesk/) — Support ticketing --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*
