jira.readme_md: # Jira > Project tracking, issue management, and agile boards ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Project_Management-8B5CF6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-28-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/atlassian/python-jira?style=social)](https://github.com/atlassian/python-jira) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/atlassian/python-jira) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Atlassian | | **Category** | Project Management | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [www.atlassian.com/software/jira](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira) | | **API Docs** | [developer.atlassian.com](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest/v3/) | | **Auth** | Basic Auth (email:api_token), OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 100 requests/minute | | **Pricing** | Free up to 10 users, Standard from $8.15/user/month | --- ## Overview The Jira REST API provides access to issues, projects, sprints, boards, and workflows. Search with JQL, manage issue transitions, track time with worklogs, and automate agile project management across teams. ## Use Cases - Issue tracking automation - Sprint planning - Bug triage workflows - Cross-tool project sync - Custom reporting ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[jira]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_JIRA_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["jira"], credentials={"jira": "your-token"}) # List projects accessible to the user result = kit.execute("jira_list_projects", {"limit": 50, "start_at": 0}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["jira"], credentials={"jira": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["jira"], credentials={"jira": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Basic Auth (email:api_token) 1. Account Settings 2. Security 3. API Tokens [Get credentials →](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("jira_list_projects", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_attachment` :warning: Add an attachment to an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `filename` | `string` | Yes | The name of the file being uploaded. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Raw file content as bytes. | **Returns:** `JiraAttachment` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_add_attachment", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "filename": "your-filename", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `add_comment` :warning: Add a comment to an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Comment text. Sent as ADF (Atlassian Document Format). | **Returns:** `JiraComment` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_add_comment", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `add_watcher` Add a watcher to an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | The Atlassian account ID of the user to add as a watcher. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_add_watcher", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "account_id": "your-account_id"}) ``` --- ### `add_worklog` :warning: Add a worklog entry to an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `time_spent` | `string` | Yes | Time spent in Jira duration format (e.g., ``"2h 30m"``, ``"1d"``, ``"45m"``). | | `comment` | `string` | No | Optional plain-text comment describing the work done. | **Returns:** `JiraWorklog` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_add_worklog", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "time_spent": "your-time_spent", "comment": "your-comment"}) ``` --- ### `assign_issue` Assign an issue to a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `assignee_account_id` | `string` | Yes | The Atlassian account ID of the user to assign. Pass ``"-1"`` to set unassigned. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_assign_issue", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "assignee_account_id": "your-assignee_account_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_issue` :warning: Create a new issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_key` | `string` | Yes | Key of the project (e.g., ``"PROJ"``). | | `summary` | `string` | Yes | One-line summary of the issue. | | `issue_type` | `string` | No | Issue type name (e.g., ``"Task"``, ``"Bug"``, ``"Story"``). (default: `Task`) | | `description` | `string` | No | Detailed description in Atlassian Document Format or plain text. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Priority name (e.g., ``"High"``, ``"Medium"``). | | `assignee` | `string` | No | Account ID of the assignee. | **Returns:** `JiraIssue` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_create_issue", {"project_key": "your-project_key", "summary": "your-summary", "issue_type": "Task", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_project` :warning: Create a new project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the project. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Unique project key (e.g., ``"PROJ"``). Must be uppercase alphanumeric, 2-10 characters. | | `project_type_key` | `string` | Yes | The type of project (e.g., ``"software"``, ``"service_desk"``, ``"business"``). | | `lead_account_id` | `string` | Yes | Atlassian account ID of the project lead. | **Returns:** `JiraProject` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_create_project", {"name": "my-name", "key": "my-key", "project_type_key": "your-project_type_key", "lead_account_id": "your-lead_account_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_comment` :warning: Delete a comment from an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `comment_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the comment to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_delete_comment", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "comment_id": "your-comment_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_issue` :warning: Delete an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_delete_issue", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_board` Get an Agile board by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `board_id` | `integer` | Yes | The ID of the board to retrieve. | **Returns:** `JiraBoard` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_get_board", {"board_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_issue` Get a single issue by key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key or ID. | **Returns:** `JiraIssue` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_get_issue", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_transitions` Get available transitions for an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_get_transitions", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a Jira user by account ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | The Atlassian account ID of the user. | **Returns:** `JiraUser` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_get_user", {"account_id": "your-account_id"}) ``` --- ### `link_issues` :warning: Create a link between two issues | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `inward_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key for the inward issue (e.g., ``"PROJ-100"``). | | `outward_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key for the outward issue (e.g., ``"PROJ-200"``). | | `link_type` | `string` | Yes | The name of the link type (e.g., ``"Blocks"``, ``"Duplicate"``, ``"Relates"``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_link_issues", {"inward_key": "your-inward_key", "outward_key": "your-outward_key", "link_type": "your-link_type"}) ``` --- ### `list_boards` List Agile boards | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_key` | `string` | No | Filter boards by project key. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum boards per page (max 50). (default: `50`) | | `start_at` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination (0-based). (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[JiraBoard]` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_boards", {"project_key": "your-project_key", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_comments` List comments on an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_comments", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `list_issue_types` List issue types available in the instance | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | No | Optional project ID to scope the issue types to a specific project. When omitted, returns all global issue types. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_issue_types", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_priorities` List available priorities **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_priorities", {}) ``` --- ### `list_projects` List projects accessible to the user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum projects per page (max 50). (default: `50`) | | `start_at` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination (0-based). (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[JiraProject]` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_projects", {"limit": 50, "start_at": 0}) ``` --- ### `list_resolutions` List available resolutions **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_resolutions", {}) ``` --- ### `list_sprints` List sprints for a board | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `board_id` | `integer` | Yes | The ID of the Agile board. | | `state` | `string` | No | Filter by sprint state: ``active``, ``closed``, or ``future``. Omit to list all. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum sprints per page (max 50). (default: `50`) | | `start_at` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination (0-based). (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[JiraSprint]` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_sprints", {"board_id": 10, "state": "open", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_watchers` List watchers on an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_watchers", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `list_worklogs` List worklog entries on an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_worklogs", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `remove_watcher` Remove a watcher from an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | The Atlassian account ID of the user to remove. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_remove_watcher", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "account_id": "your-account_id"}) ``` --- ### `search_issues` Search issues using JQL | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `jql` | `string` | Yes | JQL query string (e.g., ``"project = PROJ AND status = Open"``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `50`) | | `start_at` | `integer` | No | Index of the first result to return (0-based offset). (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[JiraIssue]` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_search_issues", {"jql": "your-jql", "limit": 50, "start_at": 0}) ``` --- ### `search_users` Search for Jira users | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | The search string to match against user display names and email addresses. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of results to return (max 1000). (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_search_users", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `transition_issue` :warning: Transition an issue to a new status | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `transition_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the transition to execute. Use ``get_transitions`` to discover available transitions. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_transition_issue", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "transition_id": "your-transition_id"}) ``` --- ### `update_issue` Update an existing issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field names to new values following the Jira field schema. | **Returns:** `JiraIssue` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_update_issue", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_issues` for filtered queries and `list_boards` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 100 requests/minute — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_attachment`, `add_comment`, `add_worklog`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 28 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Asana](../asana/) — Work management - [Linear](../linear/) — Modern issue tracker - [Trello](../trello/) — Kanban boards --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

linear.readme_md: # Linear > Streamlined issue tracking for software teams ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Project_Management-8B5CF6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-GRAPHQL-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-19-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/linear/linear?style=social)](https://github.com/linear/linear) [![TypeScript (source)](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript_(source)-SDK-3178C6?logo=github)](https://github.com/linear/linear) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Linear Inc. | | **Category** | Project Management | | **Protocol** | GraphQL | | **Website** | [linear.app](https://linear.app) | | **API Docs** | [developers.linear.app](https://developers.linear.app/docs/graphql/working-with-the-graphql-api) | | **Auth** | API Key, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 400 requests/minute | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Plus from $8/user/month | --- ## Overview The Linear GraphQL API provides access to issues, projects, cycles, teams, and labels. Track bugs and features, manage sprints, automate workflows, and build integrations with Linear's modern project management platform. ## Use Cases - Issue tracking - Sprint management - Bug triage automation - Development workflow - Cross-tool project sync ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[linear]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_LINEAR_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["linear"], credentials={"linear": "your-token"}) # List issues with optional team and state filters result = kit.execute("linear_list_issues", {"team_id": "T01234567", "state": "open"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["linear"], credentials={"linear": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["linear"], credentials={"linear": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Create an account at [Linear](https://linear.app) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://linear.app/settings/api) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("linear_list_issues", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_comment` :warning: Add a comment to an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the issue to comment on. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Comment body in Markdown format. | **Returns:** `LinearComment` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_add_comment", {"issue_id": "your-issue_id", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_issue` :warning: Create a new issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the team to create the issue in. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Issue title. | | `description` | `string` | No | Markdown description of the issue. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Priority level (0=none, 1=urgent, 2=high, 3=medium, 4=low). | | `assignee_id` | `string` | No | UUID of the user to assign. | **Returns:** `LinearIssue` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_create_issue", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "title": "My Title", "description": "your-description", "priority": "your-priority"}) ``` --- ### `create_label` :warning: Create a new issue label | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the team to create the label in. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Label name. | | `color` | `string` | No | Hex colour string (e.g. ``#ff0000``). | **Returns:** `LinearLabel` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_create_label", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "name": "my-name", "color": "your-color"}) ``` --- ### `delete_issue` :warning: Delete an issue by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the issue to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_delete_issue", {"issue_id": "your-issue_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_project` :warning: Delete a project by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the project to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_delete_project", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_cycle` Get a single cycle by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cycle_id` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the cycle. | **Returns:** `LinearCycle` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_get_cycle", {"cycle_id": "your-cycle_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_issue` Get a single issue by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_id` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the issue. | **Returns:** `LinearIssue` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_get_issue", {"issue_id": "your-issue_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a single user by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the user. | **Returns:** `LinearUser` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_get_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_workflow_states` Get workflow states for a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the team. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_get_workflow_states", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_cycles` List cycles, optionally filtered by team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | No | Optional team UUID to filter cycles by. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 250). (default: `50`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[LinearCycle]` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_list_cycles", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_issue_comments` List comments on an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the issue. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_list_issue_comments", {"issue_id": "your-issue_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_issues` List issues with optional team and state filters | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | No | Filter to issues belonging to this team ID. | | `state` | `string` | No | Filter to issues in this workflow state name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 250). (default: `50`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[LinearIssue]` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_list_issues", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "state": "open"}) ``` --- ### `list_labels` List issue labels, optionally filtered by team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | No | Optional team UUID to filter labels by. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_list_labels", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_projects` List projects with pagination | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 250). (default: `50`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[LinearProject]` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_list_projects", {"limit": 50, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_teams` List all teams in the workspace **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_list_teams", {}) ``` --- ### `list_users` List all users in the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 250). (default: `50`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[LinearUser]` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_list_users", {"limit": 50, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `search_issues` Search issues by text query | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search text to match against issue titles and descriptions. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 250). (default: `50`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[LinearIssue]` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_search_issues", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 50, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `update_issue` Update an existing issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the issue to update. | | `title` | `string` | No | New title (if changing). | | `description` | `string` | No | New description (if changing). | | `state_id` | `string` | No | UUID of the new workflow state (if changing). | | `priority` | `string` | No | New priority level (if changing). | **Returns:** `LinearIssue` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_update_issue", {"issue_id": "your-issue_id", "title": "My Title", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `update_project` :warning: Update an existing project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the project to update. | | `name` | `string` | No | New name (if changing). | | `description` | `string` | No | New description (if changing). | | `state` | `string` | No | New state (e.g. ``"planned"``, ``"started"``, ``"paused"``, ``"completed"``, ``"canceled"``). | **Returns:** `LinearProject` ```python result = kit.execute("linear_update_project", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_issues` for filtered queries and `list_cycles` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 400 requests/minute — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_comment`, `create_issue`, `create_label`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses ## Related Connectors - [Jira](../jira/) — Issue tracking - [Asana](../asana/) — Work management - [Trello](../trello/) — Kanban boards --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

mailchimp.readme_md: # Mailchimp > Email marketing and audience management ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Marketing-EC4899) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-23-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/mailchimp/mailchimp-marketing-python?style=social)](https://github.com/mailchimp/mailchimp-marketing-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/mailchimp/mailchimp-marketing-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/mailchimp/mailchimp-marketing-node) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Intuit (Mailchimp) | | **Category** | Marketing | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [mailchimp.com](https://mailchimp.com) | | **API Docs** | [mailchimp.com](https://mailchimp.com/developer/marketing/api/) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | 10 concurrent connections | | **Pricing** | Free up to 500 contacts, Essentials from $13/month | --- ## Overview The Mailchimp Marketing API provides access to audiences, campaigns, templates, and automations. Manage subscriber lists, create and send email campaigns, set up automation workflows, and track engagement analytics. ## Use Cases - Email campaign management - Subscriber list management - Marketing automation - A/B testing - Engagement analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[mailchimp]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_MAILCHIMP_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["mailchimp"], credentials={"mailchimp": "your-token"}) # Get a single Mailchimp campaign by ID result = kit.execute("mailchimp_get_campaign", {"campaign_id": "campaign-123"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["mailchimp"], credentials={"mailchimp": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["mailchimp"], credentials={"mailchimp": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Create an account at [Mailchimp](https://mailchimp.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://mailchimp.com/help/about-api-keys/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("mailchimp_get_campaign", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_member` :warning: Add a member to a Mailchimp audience list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | Email address of the new member. | | `status` | `string` | No | Subscription status (subscribed, pending, etc.). Defaults to ``subscribed``. | | `merge_fields` | `string` | No | Merge field values (FNAME, LNAME, etc.). | **Returns:** `MailchimpMember` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_add_member", {"list_id": "list-123", "email": "user@example.com", "status": "active", "merge_fields": "your-merge_fields"}) ``` --- ### `create_campaign` :warning: Create a new campaign | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID for recipients. | | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Email subject line. | | `from_name` | `string` | Yes | The ``From`` name displayed to recipients. | | `reply_to` | `string` | Yes | The reply-to email address. | | `type` | `string` | No | Campaign type. One of ``regular``, ``plaintext``, ``absplit``, or ``rss``. Defaults to ``regular``. (default: `regular`) | **Returns:** `MailchimpCampaign` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_create_campaign", {"list_id": "list-123", "subject": "Hello", "from_name": "your-from_name", "reply_to": "your-reply_to", "type": "regular"}) ``` --- ### `create_segment` :warning: Create a segment for an audience list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Segment name. | | `conditions` | `array` | Yes | List of condition dicts defining the segment rules. | **Returns:** `MailchimpSegment` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_create_segment", {"list_id": "list-123", "name": "my-name", "conditions": "your-conditions"}) ``` --- ### `delete_member` :warning: Delete a member from a list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | The member's email address. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_delete_member", {"list_id": "list-123", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `get_campaign` Get a single Mailchimp campaign by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `campaign_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp campaign ID. | **Returns:** `MailchimpCampaign` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_get_campaign", {"campaign_id": "your-campaign_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_campaign_content` Get campaign content | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `campaign_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp campaign ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_get_campaign_content", {"campaign_id": "your-campaign_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_campaign_report` Get a campaign performance report | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `campaign_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp campaign ID. | **Returns:** `MailchimpCampaignReport` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_get_campaign_report", {"campaign_id": "your-campaign_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_list` Get a single Mailchimp audience list by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID. | **Returns:** `MailchimpList` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_get_list", {"list_id": "list-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_list_growth` Get list growth history | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_get_list_growth", {"list_id": "list-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_member` Get a single member from an audience list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | The member's email address. | **Returns:** `MailchimpMember` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_get_member", {"list_id": "list-123", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `get_template` Get a single email template by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `template_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Mailchimp template ID (integer). | **Returns:** `MailchimpTemplate` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_get_template", {"template_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_automations` List automations **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_list_automations", {}) ``` --- ### `list_campaigns` List Mailchimp campaigns | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by campaign status (save, paused, schedule, sending, sent). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of campaigns to return (1-1000). (default: `10`) | | `offset` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination. (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[MailchimpCampaign]` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_list_campaigns", {"status": "active", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_lists` List Mailchimp audience lists | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of lists to return (1-1000). (default: `10`) | | `offset` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination. (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[MailchimpList]` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_list_lists", {"limit": 10, "offset": 0}) ``` --- ### `list_members` List members in a Mailchimp audience list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID. | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (subscribed, unsubscribed, cleaned, etc.). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of members to return (1-1000). (default: `10`) | | `offset` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination. (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[MailchimpMember]` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_list_members", {"list_id": "list-123", "status": "active", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_segments` List segments for an audience list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_list_segments", {"list_id": "list-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_tags` List tags for an audience list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_list_tags", {"list_id": "list-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_templates` List email templates | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of templates to return (1-1000). (default: `10`) | | `offset` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination. (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[MailchimpTemplate]` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_list_templates", {"limit": 10, "offset": 0}) ``` --- ### `schedule_campaign` :warning: Schedule a campaign for sending | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `campaign_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp campaign ID to schedule. | | `schedule_time` | `string` | Yes | UTC datetime string in ISO 8601 format (e.g. ``"2024-12-25T09:00:00+00:00"``). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_schedule_campaign", {"campaign_id": "your-campaign_id", "schedule_time": "your-schedule_time"}) ``` --- ### `send_campaign` :warning: Send a Mailchimp campaign | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `campaign_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp campaign ID to send. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_send_campaign", {"campaign_id": "your-campaign_id"}) ``` --- ### `tag_member` :warning: Add or remove tags on a member | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | The member's email address. | | `tags` | `array` | Yes | List of tag dicts, e.g. ``[{"name": "VIP", "status": "active"}]``. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_tag_member", {"list_id": "list-123", "email": "user@example.com", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ### `update_campaign` Update an existing campaign | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `campaign_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp campaign ID. | | `settings` | `object` | Yes | Dict of campaign settings to update (e.g. ``subject_line``, ``from_name``, ``reply_to``). | **Returns:** `MailchimpCampaign` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_update_campaign", {"campaign_id": "your-campaign_id", "settings": "your-settings"}) ``` --- ### `update_member` :warning: Update a member in a Mailchimp audience list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Mailchimp list/audience ID. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | Email address of the member to update. | | `status` | `string` | No | New subscription status. | | `merge_fields` | `string` | No | Updated merge field values. | **Returns:** `MailchimpMember` ```python result = kit.execute("mailchimp_update_member", {"list_id": "list-123", "email": "user@example.com", "status": "active", "merge_fields": "your-merge_fields"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 10 concurrent connections — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_member`, `create_campaign`, `create_segment`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 23 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Sendgrid](../sendgrid/) — Email delivery --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

mixpanel.readme_md: # Mixpanel > Product analytics and user behavior tracking ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Analytics-8B5CF6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-14-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/mixpanel/mixpanel-python?style=social)](https://github.com/mixpanel/mixpanel-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/mixpanel/mixpanel-python) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Mixpanel Inc. | | **Category** | Analytics | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [mixpanel.com](https://mixpanel.com) | | **API Docs** | [developer.mixpanel.com](https://developer.mixpanel.com/reference/overview) | | **Auth** | Service Account (Basic Auth) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by endpoint | | **Pricing** | Free up to 20M events, Growth from $28/month | --- ## Overview The Mixpanel API lets you track events, query analytics data, manage user profiles, and export data. Build product analytics pipelines, create funnels, analyze user retention, and automate reporting workflows. ## Use Cases - Event tracking - Funnel analysis - User retention reporting - A/B test analysis - Data export and ETL ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[mixpanel]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_MIXPANEL_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["mixpanel"], credentials={"mixpanel": "your-token"}) # Get event count for a date range result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_event_count", {"event": "your-event", "from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["mixpanel"], credentials={"mixpanel": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["mixpanel"], credentials={"mixpanel": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Service Account (Basic Auth) 1. Create an account at [Mixpanel](https://mixpanel.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://mixpanel.com/settings/project#serviceaccounts) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_event_count", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_annotation` :warning: Create an annotation in Mixpanel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `date` | `string` | Yes | Annotation date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `description` | `string` | Yes | Description text for the annotation. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_create_annotation", {"date": "your-date", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `delete_profile` :warning: Delete a user profile | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `distinct_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's distinct ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_delete_profile", {"distinct_id": "your-distinct_id"}) ``` --- ### `export_data` Export raw event data | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `event` | `string` | No | Filter by specific event name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_export_data", {"from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date", "event": "your-event"}) ``` --- ### `get_event_count` Get event count for a date range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event` | `string` | Yes | The event name to count. | | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_event_count", {"event": "your-event", "from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date"}) ``` --- ### `get_funnels` Get funnel analysis | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `funnel_id` | `integer` | Yes | The ID of the funnel to analyze. | | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | **Returns:** `MixpanelFunnel` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_funnels", {"funnel_id": 10, "from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date"}) ``` --- ### `get_property_values` Get property values for an event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event` | `string` | Yes | The event name. | | `property` | `string` | Yes | The property name to get values for. | | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of values to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_property_values", {"event": "your-event", "property": "your-property", "limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `get_retention` Get retention analysis | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `event` | `string` | Yes | The event to measure retention for. | **Returns:** `MixpanelRetention` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_retention", {"from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date", "event": "your-event"}) ``` --- ### `get_segmentation` Get segmentation data for an event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event` | `string` | Yes | The event name to segment. | | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `segment_property` | `string` | No | Optional property to segment by. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_segmentation", {"event": "your-event", "from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date", "segment_property": "your-segment_property"}) ``` --- ### `get_top_events` Get top events by volume | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of events to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_top_events", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `get_user_profile` Get a user profile by distinct ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `distinct_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique identifier for the user. | **Returns:** `MixpanelProfile` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_user_profile", {"distinct_id": "your-distinct_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_cohorts` List cohorts in Mixpanel **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_list_cohorts", {}) ``` --- ### `list_event_names` List all event names **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_list_event_names", {}) ``` --- ### `query_events` Query events within a date range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `event` | `string` | No | Filter by specific event name. | | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of events to return. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[MixpanelEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_query_events", {"from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date", "event": "your-event", "limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `track_event` Track an event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event` | `string` | Yes | Name of the event to track. | | `properties` | `object` | Yes | Event properties as key-value pairs. | | `distinct_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the user performing the event. | **Returns:** `MixpanelTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_track_event", {"event": "your-event", "properties": "your-properties", "distinct_id": "your-distinct_id"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by endpoint — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_annotation`, `delete_profile`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Segment](../segment/) — Customer data --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

mongodb.readme_md: # MongoDB Atlas > Cloud-hosted NoSQL document database ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Database-10B981) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-16-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/mongodb/mongo-python-driver?style=social)](https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | MongoDB Inc. | | **Category** | Database | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [www.mongodb.com/atlas](https://www.mongodb.com/atlas) | | **API Docs** | [www.mongodb.com](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/api/) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | 300 requests/minute (Data API) | | **Pricing** | Free tier (M0), Dedicated from $57/month | --- ## Overview The MongoDB Atlas Data API provides RESTful access to your MongoDB Atlas clusters. Query documents, run aggregation pipelines, insert and update data, and manage collections without direct driver connections. ## Use Cases - Document CRUD operations - Aggregation pipelines - Serverless data access - Edge data queries - Cross-platform data sync ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[mongodb]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_MONGODB_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["mongodb"], credentials={"mongodb": "your-token"}) # List databases available in the MongoDB cluster result = kit.execute("mongodb_list_databases", {}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["mongodb"], credentials={"mongodb": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["mongodb"], credentials={"mongodb": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Create an account at [MongoDB Atlas](https://www.mongodb.com/atlas) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://cloud.mongodb.com/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("mongodb_list_databases", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `aggregate` Run an aggregation pipeline on a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `pipeline` | `array` | Yes | List of aggregation stages. | **Returns:** `MongoAggregateResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_aggregate", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "pipeline": "your-pipeline"}) ``` --- ### `count` Count documents in a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `string` | No | Optional MongoDB query filter. | **Returns:** `MongoCountResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_count", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `create_index` Create an index on a collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `keys` | `object` | Yes | Index key specification (e.g. ``{"field": 1}`` for ascending). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_create_index", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "keys": "your-keys"}) ``` --- ### `delete_many` :warning: Delete multiple documents matching a filter | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | MongoDB query filter to match documents. | **Returns:** `MongoDeleteResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_delete_many", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `delete_one` :warning: Delete a single document from a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | MongoDB query filter to locate the document. | **Returns:** `MongoDeleteResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_delete_one", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `distinct` Get distinct values for a field in a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `field` | `string` | Yes | Field path to collect distinct values from. | | `filter` | `string` | No | Optional MongoDB query filter. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_distinct", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "field": "your-field", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `drop_collection` :warning: Drop a collection from a database | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name to drop. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_drop_collection", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database"}) ``` --- ### `find` Find documents in a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `string` | No | MongoDB query filter document. | | `sort` | `string` | No | Sort specification (e.g. ``{"created": -1}``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of documents to return. (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[MongoDocument]` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_find", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter", "sort": "your-sort"}) ``` --- ### `find_one` Find a single document in a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | MongoDB query filter document. | **Returns:** `MongoDocument` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_find_one", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `insert_many` Insert multiple documents into a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `documents` | `array` | Yes | List of documents to insert. | **Returns:** `MongoInsertResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_insert_many", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "documents": "your-documents"}) ``` --- ### `insert_one` Insert a single document into a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `document` | `object` | Yes | The document to insert. | **Returns:** `MongoInsertResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_insert_one", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "document": "your-document"}) ``` --- ### `list_collections` List collections in a MongoDB database | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_list_collections", {"database": "your-database"}) ``` --- ### `list_databases` List databases available in the MongoDB cluster **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_list_databases", {}) ``` --- ### `replace_one` Replace a single document by filter | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | Query filter to match the document. | | `replacement` | `object` | Yes | The replacement document. | **Returns:** `MongoUpdateResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_replace_one", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter", "replacement": "your-replacement"}) ``` --- ### `update_many` Update multiple documents matching a filter | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | MongoDB query filter to match documents. | | `update` | `object` | Yes | Update operations (e.g. ``{"$set": {"active": false}}``). | **Returns:** `MongoUpdateResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_update_many", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter", "update": "your-update"}) ``` --- ### `update_one` Update a single document in a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | MongoDB query filter to locate the document. | | `update` | `object` | Yes | Update operations (e.g. ``{"$set": {"name": "x"}}``). | **Returns:** `MongoUpdateResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_update_one", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter", "update": "your-update"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 300 requests/minute (Data API) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`delete_many`, `delete_one`, `drop_collection`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Airtable](../airtable/) — Spreadsheet database - [Firestore](../firestore/) — Document database - [Redis](../redis/) — Key-value store - [Supabase](../supabase/) — Postgres as a service --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

notion.readme_md: # Notion > All-in-one workspace for notes, docs, and databases ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Knowledge-0EA5E9) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-20-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/ramnes/notion-sdk-py?style=social)](https://github.com/ramnes/notion-sdk-py) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/ramnes/notion-sdk-py) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/makenotion/notion-sdk-js) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Notion Labs Inc. | | **Category** | Knowledge | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [notion.so](https://notion.so) | | **API Docs** | [developers.notion.com](https://developers.notion.com/reference) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token (Integration), OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 3 requests/second average | | **Pricing** | Free for personal, Plus from $10/user/month | --- ## Overview The Notion API provides access to pages, databases, blocks, and users. Query and update databases, create and modify pages, manage content blocks, and build integrations that sync data with your Notion workspace. ## Use Cases - Knowledge base management - Content publishing - Project tracking - CRM in Notion databases - Documentation automation ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[notion]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_NOTION_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["notion"], credentials={"notion": "your-token"}) # List all users in the workspace result = kit.execute("notion_list_users", {}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["notion"], credentials={"notion": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["notion"], credentials={"notion": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token (Integration) 1. Settings 2. My Integrations 3. New Integration [Get credentials →](https://www.notion.so/my-integrations) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("notion_list_users", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_comment` :warning: Add a comment to a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the page to comment on. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | Plain-text content of the comment. | **Returns:** `NotionComment` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_add_comment", {"page_id": "page-123", "text": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `append_block_children` :warning: Append child blocks to a page or block | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the parent block or page. | | `children` | `array` | Yes | List of block objects to append, following the Notion block schema (e.g., paragraph, heading, to_do, bulleted_list_item). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_append_block_children", {"block_id": "your-block_id", "children": "your-children"}) ``` --- ### `archive_page` :warning: Archive (soft-delete) a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the page to archive. | **Returns:** `NotionPage` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_archive_page", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `create_database` :warning: Create a new database | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `parent_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the parent page. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Title for the new database. | | `properties` | `object` | Yes | Database property schema. Each key is a property name and each value is a property configuration object (e.g., ``{"Name": {"title": {}}, "Tags": {"multi_select": {"options": []}}}``). | **Returns:** `NotionDatabase` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_create_database", {"parent_id": "your-parent_id", "title": "My Title", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ### `create_page` :warning: Create a new page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `parent_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the parent page or database. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Page title text. | | `properties` | `string` | No | Additional property values (database pages). | | `children` | `string` | No | Block children to populate the page body. | **Returns:** `NotionPage` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_create_page", {"parent_id": "your-parent_id", "title": "My Title", "properties": "your-properties", "children": "your-children"}) ``` --- ### `delete_block` :warning: Delete a block | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the block to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_delete_block", {"block_id": "your-block_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_block` Get a single block by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the block to retrieve. | **Returns:** `NotionBlock` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_block", {"block_id": "your-block_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_block_children` Get child blocks of a page or block | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the parent block or page. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum blocks per page (max 100). (default: `50`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[NotionBlock]` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_block_children", {"block_id": "your-block_id", "limit": 50, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `get_database` Get a database schema and metadata | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `database_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the database. | **Returns:** `NotionDatabase` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_database", {"database_id": "your-database_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_page` Get a single page by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the page to retrieve. | **Returns:** `NotionPage` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_page", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_page_property` Get a page property value by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the page. | | `property_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the property to retrieve (found in the page's ``properties`` dict under each property's ``id`` field). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_page_property", {"page_id": "page-123", "property_id": "your-property_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a single user by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the user to retrieve. | **Returns:** `NotionUser` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_comments` List comments on a block or page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the block or page to list comments for. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `50`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[NotionComment]` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_list_comments", {"block_id": "your-block_id", "limit": 50, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_users` List all users in the workspace **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_list_users", {}) ``` --- ### `query_database` Query a database with optional filters and sorts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `database_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the database to query. | | `filter` | `string` | No | Notion filter object (compound or property filter). | | `sorts` | `string` | No | List of sort objects with ``property`` and ``direction``. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `50`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[NotionPage]` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_query_database", {"database_id": "your-database_id", "filter": "your-filter", "sorts": "your-sorts"}) ``` --- ### `restore_page` Restore an archived page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the page to restore. | **Returns:** `NotionPage` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_restore_page", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `search` Search pages and databases in the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | No | Text to search for in page titles and content. (default: ``) | | `filter_type` | `string` | No | Restrict to ``"page"`` or ``"database"``. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of results per page. (default: `20`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[NotionPage]` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_search", {"query": "", "filter_type": "your-filter_type"}) ``` --- ### `update_block` Update a block's content | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the block to update. | | `content` | `object` | Yes | Block-type-specific content payload. | **Returns:** `NotionBlock` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_update_block", {"block_id": "your-block_id", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `update_database` Update a database's title, description, or properties | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `database_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the database to update. | | `title` | `string` | No | New title for the database, or ``None`` to keep the current title. | | `description` | `string` | No | New description text, or ``None`` to keep the current description. | | `properties` | `string` | No | Property schema updates. Each key is a property name and each value is a property configuration object. Pass ``None`` for a property value to remove it. | **Returns:** `NotionDatabase` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_update_database", {"database_id": "your-database_id", "title": "My Title", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `update_page` Update page properties | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the page to update. | | `properties` | `object` | Yes | Dict of property names to new values, following the Notion property value schema. | **Returns:** `NotionPage` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_update_page", {"page_id": "page-123", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search` for filtered queries and `list_comments` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 3 requests/second average — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_comment`, `append_block_children`, `archive_page`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses ## Related Connectors - [Confluence](../confluence/) — Team wiki --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*
