okta.readme_md: # Okta > Enterprise identity and access management ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Security-059669) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-21-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/okta/okta-sdk-python?style=social)](https://github.com/okta/okta-sdk-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/okta/okta-sdk-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/okta/okta-sdk-nodejs) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Okta Inc. | | **Category** | Security | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [okta.com](https://okta.com) | | **API Docs** | [developer.okta.com](https://developer.okta.com/docs/reference/) | | **Auth** | API Token, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by endpoint (typically 600/min) | | **Pricing** | Contact sales (SSO from $2/user/month) | --- ## Overview The Okta API provides access to users, groups, applications, and system logs. Manage enterprise identity lifecycle, configure SSO, enforce MFA, audit security events, and automate user provisioning. ## Use Cases - User lifecycle management - SSO configuration - Group and role management - Security audit logging - Application provisioning ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[okta]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_OKTA_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["okta"], credentials={"okta": "your-token"}) # List users in the Okta organization result = kit.execute("okta_list_users", {"search": "your-search", "filter": "your-filter"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["okta"], credentials={"okta": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["okta"], credentials={"okta": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Token 1. Create an account at [Okta](https://okta.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://developer.okta.com/docs/guides/create-an-api-token/main/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("okta_list_users", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `activate_user` Activate a user in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_activate_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `add_user_to_group` Add a user to an Okta group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | The group's Okta ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_add_user_to_group", {"group_id": "your-group_id", "user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `assign_user_to_app` Assign a user to an application | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `app_id` | `string` | Yes | The application's Okta ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | | `profile` | `string` | No | Optional app-specific profile attributes for the user (e.g. SSO username mappings). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_assign_user_to_app", {"app_id": "your-app_id", "user_id": "user-123", "profile": "your-profile"}) ``` --- ### `create_group` Create a new group in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | The group name. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional group description. | **Returns:** `OktaGroup` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_create_group", {"name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_user` Create a new user in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `profile` | `object` | Yes | User profile fields (firstName, lastName, email, login). | | `credentials` | `string` | No | Optional credentials (password, recovery_question). | **Returns:** `OktaUser` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_create_user", {"profile": "your-profile", "credentials": "your-credentials"}) ``` --- ### `deactivate_user` :warning: Deactivate a user in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_deactivate_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `delete_group` :warning: Delete a group from Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | The group's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_delete_group", {"group_id": "your-group_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_group` Get a single group by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | The group's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `OktaGroup` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_get_group", {"group_id": "your-group_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a single user by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID or login (email). | **Returns:** `OktaUser` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_get_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_applications` List applications in the Okta organization | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of applications to return (max 200). (default: `200`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OktaApplication]` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_applications", {"limit": 200}) ``` --- ### `list_group_members` List members of a group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | The group's Okta ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of members to return (max 200). (default: `200`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OktaUser]` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_group_members", {"group_id": "your-group_id", "limit": 200}) ``` --- ### `list_groups` List groups in the Okta organization | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `search` | `string` | No | Search query to filter groups by name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of groups to return (max 200). (default: `200`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OktaGroup]` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_groups", {"search": "your-search", "limit": 200}) ``` --- ### `list_system_logs` List system log events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `since` | `string` | No | Lower time bound (ISO 8601, e.g. ``2024-01-01T00:00:00Z``). | | `until` | `string` | No | Upper time bound (ISO 8601). | | `filter` | `string` | No | SCIM filter expression (e.g. ``eventType eq "user.session.start"``). | | `q` | `string` | No | Free-text search query across log event fields. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum events to return (max 1000, default 100). (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OktaLogEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_system_logs", {"since": "your-since", "until": "your-until"}) ``` --- ### `list_user_apps` List applications assigned to a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_user_apps", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_user_groups` List groups a user belongs to | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_user_groups", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_users` List users in the Okta organization | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `search` | `string` | No | Search expression (e.g. ``profile.email eq "user@example.com"``). | | `filter` | `string` | No | Filter expression using Okta filter syntax. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of users to return (max 200). (default: `200`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OktaUser]` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_users", {"search": "your-search", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `remove_user_from_group` Remove a user from a group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | The group's Okta ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_remove_user_from_group", {"group_id": "your-group_id", "user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `reset_password` Reset a user's password | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_reset_password", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `suspend_user` :warning: Suspend a user in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_suspend_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `unsuspend_user` Unsuspend a user in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_unsuspend_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `update_user` Update an existing user's profile in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | | `profile` | `object` | Yes | Profile fields to update. | **Returns:** `OktaUser` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_update_user", {"user_id": "user-123", "profile": "your-profile"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by endpoint (typically 600/min) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`deactivate_user`, `delete_group`, `suspend_user`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 21 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Auth0](../auth0/) — Authentication --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

openai.readme_md: # OpenAI > GPT models, embeddings, and AI capabilities ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Ai_Ml-7C3AED) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-26-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/openai/openai-python?style=social)](https://github.com/openai/openai-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/openai/openai-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/openai/openai-node) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | OpenAI | | **Category** | Ai Ml | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [openai.com](https://openai.com) | | **API Docs** | [platform.openai.com](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by model and tier | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-token (varies by model) | --- ## Overview The OpenAI API provides access to GPT models for text generation, embeddings for semantic search, image generation with DALL-E, audio transcription with Whisper, and fine-tuning capabilities. Build AI-powered features into any application. ## Use Cases - Text generation and chat - Semantic search with embeddings - Image generation - Audio transcription - Content moderation ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[openai]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_OPENAI_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["openai"], credentials={"openai": "your-token"}) # List uploaded files result = kit.execute("openai_list_files", {"purpose": "your-purpose"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["openai"], credentials={"openai": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["openai"], credentials={"openai": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Platform 2. API Keys 3. Create New [Get credentials →](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("openai_list_files", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `chat_completion` Create a chat completion | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model ID to use (e.g., 'gpt-4', 'gpt-4o', 'gpt-3.5-turbo'). | | `messages` | `array` | Yes | List of message dicts with 'role' and 'content' keys. | | `temperature` | `string` | No | Sampling temperature between 0 and 2. | | `max_tokens` | `string` | No | Maximum number of tokens to generate. | | `tools` | `string` | No | List of tool definitions for function calling. | **Returns:** `ChatCompletion` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_chat_completion", {"model": "your-model", "messages": "your-messages", "temperature": "your-temperature", "max_tokens": "your-max_tokens"}) ``` --- ### `create_assistant` Create an assistant | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model ID for the assistant (e.g., 'gpt-4o'). | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the assistant. | | `instructions` | `string` | No | System instructions for the assistant. | | `tools` | `string` | No | List of tool definitions (e.g., [{"type": "code_interpreter"}]). | **Returns:** `Assistant` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_create_assistant", {"model": "your-model", "name": "my-name", "instructions": "your-instructions", "tools": "your-tools"}) ``` --- ### `create_embedding` Create text embeddings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model ID to use (e.g., 'text-embedding-3-small'). | | `input` | `string` | Yes | The text to embed. | **Returns:** `Embedding` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_create_embedding", {"model": "your-model", "input": "your-input"}) ``` --- ### `create_fine_tuning_job` :warning: Create a fine-tuning job | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | The base model to fine-tune (e.g. ``"gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18"``). | | `training_file` | `string` | Yes | The file ID of the uploaded training data. | **Returns:** `FineTuningJob` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_create_fine_tuning_job", {"model": "your-model", "training_file": "your-training_file"}) ``` --- ### `create_image` Generate images from a text prompt | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `prompt` | `string` | Yes | A text description of the desired image(s). | | `size` | `string` | No | Image size ('256x256', '512x512', '1024x1024', '1792x1024', '1024x1792'). | | `n` | `string` | No | Number of images to generate (1-10). | **Returns:** `ImageResult` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_create_image", {"prompt": "your-prompt", "size": "your-size", "n": "your-n"}) ``` --- ### `create_moderation` Run content moderation on text | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `input` | `string` | Yes | The text to moderate. | **Returns:** `ModerationResult` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_create_moderation", {"input": "your-input"}) ``` --- ### `create_speech` Generate speech audio from text | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `input` | `string` | Yes | The text to synthesize into speech (max 4096 chars). | | `voice` | `string` | Yes | Voice to use (``'alloy'``, ``'ash'``, ``'coral'``, ``'echo'``, ``'fable'``, ``'onyx'``, ``'nova'``, ``'sage'``, ``'shimmer'``). | | `model` | `string` | No | TTS model (``'tts-1'``, ``'tts-1-hd'``, ``'gpt-4o-mini-tts'``). Defaults to ``'tts-1'``. | | `response_format` | `string` | No | Audio format (``'mp3'``, ``'opus'``, ``'aac'``, ``'flac'``, ``'wav'``, ``'pcm'``). | | `speed` | `string` | No | Playback speed from 0.25 to 4.0 (default 1.0). | **Returns:** `bytes` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_create_speech", {"input": "your-input", "voice": "your-voice", "model": "your-model", "response_format": "your-response_format"}) ``` --- ### `create_thread` Create a thread **Returns:** `Thread` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_create_thread", {}) ``` --- ### `create_thread_message` Create a message in a thread | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `thread_id` | `string` | Yes | The thread ID to add the message to. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | The text content of the message. | | `role` | `string` | No | The role of the message author (``'user'`` or ``'assistant'``). Defaults to ``'user'``. | **Returns:** `ThreadMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_create_thread_message", {"thread_id": "your-thread_id", "content": "your-content", "role": "your-role"}) ``` --- ### `delete_assistant` :warning: Delete an assistant | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `assistant_id` | `string` | Yes | The assistant ID to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_delete_assistant", {"assistant_id": "your-assistant_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_file` :warning: Delete an uploaded file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_delete_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_model` :warning: Delete a fine-tuned model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model_id` | `string` | Yes | The model ID to delete (must be a fine-tuned model). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_delete_model", {"model_id": "your-model_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_assistant` Get an assistant by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `assistant_id` | `string` | Yes | The assistant ID to retrieve. | **Returns:** `Assistant` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_get_assistant", {"assistant_id": "your-assistant_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_file` Get file metadata by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID to retrieve. | **Returns:** `OpenAIFile` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_get_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_file_content` Get file content by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID whose content to retrieve. | **Returns:** `str` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_get_file_content", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_model` Get a model by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model_id` | `string` | Yes | The model identifier (e.g., ``'gpt-4o'``). | **Returns:** `OpenAIModel` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_get_model", {"model_id": "your-model_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_thread_run` Get a thread run by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `thread_id` | `string` | Yes | The thread ID the run belongs to. | | `run_id` | `string` | Yes | The run ID to retrieve. | **Returns:** `ThreadRun` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_get_thread_run", {"thread_id": "your-thread_id", "run_id": "your-run_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_assistants` List assistants | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of assistants to return (1-100). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Assistant]` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_list_assistants", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `list_files` List uploaded files | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `purpose` | `string` | No | Filter by purpose (e.g., 'assistants', 'fine-tune', 'batch'). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_list_files", {"purpose": "your-purpose"}) ``` --- ### `list_fine_tuning_jobs` List fine-tuning jobs | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of jobs to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_list_fine_tuning_jobs", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `list_models` List available models **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_list_models", {}) ``` --- ### `list_thread_messages` List messages in a thread | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `thread_id` | `string` | Yes | The thread ID to list messages from. | | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of messages to return (1-100). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_list_thread_messages", {"thread_id": "your-thread_id", "limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `run_thread` Run a thread with an assistant | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `thread_id` | `string` | Yes | The thread ID to run. | | `assistant_id` | `string` | Yes | The assistant ID to execute. | **Returns:** `ThreadRun` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_run_thread", {"thread_id": "your-thread_id", "assistant_id": "your-assistant_id"}) ``` --- ### `transcribe_audio` Transcribe audio to text | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_url` | `string` | Yes | URL of the audio file to transcribe. | | `model` | `string` | No | Model to use for transcription (default: 'whisper-1'). | **Returns:** `AudioTranscription` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_transcribe_audio", {"file_url": "your-file_url", "model": "your-model"}) ``` --- ### `update_assistant` Update an assistant | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `assistant_id` | `string` | Yes | The assistant ID to update. | | `name` | `string` | No | New name for the assistant. | | `instructions` | `string` | No | New system instructions. | | `model` | `string` | No | New model ID for the assistant. | **Returns:** `Assistant` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_update_assistant", {"assistant_id": "your-assistant_id", "name": "my-name", "instructions": "your-instructions"}) ``` --- ### `upload_file` Upload a file to OpenAI | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_content` | `string` | Yes | Raw file bytes to upload. | | `purpose` | `string` | Yes | Intended purpose (``'assistants'``, ``'fine-tune'``, ``'batch'``, ``'vision'``). | | `filename` | `string` | No | Name for the uploaded file (default: ``"upload.jsonl"``). | **Returns:** `OpenAIFile` ```python result = kit.execute("openai_upload_file", {"file_content": "your-file_content", "purpose": "your-purpose", "filename": "your-filename"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by model and tier — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_fine_tuning_job`, `delete_assistant`, `delete_file`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 26 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Anthropic](../anthropic/) — Claude models - [Pinecone](../pinecone/) — Vector database --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

outlook.readme_md: # Microsoft Outlook > Email, calendar, and contacts via Microsoft Graph ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Communication-3B82F6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-23-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-python?style=social)](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-javascript) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Microsoft | | **Category** | Communication | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [outlook.com](https://outlook.com) | | **API Docs** | [learn.microsoft.com](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/mail-api-overview) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0 (Microsoft Graph) | | **Rate Limit** | 10,000 requests/10 minutes per app | | **Pricing** | Included with Microsoft 365 | --- ## Overview The Microsoft Graph Mail API provides access to Outlook email, calendar, and contacts. Read, send, and organize emails, manage calendar events, and work with contact data across Microsoft 365 accounts. ## Use Cases - Email automation - Calendar management - Contact sync - Meeting scheduling - Email analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[outlook]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_OUTLOOK_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["outlook"], credentials={"outlook": "your-token"}) # List email messages from a folder result = kit.execute("outlook_list_messages", {"folder": "your-folder", "limit": 25}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["outlook"], credentials={"outlook": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["outlook"], credentials={"outlook": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 (Microsoft Graph) 1. Create an account at [Microsoft Outlook](https://outlook.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_RegisteredApps) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("outlook_list_messages", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_calendar_event` :warning: Create a calendar event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Event title/subject. | | `start` | `string` | Yes | ISO 8601 start datetime (e.g. ``2024-06-15T09:00:00``). | | `end` | `string` | Yes | ISO 8601 end datetime (e.g. ``2024-06-15T10:00:00``). | | `attendees` | `string` | No | Optional list of attendee email addresses. | | `body` | `string` | No | Optional event body/description (HTML supported). | **Returns:** `OutlookCalendarEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_create_calendar_event", {"subject": "Hello", "start": "your-start", "end": "your-end", "attendees": "your-attendees", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_contact` :warning: Create a new contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `given_name` | `string` | Yes | First name of the contact. | | `surname` | `string` | No | Last name of the contact. | | `email` | `string` | No | Primary email address. | | `phone` | `string` | No | Primary phone number. | **Returns:** `OutlookContact` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_create_contact", {"given_name": "your-given_name", "surname": "your-surname", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `create_draft` Create an email draft | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `array` | Yes | List of recipient email addresses. | | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Email subject line. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Email body content (HTML supported). | **Returns:** `OutlookMessageId` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_create_draft", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "subject": "Hello", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_folder` :warning: Create a mail folder | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `display_name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the new folder. | | `parent_folder_id` | `string` | No | Optional parent folder ID. If omitted the folder is created at the top level of the mailbox. | **Returns:** `MailFolder` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_create_folder", {"display_name": "your-display_name", "parent_folder_id": "your-parent_folder_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_mail_rule` :warning: Create a new inbox message rule | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `display_name` | `string` | Yes | Human-readable name for the rule. | | `conditions` | `object` | Yes | Rule conditions that trigger the rule. | | `actions` | `object` | Yes | Actions to take when conditions are met. | **Returns:** `MailRule` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_create_mail_rule", {"display_name": "your-display_name", "conditions": "your-conditions", "actions": "your-actions"}) ``` --- ### `delete_message` :warning: Delete an email message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the message to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_delete_message", {"message_id": "your-message_id"}) ``` --- ### `forward_message` :warning: Forward an email message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the message to forward. | | `to` | `array` | Yes | List of recipient email addresses to forward to. | | `comment` | `string` | No | Optional comment to include with the forwarded message. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_forward_message", {"message_id": "your-message_id", "to": "recipient@example.com", "comment": "your-comment"}) ``` --- ### `get_attachment` Get a single attachment by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the email message. | | `attachment_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the attachment. | **Returns:** `OutlookAttachment` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_get_attachment", {"message_id": "your-message_id", "attachment_id": "your-attachment_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_contact` Get a single contact by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the contact. | **Returns:** `OutlookContact` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_get_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_mail_tips` Get mail tips for email addresses | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email_addresses` | `array` | Yes | List of email addresses to retrieve tips for. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_get_mail_tips", {"email_addresses": "your-email_addresses"}) ``` --- ### `get_message` Get a single email message by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the email message. | **Returns:** `OutlookMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_get_message", {"message_id": "your-message_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_attachments` List attachments on an email message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the email message. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_list_attachments", {"message_id": "your-message_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_calendar_events` List calendar events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `start` | `string` | No | ISO 8601 start datetime for the range filter (e.g. ``2024-01-01T00:00:00Z``). | | `end` | `string` | No | ISO 8601 end datetime for the range filter. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum events per page (max 1000). (default: `25`) | | `page_url` | `string` | No | Full ``@odata.nextLink`` URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OutlookCalendarEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_list_calendar_events", {"start": "your-start", "end": "your-end"}) ``` --- ### `list_categories` List master categories **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_list_categories", {}) ``` --- ### `list_contacts` List contacts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of contacts per page (max 1000). (default: `25`) | | `skip` | `integer` | No | Number of contacts to skip (offset pagination). (default: `0`) | | `page_url` | `string` | No | Full ``@odata.nextLink`` URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OutlookContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_list_contacts", {"limit": 25, "skip": 0}) ``` --- ### `list_folders` List mail folders **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_list_folders", {}) ``` --- ### `list_mail_rules` List inbox message rules **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_list_mail_rules", {}) ``` --- ### `list_messages` List email messages from a folder | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `folder` | `string` | No | Mail folder ID to list from (defaults to Inbox). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of messages per page (max 1000). (default: `25`) | | `skip` | `integer` | No | Number of messages to skip (offset pagination). (default: `0`) | | `page_url` | `string` | No | Full ``@odata.nextLink`` URL for fetching the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OutlookMessage]` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_list_messages", {"folder": "your-folder", "limit": 25}) ``` --- ### `move_message` Move a message to another folder | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the message to move. | | `destination_folder_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the destination mail folder. | **Returns:** `OutlookMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_move_message", {"message_id": "your-message_id", "destination_folder_id": "your-destination_folder_id"}) ``` --- ### `reply_message` :warning: Reply to an email message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the message to reply to. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Reply body content (HTML supported). | | `reply_all` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, reply to all original recipients. Defaults to ``False`` (reply to sender only). (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_reply_message", {"message_id": "your-message_id", "body": "Hello!", "reply_all": False}) ``` --- ### `search_messages` Search email messages | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query string (e.g. ``"subject:meeting"``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page. (default: `25`) | | `page_url` | `string` | No | Full ``@odata.nextLink`` URL for fetching the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OutlookMessage]` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_search_messages", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 25, "page_url": "your-page_url"}) ``` --- ### `send_message` :warning: Send an email message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `array` | Yes | List of recipient email addresses. | | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Email subject line. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Email body content (HTML supported). | | `cc` | `string` | No | Optional list of CC recipient email addresses. | **Returns:** `OutlookMessageId` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_send_message", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "subject": "Hello", "body": "Hello!", "cc": "your-cc"}) ``` --- ### `update_message` Update properties of an email message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the message to update. | | `is_read` | `string` | No | Mark the message as read (``True``) or unread (``False``). | | `categories` | `string` | No | List of category names to assign to the message. | | `importance` | `string` | No | Message importance (``"low"``, ``"normal"``, or ``"high"``). | **Returns:** `OutlookMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("outlook_update_message", {"message_id": "your-message_id", "is_read": "your-is_read", "categories": "your-categories"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_messages` for filtered queries and `list_attachments` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 10,000 requests/10 minutes per app — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_calendar_event`, `create_contact`, `create_folder`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 23 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Gmail](../gmail/) — Email automation - [Slack](../slack/) — Team messaging - [Discord](../discord/) — Community messaging - [Teams](../teams/) — Microsoft collaboration --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

pagerduty.readme_md: # PagerDuty > Incident management and on-call scheduling ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Devops-06B6D4) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-16-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/PagerDuty/pdpyras?style=social)](https://github.com/PagerDuty/pdpyras) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/PagerDuty/pdpyras) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | PagerDuty Inc. | | **Category** | Devops | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [pagerduty.com](https://pagerduty.com) | | **API Docs** | [developer.pagerduty.com](https://developer.pagerduty.com/api-reference/) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | 960 requests/minute | | **Pricing** | Free up to 5 users, Professional from $21/user/month | --- ## Overview The PagerDuty API provides access to incidents, services, users, schedules, and escalation policies. Automate incident response, manage on-call rotations, trigger and resolve alerts, and build custom monitoring integrations. ## Use Cases - Incident management - On-call scheduling - Alert automation - Escalation policy management - Service health dashboards ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[pagerduty]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_PAGERDUTY_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["pagerduty"], credentials={"pagerduty": "your-token"}) # List PagerDuty users result = kit.execute("pagerduty_list_users", {"limit": "your-limit"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["pagerduty"], credentials={"pagerduty": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["pagerduty"], credentials={"pagerduty": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Create an account at [PagerDuty](https://pagerduty.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://support.pagerduty.com/main/docs/api-access-keys) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("pagerduty_list_users", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `acknowledge_incident` :warning: Acknowledge a PagerDuty incident | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `incident_id` | `string` | Yes | The PagerDuty incident ID to acknowledge. | **Returns:** `PDIncident` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_acknowledge_incident", {"incident_id": "your-incident_id"}) ``` --- ### `add_note` Add a note to an incident | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `incident_id` | `string` | Yes | The incident ID. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | The note content text. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_add_note", {"incident_id": "your-incident_id", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `create_incident` :warning: Create a PagerDuty incident | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `service_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the service to create the incident on. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Incident title. | | `body` | `string` | No | Incident body text with details. | | `urgency` | `string` | No | Urgency level (``high`` or ``low``). | **Returns:** `PDIncident` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_create_incident", {"service_id": "your-service_id", "title": "My Title", "body": "Hello!", "urgency": "your-urgency"}) ``` --- ### `create_service` :warning: Create a new PagerDuty service | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Service name. | | `escalation_policy_id` | `string` | Yes | ID of the escalation policy. | **Returns:** `PDService` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_create_service", {"name": "my-name", "escalation_policy_id": "your-escalation_policy_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_escalation_policy` Get a single escalation policy by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `policy_id` | `string` | Yes | The PagerDuty escalation policy ID. | **Returns:** `PDEscalationPolicy` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_get_escalation_policy", {"policy_id": "your-policy_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_incident` Get a single PagerDuty incident by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `incident_id` | `string` | Yes | The PagerDuty incident ID. | **Returns:** `PDIncident` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_get_incident", {"incident_id": "your-incident_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_service` Get a single PagerDuty service by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `service_id` | `string` | Yes | The PagerDuty service ID. | **Returns:** `PDService` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_get_service", {"service_id": "your-service_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_escalation_policies` List escalation policies | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum policies per page (max 100). (default: `25`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PDEscalationPolicy]` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_list_escalation_policies", {"limit": 25}) ``` --- ### `list_incidents` List PagerDuty incidents | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (``triggered``, ``acknowledged``, ``resolved``). Comma-separate for multiple. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum incidents per page (max 100). (default: `25`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor (offset string) for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PDIncident]` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_list_incidents", {"status": "active", "limit": 25}) ``` --- ### `list_oncalls` List PagerDuty on-call entries | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `schedule_id` | `string` | No | Filter to a specific schedule ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PDOncall]` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_list_oncalls", {"schedule_id": "your-schedule_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_schedules` List schedules | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum schedules per page (max 100). (default: `25`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PDSchedule]` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_list_schedules", {"limit": 25}) ``` --- ### `list_services` List PagerDuty services | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum services per page (max 100). (default: `25`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor (offset string) for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PDService]` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_list_services", {"limit": 25, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `list_users` List PagerDuty users | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of users to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_list_users", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `merge_incidents` :warning: Merge incidents into a target incident | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of incident IDs to merge from. | | `target_id` | `string` | Yes | The incident ID to merge into. | **Returns:** `PDIncident` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_merge_incidents", {"source_ids": "your-source_ids", "target_id": "your-target_id"}) ``` --- ### `resolve_incident` Resolve an incident | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `incident_id` | `string` | Yes | The incident ID. | **Returns:** `PDIncident` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_resolve_incident", {"incident_id": "your-incident_id"}) ``` --- ### `update_incident` :warning: Update a PagerDuty incident status | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `incident_id` | `string` | Yes | The PagerDuty incident ID. | | `status` | `string` | Yes | New status (``acknowledged`` or ``resolved``). | **Returns:** `PDIncident` ```python result = kit.execute("pagerduty_update_incident", {"incident_id": "your-incident_id", "status": "active"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 960 requests/minute — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`acknowledge_incident`, `create_incident`, `create_service`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Cloudflare](../cloudflare/) — CDN and security - [Datadog](../datadog/) — Monitoring - [Vercel](../vercel/) — Frontend deployment - [Dockerhub](../dockerhub/) — Container registry --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

pinecone.readme_md: # Pinecone > Vector database for AI and machine learning ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Ai_Ml-7C3AED) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-15-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/pinecone-io/pinecone-python-client?style=social)](https://github.com/pinecone-io/pinecone-python-client) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/pinecone-io/pinecone-python-client) [![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-SDK-3178C6?logo=github)](https://github.com/pinecone-io/pinecone-ts-client) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Pinecone Systems Inc. | | **Category** | Ai Ml | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [pinecone.io](https://pinecone.io) | | **API Docs** | [docs.pinecone.io](https://docs.pinecone.io/reference/api/introduction) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan and pod type | | **Pricing** | Free tier (Starter), Standard from $70/month | --- ## Overview The Pinecone API provides vector database operations for AI applications. Upsert, query, and manage high-dimensional vector embeddings. Build semantic search, recommendation engines, RAG pipelines, and anomaly detection systems. ## Use Cases - Semantic search - RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) - Recommendation engines - Anomaly detection - Image similarity ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[pinecone]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_PINECONE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["pinecone"], credentials={"pinecone": "your-token"}) # List vector IDs in the index result = kit.execute("pinecone_list_vectors", {"prefix": "your-prefix", "namespace": "default"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["pinecone"], credentials={"pinecone": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["pinecone"], credentials={"pinecone": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Create an account at [Pinecone](https://pinecone.io) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://app.pinecone.io/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("pinecone_list_vectors", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `configure_index` Configure an existing Pinecone index | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the index to configure. | | `replicas` | `string` | No | Number of replicas. | | `pod_type` | `string` | No | Pod type (e.g. ``"p1.x1"``). | **Returns:** `PineconeIndex` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_configure_index", {"name": "my-name", "replicas": "your-replicas", "pod_type": "your-pod_type"}) ``` --- ### `create_collection` :warning: Create a collection from an index | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new collection. | | `source_index` | `string` | Yes | Name of the source index to snapshot. | **Returns:** `PineconeCollection` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_create_collection", {"name": "my-name", "source_index": "your-source_index"}) ``` --- ### `create_index` :warning: Create a new Pinecone index | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the index. | | `dimension` | `integer` | Yes | Vector dimension. | | `metric` | `string` | No | Distance metric (``cosine``, ``euclidean``, ``dotproduct``). | **Returns:** `PineconeIndex` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_create_index", {"name": "my-name", "dimension": 10, "metric": "your-metric"}) ``` --- ### `delete` :warning: Delete vectors from the index | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ids` | `string` | No | List of vector IDs to delete. | | `namespace` | `string` | No | Namespace to delete from. | | `delete_all` | `string` | No | If True, deletes all vectors in the namespace. | **Returns:** `DeleteResult` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_delete", {"ids": "your-ids", "namespace": "your-namespace"}) ``` --- ### `delete_collection` :warning: Delete a collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the collection to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_delete_collection", {"name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `delete_index` :warning: Delete a Pinecone index | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the index to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_delete_index", {"name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `describe_index` Describe a Pinecone index | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `index_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the index to describe. | **Returns:** `PineconeIndex` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_describe_index", {"index_name": "your-index_name"}) ``` --- ### `describe_index_stats` Get index statistics **Returns:** `PineconeStats` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_describe_index_stats", {}) ``` --- ### `fetch` Fetch vectors by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ids` | `array` | Yes | List of vector IDs to fetch. | | `namespace` | `string` | No | Namespace to fetch from. | **Returns:** `PineconeFetchResult` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_fetch", {"ids": "your-ids", "namespace": "your-namespace"}) ``` --- ### `list_collections` List all collections **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_list_collections", {}) ``` --- ### `list_indexes` List all indexes in the account **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_list_indexes", {}) ``` --- ### `list_vectors` List vector IDs in the index | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `prefix` | `string` | No | ID prefix to filter vectors by. | | `namespace` | `string` | No | Namespace to list vectors from. | | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of IDs to return. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PineconeVectorListItem]` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_list_vectors", {"prefix": "your-prefix", "namespace": "your-namespace"}) ``` --- ### `query` Query vectors by similarity | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vector` | `array` | Yes | The query vector. | | `top_k` | `string` | No | Number of top results to return (default: 10). | | `namespace` | `string` | No | Namespace to query within. | | `filter` | `string` | No | Metadata filter expression. | | `include_metadata` | `string` | No | Whether to include metadata in results. | **Returns:** `PineconeQueryResult` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_query", {"vector": "your-vector", "top_k": "your-top_k", "namespace": "your-namespace"}) ``` --- ### `update` Update a vector's values or metadata | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the vector to update. | | `values` | `string` | No | New vector values to set. | | `metadata` | `string` | No | New metadata to set or merge. | | `namespace` | `string` | No | Namespace of the vector. | **Returns:** `DeleteResult` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_update", {"id": "your-id", "values": "your-values", "metadata": "your-metadata"}) ``` --- ### `upsert` Upsert vectors into the index | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vectors` | `array` | Yes | List of vector dicts, each with 'id', 'values', and optional 'metadata'. | | `namespace` | `string` | No | Target namespace for the vectors. | **Returns:** `PineconeUpsertResult` ```python result = kit.execute("pinecone_upsert", {"vectors": "your-vectors", "namespace": "your-namespace"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by plan and pod type — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_collection`, `create_index`, `delete`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Openai](../openai/) — GPT models - [Anthropic](../anthropic/) — Claude models --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

plaid.readme_md: # Plaid > Financial data connectivity and banking APIs ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Finance-6366F1) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-17-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/plaid/plaid-python?style=social)](https://github.com/plaid/plaid-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/plaid/plaid-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/plaid/plaid-node) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/plaid/plaid-go) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Plaid Inc. | | **Category** | Finance | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [plaid.com](https://plaid.com) | | **API Docs** | [plaid.com](https://plaid.com/docs/api/) | | **Auth** | API Key (Client ID + Secret) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by product | | **Pricing** | Pay per connection (custom pricing) | --- ## Overview The Plaid API provides access to financial account data, transactions, balances, and identity information. Connect to bank accounts, verify identity, check balances, categorize transactions, and power fintech applications. ## Use Cases - Bank account linking - Transaction history - Balance verification - Identity verification - Financial data aggregation ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[plaid]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_PLAID_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["plaid"], credentials={"plaid": "your-token"}) # Get accounts linked to an access token result = kit.execute("plaid_get_accounts", {"access_token": "your-access_token"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["plaid"], credentials={"plaid": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["plaid"], credentials={"plaid": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key (Client ID + Secret) 1. Create an account at [Plaid](https://plaid.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://dashboard.plaid.com/team/keys) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("plaid_get_accounts", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_link_token` Create a Link token for client-side initialization | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_client_id` | `string` | Yes | A unique identifier for the end user. | | `products` | `array` | Yes | List of Plaid products to enable (e.g. ``["transactions", "auth"]``). | **Returns:** `PlaidLinkToken` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_create_link_token", {"user_client_id": "your-user_client_id", "products": "your-products"}) ``` --- ### `create_processor_token` Create a processor token for a third-party integration | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token. | | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | The account ID to create the processor token for. | | `processor` | `string` | Yes | Processor name (e.g. ``"dwolla"``, ``"stripe"``). | **Returns:** `PlaidProcessorToken` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_create_processor_token", {"access_token": "your-access_token", "account_id": "your-account_id", "processor": "your-processor"}) ``` --- ### `exchange_public_token` Exchange a public token for an access token | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `public_token` | `string` | Yes | The temporary public token from Plaid Link. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_exchange_public_token", {"public_token": "your-public_token"}) ``` --- ### `get_accounts` Get accounts linked to an access token | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token from a completed Link flow. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PlaidAccount]` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_get_accounts", {"access_token": "your-access_token"}) ``` --- ### `get_auth` Get account and routing numbers | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token with the auth product. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_get_auth", {"access_token": "your-access_token"}) ``` --- ### `get_balance` Get real-time account balances | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PlaidBalance]` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_get_balance", {"access_token": "your-access_token"}) ``` --- ### `get_identity` Get identity information for accounts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PlaidAccount]` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_get_identity", {"access_token": "your-access_token"}) ``` --- ### `get_institution` Get institution details by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `institution_id` | `string` | Yes | The Plaid institution ID (e.g. ``"ins_109508"``). | **Returns:** `PlaidInstitution` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_get_institution", {"institution_id": "your-institution_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_investment_holdings` Get investment holdings for an access token | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token with the investments product. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_get_investment_holdings", {"access_token": "your-access_token"}) ``` --- ### `get_investment_transactions` Get investment transactions for a date range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token with the investments product. | | `start_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | | `end_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of transactions (max 500). (default: `100`) | | `offset` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination. (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PlaidInvestmentTransaction]` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_get_investment_transactions", {"access_token": "your-access_token", "start_date": "your-start_date", "end_date": "your-end_date", "limit": 100, "offset": 0}) ``` --- ### `get_item` Get Item details | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_get_item", {"access_token": "your-access_token"}) ``` --- ### `get_liabilities` Get liabilities for an access token | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token with the liabilities product. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_get_liabilities", {"access_token": "your-access_token"}) ``` --- ### `get_transactions` Get transactions for a date range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token. | | `start_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | | `end_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of transactions (max 500). (default: `100`) | | `offset` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination. (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PlaidTransaction]` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_get_transactions", {"access_token": "your-access_token", "start_date": "your-start_date", "end_date": "your-end_date", "limit": 100, "offset": 0}) ``` --- ### `item_webhook_update` Update the webhook URL for an Item | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token. | | `webhook` | `string` | No | The new webhook URL, or ``None`` to remove. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_item_webhook_update", {"access_token": "your-access_token", "webhook": "your-webhook"}) ``` --- ### `refresh_investments` Refresh investment data for an Item | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token with the investments product. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_refresh_investments", {"access_token": "your-access_token"}) ``` --- ### `remove_item` :warning: Remove a linked Item | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_token` | `string` | Yes | A Plaid access token to remove. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_remove_item", {"access_token": "your-access_token"}) ``` --- ### `search_institutions` Search for institutions by name | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Institution name search query. | | `country_codes` | `string` | No | Country codes to search within (default ``["US"]``). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PlaidInstitution]` ```python result = kit.execute("plaid_search_institutions", {"query": "is:unread", "country_codes": "your-country_codes"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by product — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`remove_item`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Stripe](../stripe/) — Payments --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.
PageState.title: PageState

rabbitmq.readme_md: # RabbitMQ > Open-source message broker and queue management ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Message_Queue-EF4444) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-21-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/rabbitmq/rabbitmq-server?style=social)](https://github.com/rabbitmq/rabbitmq-server) [![Erlang (source)](https://img.shields.io/badge/Erlang_(source)-SDK-A90533?logo=github)](https://github.com/rabbitmq/rabbitmq-server) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/pika/pika) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | VMware (Broadcom) | | **Category** | Message Queue | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [rabbitmq.com](https://rabbitmq.com) | | **API Docs** | [rawcdn.githack.com](https://rawcdn.githack.com/rabbitmq/rabbitmq-server/v4.1.1/deps/rabbitmq_management/priv/www/api/index.html) | | **Auth** | Basic Auth | | **Rate Limit** | No hard limit (management API) | | **Pricing** | Free (open-source), CloudAMQP from $0/month | --- ## Overview The RabbitMQ Management HTTP API provides access to exchanges, queues, bindings, connections, and channels. Monitor broker health, manage queue configurations, publish and consume messages, and build reliable messaging workflows. ## Use Cases - Message queue management - Broker monitoring - Queue configuration - Exchange and binding setup - Dead letter handling ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[rabbitmq]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_RABBITMQ_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["rabbitmq"], credentials={"rabbitmq": "your-token"}) # List active channels in the broker result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_list_channels", {}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["rabbitmq"], credentials={"rabbitmq": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["rabbitmq"], credentials={"rabbitmq": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Basic Auth 1. Create an account at [RabbitMQ](https://rabbitmq.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://rabbitmq.com/docs/management) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_list_channels", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_binding` :warning: Create a binding between an exchange and a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | Virtual host containing the exchange and queue. | | `exchange` | `string` | Yes | Source exchange name. | | `queue` | `string` | Yes | Destination queue name. | | `routing_key` | `string` | No | Routing key for the binding. Defaults to ``""``. | | `arguments` | `string` | No | Optional arguments for header exchange bindings. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_create_binding", {"vhost": "your-vhost", "exchange": "your-exchange", "queue": "your-queue", "routing_key": "your-routing_key", "arguments": "your-arguments"}) ``` --- ### `create_exchange` :warning: Create a new exchange | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | The virtual host name. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | The exchange name. | | `type` | `string` | Yes | Exchange type (``direct``, ``fanout``, ``topic``, ``headers``). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_create_exchange", {"vhost": "your-vhost", "name": "my-name", "type": "your-type"}) ``` --- ### `create_queue` :warning: Create a new queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | The virtual host name (use ``%2F`` for the default ``/``). | | `queue_name` | `string` | Yes | The name for the new queue. | | `durable` | `string` | No | Whether the queue survives broker restart. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_create_queue", {"vhost": "your-vhost", "queue_name": "your-queue_name", "durable": "your-durable"}) ``` --- ### `delete_exchange` :warning: Delete an exchange | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | The virtual host name. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | The exchange name to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_delete_exchange", {"vhost": "your-vhost", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `delete_queue` :warning: Delete a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | The virtual host name. | | `queue_name` | `string` | Yes | The queue name to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_delete_queue", {"vhost": "your-vhost", "queue_name": "your-queue_name"}) ``` --- ### `get_exchange` Get details for a specific exchange | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | Virtual host containing the exchange. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the exchange. | **Returns:** `RabbitExchange` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_get_exchange", {"vhost": "your-vhost", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `get_messages` Get messages from a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | Virtual host containing the queue. | | `queue_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the queue. | | `count` | `integer` | No | Number of messages to retrieve (default 1). (default: `1`) | | `ack_mode` | `string` | No | Acknowledgement mode. One of ``ack_requeue_true``, ``ack_requeue_false``, or ``reject_requeue_true``. (default: `ack_requeue_true`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_get_messages", {"vhost": "your-vhost", "queue_name": "your-queue_name", "count": 1, "ack_mode": "ack_requeue_true"}) ``` --- ### `get_node_info` Get information about a broker node **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_get_node_info", {}) ``` --- ### `get_overview` Get broker overview and cluster statistics **Returns:** `RabbitOverview` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_get_overview", {}) ``` --- ### `get_queue` Get details for a specific queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | Virtual host containing the queue. | | `queue_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the queue. | **Returns:** `RabbitQueue` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_get_queue", {"vhost": "your-vhost", "queue_name": "your-queue_name"}) ``` --- ### `get_queue_bindings` Get bindings for a specific queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | Virtual host containing the queue. | | `queue_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the queue. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_get_queue_bindings", {"vhost": "your-vhost", "queue_name": "your-queue_name"}) ``` --- ### `health_check` Check broker health **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_health_check", {}) ``` --- ### `list_bindings` List bindings in a virtual host | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | Virtual host name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_list_bindings", {"vhost": "your-vhost"}) ``` --- ### `list_channels` List active channels in the broker **Returns:** `PaginatedList[RabbitChannel]` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_list_channels", {}) ``` --- ### `list_connections` List active connections to the broker **Returns:** `PaginatedList[RabbitConnection]` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_list_connections", {}) ``` --- ### `list_consumers` List consumers | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | No | Filter consumers by virtual host. If omitted, lists consumers across all virtual hosts. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_list_consumers", {"vhost": "your-vhost"}) ``` --- ### `list_exchanges` List exchanges in the RabbitMQ broker | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | No | Filter exchanges by virtual host name. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[RabbitExchange]` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_list_exchanges", {"vhost": "your-vhost"}) ``` --- ### `list_queues` List queues in the RabbitMQ broker | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | No | Filter queues by virtual host name. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[RabbitQueue]` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_list_queues", {"vhost": "your-vhost"}) ``` --- ### `list_vhosts` List virtual hosts **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_list_vhosts", {}) ``` --- ### `publish_message` Publish a message to an exchange | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | Virtual host containing the exchange. | | `exchange` | `string` | Yes | Exchange name (use ``""`` for the default exchange). | | `routing_key` | `string` | Yes | Routing key for the message. | | `payload` | `string` | Yes | Message body as a string. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_publish_message", {"vhost": "your-vhost", "exchange": "your-exchange", "routing_key": "your-routing_key", "payload": "your-payload"}) ``` --- ### `purge_queue` :warning: Purge all messages from a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vhost` | `string` | Yes | Virtual host containing the queue. | | `queue_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the queue to purge. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("rabbitmq_purge_queue", {"vhost": "your-vhost", "queue_name": "your-queue_name"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is No hard limit (management API) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_binding`, `create_exchange`, `create_queue`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 21 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Sqs](../sqs/) — AWS queuing --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*
