redis.readme_md: # Redis (Upstash) > Serverless Redis with HTTP API ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Database-10B981) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-18-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/upstash/redis-py?style=social)](https://github.com/upstash/redis-py) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/upstash/redis-py) [![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-SDK-3178C6?logo=github)](https://github.com/upstash/upstash-redis) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Upstash Inc. | | **Category** | Database | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [upstash.com](https://upstash.com) | | **API Docs** | [upstash.com](https://upstash.com/docs/redis/overall/getstarted) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token | | **Rate Limit** | 1,000 requests/second | | **Pricing** | Free tier (10K commands/day), Pay-as-you-go from $0.2/100K commands | --- ## Overview The Upstash Redis REST API provides HTTP-based access to Redis data structures. Execute Redis commands over HTTP, manage keys, work with strings, lists, sets, hashes, and sorted sets without maintaining persistent connections. ## Use Cases - Serverless caching - Session management - Rate limiting - Real-time leaderboards - Pub/sub messaging ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[redis]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_REDIS_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["redis"], credentials={"redis": "your-token"}) # Append a value to a Redis string key result = kit.execute("redis_append", {"key": "my-key", "value": "my-value"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["redis"], credentials={"redis": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["redis"], credentials={"redis": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token 1. Create an account at [Redis (Upstash)](https://upstash.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.upstash.com/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("redis_append", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `append` Append a value to a Redis string key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key name. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | String to append. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_append", {"key": "my-key", "value": "my-value"}) ``` --- ### `dbsize` Get the number of keys in the Redis database **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_dbsize", {}) ``` --- ### `delete` :warning: Delete a Redis key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key name to delete. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_delete", {"key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `exists` Check if one or more keys exist | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `keys` | `array` | Yes | List of keys to check. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_exists", {"keys": "your-keys"}) ``` --- ### `expire` Set a TTL (expiry) on a key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key to set expiry on. | | `seconds` | `integer` | Yes | Time to live in seconds. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_expire", {"key": "my-key", "seconds": 10}) ``` --- ### `get` Get the value of a Redis key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key name. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_get", {"key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `hget` Get a field from a Redis hash | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key holding the hash. | | `field` | `string` | Yes | Field name within the hash. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_hget", {"key": "my-key", "field": "your-field"}) ``` --- ### `hset` Set a field in a Redis hash | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key holding the hash. | | `field` | `string` | Yes | Field name within the hash. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | Value to set. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_hset", {"key": "my-key", "field": "your-field", "value": "my-value"}) ``` --- ### `incr` Increment a key's integer value | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key to increment. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_incr", {"key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `keys` Find Redis keys matching a pattern | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `pattern` | `string` | No | Glob pattern (e.g. ``user:*``). Defaults to ``*``. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_keys", {"pattern": "your-pattern"}) ``` --- ### `lpush` Push values onto the head of a Redis list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key holding the list. | | `values` | `array` | Yes | Values to push (leftmost first). | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_lpush", {"key": "my-key", "values": "your-values"}) ``` --- ### `lrange` Get a range of elements from a Redis list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key holding the list. | | `start` | `integer` | Yes | Start index (0-based, supports negative). | | `stop` | `integer` | Yes | Stop index (inclusive, supports negative). | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_lrange", {"key": "my-key", "start": 10, "stop": 10}) ``` --- ### `mget` Get the values of multiple Redis keys | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `keys` | `array` | Yes | List of Redis key names. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_mget", {"keys": "your-keys"}) ``` --- ### `mset` Set multiple Redis keys at once | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key_values` | `object` | Yes | Dict mapping key names to string values. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_mset", {"key_values": "your-key_values"}) ``` --- ### `set` Set the value of a Redis key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key name. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | Value to store. | | `ex` | `string` | No | Optional expiry time in seconds. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_set", {"key": "my-key", "value": "my-value", "ex": "your-ex"}) ``` --- ### `setnx` Set a key only if it does not exist | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key name. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | Value to store. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_setnx", {"key": "my-key", "value": "my-value"}) ``` --- ### `ttl` Get the remaining TTL of a key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key to check. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_ttl", {"key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `type` Get the type of a key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key to check. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_type", {"key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 1,000 requests/second — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`delete`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Airtable](../airtable/) — Spreadsheet database - [Firestore](../firestore/) — Document database - [Mongodb](../mongodb/) — NoSQL database - [Supabase](../supabase/) — Postgres as a service --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

s3.readme_md: # AWS S3 > Scalable object storage in the cloud ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Storage-14B8A6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-20-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Storage | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/s3](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 (Access Key + Secret Key) | | **Rate Limit** | 5,500 GET/sec, 3,500 PUT/sec per prefix | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-use (from $0.023/GB/month) | --- ## Overview The AWS S3 API provides object storage operations for buckets and objects. Upload, download, list, and manage files. Configure lifecycle policies, access permissions, versioning, and static website hosting. ## Use Cases - File storage and retrieval - Static website hosting - Data lake storage - Backup and archival - Content distribution ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[s3]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_S3_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["s3"], credentials={"s3": "your-token"}) # Get the region (location) of an S3 bucket result = kit.execute("s3_get_bucket_location", {"bucket": "my-bucket"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["s3"], credentials={"s3": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["s3"], credentials={"s3": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 (Access Key + Secret Key) 1. Create an account at [AWS S3](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("s3_get_bucket_location", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `copy_object` Copy an object between S3 locations | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_bucket` | `string` | Yes | Source bucket name. | | `source_key` | `string` | Yes | Source object key. | | `dest_bucket` | `string` | Yes | Destination bucket name. | | `dest_key` | `string` | Yes | Destination object key. | **Returns:** `S3CopyResult` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_copy_object", {"source_bucket": "your-source_bucket", "source_key": "your-source_key", "dest_bucket": "your-dest_bucket", "dest_key": "your-dest_key"}) ``` --- ### `create_bucket` :warning: Create a new S3 bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name (must be globally unique). | | `region` | `string` | No | AWS region for the bucket. Defaults to connector region. | **Returns:** `S3Bucket` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_create_bucket", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "region": "your-region"}) ``` --- ### `delete_bucket` :warning: Delete an S3 bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_delete_bucket", {"bucket": "my-bucket"}) ``` --- ### `delete_object` :warning: Delete an object from S3 | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_delete_object", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `delete_object_tags` :warning: Delete all tags from an S3 object | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | | `version_id` | `string` | No | Optional version ID to delete tags for a specific object version. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_delete_object_tags", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key", "version_id": "your-version_id"}) ``` --- ### `generate_presigned_url` Generate a pre-signed URL for an S3 object | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | | `expiration` | `integer` | No | URL validity in seconds (default 3600 = 1 hour, max 604800 = 7 days). (default: `3600`) | | `method` | `string` | No | HTTP method the URL authorises (``GET`` or ``PUT``). (default: `GET`) | **Returns:** `S3PresignedUrl` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_generate_presigned_url", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key", "expiration": 3600, "method": "GET"}) ``` --- ### `get_bucket_location` Get the region (location) of an S3 bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | **Returns:** `S3BucketLocation` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_get_bucket_location", {"bucket": "my-bucket"}) ``` --- ### `get_bucket_policy` Get the bucket policy | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | **Returns:** `S3BucketPolicy` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_get_bucket_policy", {"bucket": "my-bucket"}) ``` --- ### `get_bucket_versioning` Get bucket versioning configuration | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | **Returns:** `S3BucketVersioning` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_get_bucket_versioning", {"bucket": "my-bucket"}) ``` --- ### `get_object` Download an object from S3 | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | **Returns:** `S3ObjectData` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_get_object", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `get_object_tags` Get tags on an S3 object | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | | `version_id` | `string` | No | Optional version ID to get tags for a specific object version. | **Returns:** `S3ObjectTagSet` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_get_object_tags", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key", "version_id": "your-version_id"}) ``` --- ### `head_object` Get object metadata without downloading the content | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | **Returns:** `S3ObjectMetadata` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_head_object", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `list_buckets` List all S3 buckets in the account **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_list_buckets", {}) ``` --- ### `list_multipart_uploads` List in-progress multipart uploads in a bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `prefix` | `string` | No | Filter uploads by key prefix. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum uploads per page (max 1000). (default: `1000`) | | `key_marker` | `string` | No | Key marker from a previous response for pagination. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[S3MultipartUpload]` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_list_multipart_uploads", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "prefix": "your-prefix", "limit": 1000}) ``` --- ### `list_object_versions` List object versions in a bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `prefix` | `string` | No | Filter versions by key prefix. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum versions per page (max 1000). (default: `1000`) | | `key_marker` | `string` | No | Key marker from a previous response for pagination. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[S3ObjectVersion]` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_list_object_versions", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "prefix": "your-prefix", "limit": 1000}) ``` --- ### `list_objects` List objects in an S3 bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `prefix` | `string` | No | Filter objects by key prefix. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of objects per page (max 1000). (default: `1000`) | | `continuation_token` | `string` | No | Token from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[S3Object]` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_list_objects", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "prefix": "your-prefix", "limit": 1000}) ``` --- ### `put_bucket_policy` :warning: Set the bucket policy | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `policy` | `string` | Yes | JSON policy document as a string. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_put_bucket_policy", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "policy": "your-policy"}) ``` --- ### `put_bucket_versioning` :warning: Set bucket versioning configuration | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `status` | `string` | Yes | Versioning status — ``"Enabled"`` or ``"Suspended"``. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_put_bucket_versioning", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "status": "active"}) ``` --- ### `put_object` :warning: Upload an object to S3 | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Object content as bytes. | | `content_type` | `string` | No | MIME type. Defaults to ``application/octet-stream``. | **Returns:** `S3PutResult` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_put_object", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key", "body": "Hello!", "content_type": "your-content_type"}) ``` --- ### `set_object_tags` Set tags on an S3 object | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | | `tags` | `object` | Yes | Dictionary of tag key-value pairs (max 10 tags). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_set_object_tags", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 5,500 GET/sec, 3,500 PUT/sec per prefix — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_bucket`, `delete_bucket`, `delete_object`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Gdrive](../gdrive/) — Google storage --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

salesforce.readme_md: # Salesforce > CRM, sales, service, and marketing platform ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Crm-F59E0B) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-21-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/simple-salesforce/simple-salesforce?style=social)](https://github.com/simple-salesforce/simple-salesforce) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/simple-salesforce/simple-salesforce) [![CLI](https://img.shields.io/badge/CLI-SDK-4D4D4D?logo=github)](https://github.com/forcedotcom/cli) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Salesforce Inc. | | **Category** | Crm | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [salesforce.com](https://salesforce.com) | | **API Docs** | [developer.salesforce.com](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.api_rest.meta/api_rest/) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0, Bearer Token | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by edition | | **Pricing** | Enterprise pricing (from $25/user/month) | --- ## Overview The Salesforce REST API provides access to CRM objects including leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, and cases. Run SOQL queries, manage records, and integrate with the world's most widely-used CRM platform. ## Use Cases - CRM data sync - Lead management - Sales pipeline automation - Customer support workflows - Marketing analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[salesforce]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SALESFORCE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["salesforce"], credentials={"salesforce": "your-token"}) # Run a SOSL search result = kit.execute("salesforce_search", {"sosl": "your-sosl"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["salesforce"], credentials={"salesforce": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["salesforce"], credentials={"salesforce": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 1. Setup 2. Apps 3. App Manager 4. Connected App [Get credentials →](https://login.salesforce.com) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("salesforce_search", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_account` :warning: Create a new Account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Account name. | | `industry` | `string` | No | Industry classification. | | `website` | `string` | No | Company website URL. | | `phone` | `string` | No | Phone number. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_account", {"name": "my-name", "industry": "your-industry", "website": "your-website"}) ``` --- ### `create_case` :warning: Create a new Case | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Case subject line. | | `description` | `string` | No | Detailed case description. | | `contact_id` | `string` | No | Contact ID to associate with the case. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Priority level (e.g., ``"High"``, ``"Medium"``). | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_case", {"subject": "Hello", "description": "your-description", "contact_id": "your-contact_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_contact` :warning: Create a new Contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `last_name` | `string` | Yes | Contact last name (required by Salesforce). | | `account_id` | `string` | No | Parent Account ID. | | `email` | `string` | No | Email address. | | `first_name` | `string` | No | Contact first name. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_contact", {"last_name": "your-last_name", "account_id": "your-account_id", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `create_event` :warning: Create a new Event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Event subject line. | | `start` | `string` | Yes | Start datetime in ISO 8601 format. | | `end` | `string` | Yes | End datetime in ISO 8601 format. | | `who_id` | `string` | No | Optional Contact or Lead ID to associate. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_event", {"subject": "Hello", "start": "your-start", "end": "your-end", "who_id": "your-who_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_lead` :warning: Create a new Lead | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company` | `string` | Yes | Company name (required by Salesforce). | | `last_name` | `string` | Yes | Contact last name (required by Salesforce). | | `email` | `string` | No | Email address. | | `first_name` | `string` | No | Contact first name. | | `phone` | `string` | No | Phone number. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_lead", {"company": "your-company", "last_name": "your-last_name", "email": "user@example.com", "first_name": "your-first_name"}) ``` --- ### `create_opportunity` :warning: Create a new Opportunity | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Opportunity name. | | `stage` | `string` | Yes | Sales stage (e.g., ``"Prospecting"``). | | `close_date` | `string` | Yes | Expected close date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | | `amount` | `string` | No | Monetary amount of the opportunity. | | `account_id` | `string` | No | Parent Account ID. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_opportunity", {"name": "my-name", "stage": "your-stage", "close_date": "your-close_date", "amount": "your-amount", "account_id": "your-account_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_record` :warning: Create a new sObject record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sobject` | `string` | Yes | sObject API name (e.g., ``"Account"``). | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field API names to values. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_record", {"sobject": "your-sobject", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ### `create_task` :warning: Create a new Task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Task subject line. | | `who_id` | `string` | No | Contact or Lead ID (the ``WhoId`` field). | | `what_id` | `string` | No | Account or Opportunity ID (the ``WhatId`` field). | | `due_date` | `string` | No | Due date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Priority level (e.g., ``"High"``, ``"Normal"``). | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_task", {"subject": "Hello", "who_id": "your-who_id", "what_id": "your-what_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_record` :warning: Delete an sObject record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sobject` | `string` | Yes | sObject API name (e.g., ``"Account"``). | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The Salesforce record ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_delete_record", {"sobject": "your-sobject", "record_id": "your-record_id"}) ``` --- ### `describe_global` Describe all sObjects in the org (global describe) **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_describe_global", {}) ``` --- ### `describe_object` Describe an sObject schema | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sobject` | `string` | Yes | sObject API name (e.g., ``"Account"``). | **Returns:** `SObjectDescription` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_describe_object", {"sobject": "your-sobject"}) ``` --- ### `get_limits` Get org API usage limits **Returns:** `SalesforceLimits` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_get_limits", {}) ``` --- ### `get_record` Get a single sObject record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sobject` | `string` | Yes | sObject API name (e.g., ``"Account"``). | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The Salesforce record ID. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_get_record", {"sobject": "your-sobject", "record_id": "your-record_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_objects` List all sObjects in the org **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_list_objects", {}) ``` --- ### `list_recent` List recently viewed records for an sObject | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sobject` | `string` | Yes | sObject API name (e.g., ``"Account"``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum records to return (max 200). (default: `25`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SalesforceRecord]` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_list_recent", {"sobject": "your-sobject", "limit": 25}) ``` --- ### `list_reports` List available reports **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_list_reports", {}) ``` --- ### `query` Run a SOQL query | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `soql` | `string` | Yes | SOQL query string (e.g., ``"SELECT Id, Name FROM Account"``). | | `limit` | `string` | No | Optional LIMIT clause override. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SalesforceRecord]` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_query", {"soql": "your-soql", "limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `run_report` Run a Salesforce report | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `report_id` | `string` | Yes | The 15/18-character report ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_run_report", {"report_id": "your-report_id"}) ``` --- ### `search` Run a SOSL search | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sosl` | `string` | Yes | SOSL query string. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_search", {"sosl": "your-sosl"}) ``` --- ### `update_record` Update an existing sObject record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sobject` | `string` | Yes | sObject API name (e.g., ``"Account"``). | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The Salesforce record ID. | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field API names to new values. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_update_record", {"sobject": "your-sobject", "record_id": "your-record_id", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ### `upsert_record` :warning: Upsert a record using an external ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sobject` | `string` | Yes | sObject API name (e.g., ``"Account"``). | | `external_id_field` | `string` | Yes | API name of the external ID field. | | `external_id` | `string` | Yes | The external ID value to match on. | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field API names to values. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_upsert_record", {"sobject": "your-sobject", "external_id_field": "your-external_id_field", "external_id": "your-external_id", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search` for filtered queries and `list_objects` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is Varies by edition — use segment.readme_md: # Segment > Customer data platform and event tracking ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Analytics-8B5CF6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-14-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/segmentio/analytics-python?style=social)](https://github.com/segmentio/analytics-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/segmentio/analytics-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/segmentio/analytics-node) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Twilio (Segment) | | **Category** | Analytics | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [segment.com](https://segment.com) | | **API Docs** | [segment.com](https://segment.com/docs/api/) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token (API Token) | | **Rate Limit** | 100 requests/second | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Team from $120/month | --- ## Overview The Segment API provides access to sources, destinations, tracking events, and user profiles. Collect and route customer data, manage tracking plans, configure destinations, and build unified customer data pipelines. ## Use Cases - Event tracking - Customer data routing - Analytics pipeline management - Data governance - User profile unification ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[segment]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SEGMENT_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["segment"], credentials={"segment": "your-token"}) # Get a specific destination for a source result = kit.execute("segment_get_destination", {"source_id": "your-source_id", "destination_id": "your-destination_id"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["segment"], credentials={"segment": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["segment"], credentials={"segment": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token (API Token) 1. Create an account at [Segment](https://segment.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://app.segment.com/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("segment_get_destination", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `alias` Create an alias for a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `previous_id` | `string` | Yes | The previous (anonymous) user identifier. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The new canonical user identifier. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_alias", {"previous_id": "your-previous_id", "user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `batch_identify` :warning: Send a batch of identify calls | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `users` | `array` | Yes | List of user dicts to identify. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_batch_identify", {"users": "your-users"}) ``` --- ### `batch_track` :warning: Send a batch of track events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `events` | `array` | Yes | List of event dicts to track. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_batch_track", {"events": "your-events"}) ``` --- ### `create_source` :warning: Create a new Segment source | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the source. | | `catalog_name` | `string` | Yes | Catalog name (e.g. ``"catalog/sources/javascript"``). | **Returns:** `SegmentSource` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_create_source", {"name": "my-name", "catalog_name": "your-catalog_name"}) ``` --- ### `delete_source` :warning: Delete a Segment source | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_id` | `string` | Yes | The source ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_delete_source", {"source_id": "your-source_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_destination` Get a specific destination for a source | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_id` | `string` | Yes | The source ID. | | `destination_id` | `string` | Yes | The destination ID. | **Returns:** `SegmentDestination` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_get_destination", {"source_id": "your-source_id", "destination_id": "your-destination_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_source` Get a specific source by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique identifier of the source. | **Returns:** `SegmentSource` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_get_source", {"source_id": "your-source_id"}) ``` --- ### `group` Associate a user with a group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the user. | | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the group. | | `traits` | `string` | No | Group traits such as name, industry, employees, etc. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_group", {"user_id": "user-123", "group_id": "your-group_id", "traits": "your-traits"}) ``` --- ### `identify` Identify a user with traits | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the user. | | `traits` | `string` | No | User traits such as name, email, plan, etc. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_identify", {"user_id": "user-123", "traits": "your-traits"}) ``` --- ### `list_destinations` List destinations for a source | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique identifier of the source. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_list_destinations", {"source_id": "your-source_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_sources` List sources in the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of sources to return. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SegmentSource]` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_list_sources", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `list_warehouses` List connected warehouses **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_list_warehouses", {}) ``` --- ### `page` Track a page view | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the user. | | `name` | `string` | No | Name of the page being viewed. | | `properties` | `string` | No | Additional properties for the page view. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_page", {"user_id": "user-123", "name": "my-name", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ### `track` Track an event for a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the user. | | `event` | `string` | Yes | Name of the event to track. | | `properties` | `string` | No | Properties associated with the event. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_track", {"user_id": "user-123", "event": "your-event", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use batch operations (`batch_identify`, `batch_track`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls - Rate limit is 100 requests/second — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`batch_identify`, `batch_track`, `create_source`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Mixpanel](../mixpanel/) — Product analytics --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

sendgrid.readme_md: # SendGrid > Transactional and marketing email delivery ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Marketing-EC4899) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-20-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/sendgrid/sendgrid-python?style=social)](https://github.com/sendgrid/sendgrid-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/sendgrid/sendgrid-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/sendgrid/sendgrid-nodejs) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Twilio (SendGrid) | | **Category** | Marketing | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [sendgrid.com](https://sendgrid.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.sendgrid.com](https://docs.sendgrid.com/api-reference) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan | | **Pricing** | Free up to 100 emails/day, Essentials from $19.95/month | --- ## Overview The SendGrid API provides email sending, template management, contact lists, and analytics. Send transactional and marketing emails at scale, manage dynamic templates, track opens and clicks, and maintain sender reputation. ## Use Cases - Transactional email - Marketing campaigns - Email template management - Delivery analytics - Sender reputation management ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[sendgrid]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SENDGRID_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["sendgrid"], credentials={"sendgrid": "your-token"}) # List marketing contacts from SendGrid result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_contacts", {"limit": 50}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["sendgrid"], credentials={"sendgrid": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["sendgrid"], credentials={"sendgrid": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Create an account at [SendGrid](https://sendgrid.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://app.sendgrid.com/settings/api_keys) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_contacts", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_contacts` :warning: Add or update marketing contacts in SendGrid | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contacts` | `array` | Yes | List of contact dicts with at least ``email`` key. | **Returns:** `SendGridJobId` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_add_contacts", {"contacts": "your-contacts"}) ``` --- ### `add_to_suppression` :warning: Add emails to global suppression group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `emails` | `array` | Yes | List of email addresses to globally suppress. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_add_to_suppression", {"emails": "your-emails"}) ``` --- ### `create_list` :warning: Create a new contact list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new list. | **Returns:** `SendGridList` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_create_list", {"name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `delete_contact` :warning: Delete a contact by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The SendGrid contact ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_delete_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_bounces` Get bounced email addresses | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of bounce records to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_bounces", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `get_email_activity` Query email activity feed | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | No | SGQL-style filter query string (e.g. ``"status=delivered AND to_email=user@example.com"``). If omitted, returns the most recent messages. | | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of messages to return (default 10). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_email_activity", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `get_global_suppressions` Get global suppressions with limit | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of suppression records to return. | | `offset` | `string` | No | Starting index for pagination. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_global_suppressions", {"limit": "your-limit", "offset": "your-offset"}) ``` --- ### `get_spam_reports` Get spam report records | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of spam reports to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_spam_reports", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `get_stats` Retrieve email statistics from SendGrid | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `start_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | | `end_date` | `string` | No | End date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. Defaults to today. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_stats", {"start_date": "your-start_date", "end_date": "your-end_date"}) ``` --- ### `get_template` Retrieve a single SendGrid template by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `template_id` | `string` | Yes | The SendGrid template ID. | **Returns:** `SendGridTemplate` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_template", {"template_id": "your-template_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_template_version` Get a specific template version | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `template_id` | `string` | Yes | The SendGrid template ID. | | `version_id` | `string` | Yes | The template version ID. | **Returns:** `SendGridTemplateVersion` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_template_version", {"template_id": "your-template_id", "version_id": "your-version_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_contacts` List marketing contacts from SendGrid | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of contacts to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SendGridContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_contacts", {"limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_lists` List all contact lists in SendGrid **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_lists", {}) ``` --- ### `list_sender_identities` List verified sender identities **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_sender_identities", {}) ``` --- ### `list_suppressions` List global email suppressions **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_suppressions", {}) ``` --- ### `list_templates` List transactional templates from SendGrid | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of templates to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SendGridTemplate]` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_templates", {"limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `remove_from_suppression` :warning: Remove an email from global suppression group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | Yes | The email address to remove from global suppression. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_remove_from_suppression", {"email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `search_contacts` Search marketing contacts using a SGQL query | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | SGQL query string. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SendGridContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_search_contacts", {"query": "is:unread"}) ``` --- ### `send_email` :warning: Send an email via SendGrid | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `string` | Yes | Recipient email address. | | `from_email` | `string` | Yes | Sender email address. | | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Email subject line. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Email body content. | | `content_type` | `string` | No | MIME type of content (``text/plain`` or ``text/html``). Defaults to ``text/plain``. (default: `text/plain`) | **Returns:** `SendGridResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_send_email", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "from_email": "your-from_email", "subject": "Hello", "content": "your-content", "content_type": "text/plain"}) ``` --- ### `send_template_email` :warning: Send an email using a dynamic template | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `string` | Yes | Recipient email address. | | `from_email` | `string` | Yes | Sender email address. | | `template_id` | `string` | Yes | The SendGrid template ID to use. | | `dynamic_data` | `string` | No | Dynamic template data (Handlebars variables). | **Returns:** `SendGridResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_send_template_email", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "from_email": "your-from_email", "template_id": "your-template_id", "dynamic_data": "your-dynamic_data"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_contacts` for filtered queries and `list_contacts` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is Varies by plan — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_contacts`, `add_to_suppression`, `create_list`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Mailchimp](../mailchimp/) — Email marketing --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

shopify.readme_md: # Shopify > E-commerce platform for online stores ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Ecommerce-84CC16) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-27-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/Shopify/shopify-api-python?style=social)](https://github.com/Shopify/shopify-api-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/Shopify/shopify-api-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/Shopify/shopify-api-js) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Shopify Inc. | | **Category** | Ecommerce | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [shopify.com](https://shopify.com) | | **API Docs** | [shopify.dev](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-rest) | | **Auth** | API Key (Admin API Access Token) | | **Rate Limit** | 40 requests/second (Plus: 80/sec) | | **Pricing** | Basic from $39/month | --- ## Overview The Shopify Admin API provides access to products, orders, customers, inventory, and fulfillments. Manage your online store, process orders, track inventory, handle refunds, and build custom e-commerce integrations. ## Use Cases - Product catalog management - Order processing - Inventory tracking - Customer management - Fulfillment automation ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[shopify]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SHOPIFY_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["shopify"], credentials={"shopify": "your-token"}) # List products from your Shopify store result = kit.execute("shopify_list_products", {"limit": 50, "since_id": "your-since_id"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["shopify"], credentials={"shopify": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["shopify"], credentials={"shopify": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key (Admin API Access Token) 1. Create an account at [Shopify](https://shopify.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/authentication/access-tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("shopify_list_products", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `cancel_order` :warning: Cancel an order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify order ID. | **Returns:** `ShopifyOrder` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_cancel_order", {"order_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `complete_draft_order` :warning: Complete a draft order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `draft_order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify draft order ID. | **Returns:** `ShopifyDraftOrder` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_complete_draft_order", {"draft_order_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `count_products` Count products in your Shopify store **Returns:** `int` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_count_products", {}) ``` --- ### `create_customer` :warning: Create a new customer | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | Yes | Customer email address (required). | | `first_name` | `string` | No | Customer's first name. | | `last_name` | `string` | No | Customer's last name. | **Returns:** `ShopifyCustomer` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_customer", {"email": "user@example.com", "first_name": "your-first_name", "last_name": "your-last_name"}) ``` --- ### `create_discount` :warning: Create a price rule / discount | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | The discount title. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | The discount value (e.g. ``"-10.0"`` for 10 off). | | `type` | `string` | Yes | Value type (``"percentage"`` or ``"fixed_amount"``). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_discount", {"title": "My Title", "value": "my-value", "type": "your-type"}) ``` --- ### `create_draft_order` :warning: Create a draft order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `line_items` | `array` | Yes | List of line item dicts, each containing ``variant_id`` and ``quantity`` (or ``title``, ``price``, and ``quantity`` for custom items). | | `customer` | `string` | No | Optional customer dict with an ``id`` key to associate the draft order with an existing customer. | | `note` | `string` | No | Optional note text for the draft order. | **Returns:** `ShopifyDraftOrder` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_draft_order", {"line_items": "your-line_items", "customer": "your-customer", "note": "your-note"}) ``` --- ### `create_product` :warning: Create a new product in your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | Product title. | | `body_html` | `string` | No | HTML description of the product. | | `vendor` | `string` | No | Product vendor name. | | `product_type` | `string` | No | Product type/category. | | `variants` | `string` | No | List of variant dicts (price, sku, etc.). | **Returns:** `ShopifyProduct` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_product", {"title": "My Title", "body_html": "your-body_html", "vendor": "your-vendor"}) ``` --- ### `create_product_variant` :warning: Create a variant for a product | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify product ID. | | `option1` | `string` | Yes | Value for the first product option (e.g. size/colour). | | `price` | `string` | Yes | Variant price as a decimal string (e.g. ``"29.99"``). | | `sku` | `string` | No | Optional stock keeping unit identifier. | **Returns:** `ShopifyVariant` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_product_variant", {"product_id": 10, "option1": "your-option1", "price": "your-price", "sku": "your-sku"}) ``` --- ### `create_webhook` :warning: Create a webhook subscription | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `topic` | `string` | Yes | Event topic to subscribe to (e.g. ``"orders/create"``, ``"products/update"``). | | `address` | `string` | Yes | HTTPS URL that will receive webhook POST requests. | | `format_` | `string` | No | Payload format (``"json"`` or ``"xml"``). (default: `json`) | **Returns:** `ShopifyWebhook` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_webhook", {"topic": "your-topic", "address": "your-address", "format_": "json"}) ``` --- ### `delete_product` :warning: Delete a product from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify product ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_delete_product", {"product_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `fulfill_order` :warning: Fulfill an order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify order ID to fulfill. | | `tracking_number` | `string` | No | Optional shipment tracking number. | | `tracking_company` | `string` | No | Optional shipping carrier name. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_fulfill_order", {"order_id": 10, "tracking_number": "your-tracking_number", "tracking_company": "your-tracking_company"}) ``` --- ### `get_customer` Get a single Shopify customer by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify customer ID. | **Returns:** `ShopifyCustomer` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_get_customer", {"customer_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_order` Get a single Shopify order by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify order ID. | **Returns:** `ShopifyOrder` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_get_order", {"order_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_product` Get a single Shopify product by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify product ID. | **Returns:** `ShopifyProduct` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_get_product", {"product_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_collections` List collections from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of collections to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_collections", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `list_customers` List customers from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of customers to return (1-250). (default: `50`) | | `since_id` | `string` | No | Only return customers after this ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ShopifyCustomer]` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_customers", {"limit": 50, "since_id": "your-since_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_draft_orders` List draft orders from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of draft orders to return (1-250). (default: `50`) | | `since_id` | `string` | No | Only return draft orders after this ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ShopifyDraftOrder]` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_draft_orders", {"limit": 50, "since_id": "your-since_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_fulfillments` List fulfillments for an order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify order ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_fulfillments", {"order_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_inventory_levels` List inventory levels by location | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `location_id` | `string` | No | Optional location ID to filter by. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_inventory_levels", {"location_id": "your-location_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_locations` List locations for your Shopify store **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_locations", {}) ``` --- ### `list_orders` List orders from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (open, closed, cancelled, any). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of orders to return (1-250). (default: `50`) | | `since_id` | `string` | No | Only return orders after this ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ShopifyOrder]` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_orders", {"status": "active", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_product_variants` List variants for a product | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify product ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_product_variants", {"product_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_products` List products from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of products to return (1-250). (default: `50`) | | `since_id` | `string` | No | Only return products after this ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ShopifyProduct]` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_products", {"limit": 50, "since_id": "your-since_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_webhooks` List webhooks from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of webhooks to return (max 250). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_webhooks", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `update_inventory` Update inventory quantity for an item | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `inventory_item_id` | `integer` | Yes | The inventory item ID. | | `available` | `integer` | Yes | The new available quantity. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_update_inventory", {"inventory_item_id": 10, "available": 10}) ``` --- ### `update_order` Update an existing order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify order ID. | | `note` | `string` | No | New note for the order. | **Returns:** `ShopifyOrder` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_update_order", {"order_id": 10, "note": "your-note"}) ``` --- ### `update_product` :warning: Update an existing Shopify product | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify product ID. | | `title` | `string` | No | New product title. | | `body_html` | `string` | No | New HTML description. | **Returns:** `ShopifyProduct` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_update_product", {"product_id": 10, "title": "My Title", "body_html": "your-body_html"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 40 requests/second (Plus: 80/sec) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`cancel_order`, `complete_draft_order`, `create_customer`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 27 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Stripe](../stripe/) — Payments --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:

strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:

    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.
