slack.readme_md: # Slack > Team messaging, channels, and collaboration ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Communication-3B82F6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-51-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/slackapi/python-slack-sdk?style=social)](https://github.com/slackapi/python-slack-sdk) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/slackapi/python-slack-sdk) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/slackapi/node-slack-sdk) [![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/Java-SDK-ED8B00?logo=github)](https://github.com/slackapi/java-slack-sdk) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Salesforce (Slack Technologies) | | **Category** | Communication | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [slack.com](https://slack.com) | | **API Docs** | [api.slack.com](https://api.slack.com/methods) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0, Bot Token | | **Rate Limit** | 1 request/second (Tier 2) | | **Pricing** | Free tier available, Pro from $8.75/user/month | --- ## Overview The Slack Web API provides comprehensive access to Slack workspaces. Send and manage messages, create and manage channels, upload files, manage users, set reminders, and build interactive bot experiences. Supports both bot tokens and user tokens. ## Use Cases - Team notifications and alerts - Customer support bots - DevOps alerting - Workflow automation - Internal tooling ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[slack]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SLACK_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["slack"], credentials={"slack": "your-token"}) # List messages in a channel result = kit.execute("slack_list_messages", {"channel": "general", "limit": 100, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["slack"], credentials={"slack": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["slack"], credentials={"slack": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 1. Slack API 2. Your Apps 3. Create New App 4. Bot Token [Get credentials →](https://api.slack.com/apps) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("slack_list_messages", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_bookmark` Add a bookmark to a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Display title for the bookmark. | | `link` | `string` | Yes | URL for the bookmark. | | `emoji` | `string` | No | Optional emoji to display (e.g. ``:link:``). | **Returns:** `Bookmark` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_add_bookmark", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "title": "My Title", "link": "your-link", "emoji": "your-emoji"}) ``` --- ### `add_reaction` Add a reaction emoji to a message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the message. | | `timestamp` | `string` | Yes | Message timestamp (``ts`` field) to react to. | | `emoji` | `string` | Yes | Emoji name without colons (e.g. ``thumbsup``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_add_reaction", {"channel": "general", "timestamp": "your-timestamp", "emoji": "your-emoji"}) ``` --- ### `add_reminder` Create a reminder | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `text` | `string` | Yes | The reminder text. | | `time` | `string` | Yes | When to trigger — Unix timestamp, or natural language like ``in 15 minutes``, ``every Thursday``. | | `user` | `string` | No | Optional user ID. Defaults to the authed user. | **Returns:** `Reminder` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_add_reminder", {"text": "Hello!", "time": "your-time", "user": "your-user"}) ``` --- ### `archive_channel` :warning: Archive a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to archive. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_archive_channel", {"channel": "general"}) ``` --- ### `auth_test` Test authentication and get workspace info **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_auth_test", {}) ``` --- ### `create_channel` :warning: Create a new channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the channel (lowercase, no spaces, max 80 characters). | | `is_private` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, create a private channel. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `Channel` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_create_channel", {"name": "my-name", "is_private": False}) ``` --- ### `create_usergroup` :warning: Create a user group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the user group. | | `handle` | `string` | Yes | Short mention handle (e.g. ``engineering`` for ``@engineering``). Must be unique in the workspace. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional purpose/description for the group. | | `channels` | `string` | No | Optional comma-separated channel IDs to associate with the group as default channels. | **Returns:** `SlackUserGroup` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_create_usergroup", {"name": "my-name", "handle": "your-handle", "description": "your-description", "channels": "your-channels"}) ``` --- ### `delete_file` :warning: Delete a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_delete_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_message` :warning: Delete a message from a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the message. | | `ts` | `string` | Yes | Timestamp (``ts``) of the message to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_delete_message", {"channel": "general", "ts": "your-ts"}) ``` --- ### `delete_reminder` :warning: Delete a reminder | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `reminder_id` | `string` | Yes | The reminder ID. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_delete_reminder", {"reminder_id": "your-reminder_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_scheduled_message` :warning: Delete a scheduled message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID the message was scheduled in. | | `scheduled_message_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the scheduled message. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_delete_scheduled_message", {"channel": "general", "scheduled_message_id": "your-scheduled_message_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_channel` Get a single channel by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel ID (e.g. ``C01234ABCDE``). | **Returns:** `Channel` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_get_channel", {"channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `get_file_info` Get file info | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID (e.g. ``F01234ABCDE``). | **Returns:** `SlackFile` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_get_file_info", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_permalink` Get a permalink URL for a specific message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the message. | | `message_ts` | `string` | Yes | Timestamp (``ts``) of the message. | **Returns:** `str` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_get_permalink", {"channel": "general", "message_ts": "your-message_ts"}) ``` --- ### `get_reactions` Get reactions for a message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the message. | | `timestamp` | `string` | Yes | Message timestamp (``ts`` field). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_get_reactions", {"channel": "general", "timestamp": "your-timestamp"}) ``` --- ### `get_team_info` Get workspace team information **Returns:** `SlackTeam` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_get_team_info", {}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a single user by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user ID (e.g. ``U01234ABCDE``). | **Returns:** `SlackUser` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_get_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_user_presence` Get a user's presence status | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user ID. | **Returns:** `UserPresence` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_get_user_presence", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_user_profile` Get a user's profile | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user ID. | **Returns:** `UserProfile` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_get_user_profile", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `invite_to_channel` Invite a user to a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to invite users to. | | `users` | `string` | Yes | Comma-separated user IDs (e.g. ``U01,U02``). | **Returns:** `Channel` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_invite_to_channel", {"channel": "general", "users": "your-users"}) ``` --- ### `join_channel` Join a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to join. | **Returns:** `Channel` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_join_channel", {"channel": "general"}) ``` --- ### `kick_from_channel` :warning: Remove a user from a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID. | | `user` | `string` | Yes | User ID to remove. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_kick_from_channel", {"channel": "general", "user": "your-user"}) ``` --- ### `leave_channel` Leave a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to leave. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_leave_channel", {"channel": "general"}) ``` --- ### `list_bookmarks` List bookmarks in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_bookmarks", {"channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `list_channel_members` List members of a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of members to return (max 1000). (default: `100`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[str]` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_channel_members", {"channel": "general", "limit": 100, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_channels` List channels in the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of channels to return (max 1000). (default: `100`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | | `exclude_archived` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, exclude archived channels. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Channel]` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_channels", {"limit": 100, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_emoji` List custom emoji in the workspace **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_emoji", {}) ``` --- ### `list_messages` List messages in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to fetch messages from. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of messages to return (max 1000). (default: `100`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | | `oldest` | `string` | No | Only messages after this Unix timestamp. | | `latest` | `string` | No | Only messages before this Unix timestamp. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Message]` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_messages", {"channel": "general", "limit": 100, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_pins` List pinned items in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to list pins for. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_pins", {"channel": "general"}) ``` --- ### `list_reminders` List all reminders **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_reminders", {}) ``` --- ### `list_scheduled_messages` List scheduled messages | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | No | Optional channel ID to filter by. | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of results to return (max 100). (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ScheduledMessage]` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_scheduled_messages", {"channel": "general", "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_thread_replies` List replies in a message thread | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the thread. | | `thread_ts` | `string` | Yes | Timestamp of the parent message. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of replies to return (max 1000). (default: `100`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Message]` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_thread_replies", {"channel": "general", "thread_ts": "your-thread_ts", "limit": 100, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_usergroups` List user groups in the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `include_users` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, include the list of user IDs for each user group. (default: `False`) | | `include_disabled` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, include disabled user groups. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_usergroups", {"include_users": False, "include_disabled": False}) ``` --- ### `list_users` List users in the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of users to return (max 1000). (default: `100`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SlackUser]` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_users", {"limit": 100, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `lookup_user_by_email` Look up a user by email address | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | Yes | The email address to look up. | **Returns:** `SlackUser` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_lookup_user_by_email", {"email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `pin_message` Pin a message to a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the message. | | `timestamp` | `string` | Yes | Message timestamp (``ts`` field) to pin. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_pin_message", {"channel": "general", "timestamp": "your-timestamp"}) ``` --- ### `remove_bookmark` :warning: Remove a bookmark from a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bookmark_id` | `string` | Yes | The bookmark ID. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel ID. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_remove_bookmark", {"bookmark_id": "your-bookmark_id", "channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `remove_reaction` Remove a reaction emoji from a message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the message. | | `timestamp` | `string` | Yes | Message timestamp (``ts`` field). | | `emoji` | `string` | Yes | Emoji name without colons (e.g. ``thumbsup``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_remove_reaction", {"channel": "general", "timestamp": "your-timestamp", "emoji": "your-emoji"}) ``` --- ### `rename_channel` :warning: Rename a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to rename. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | New channel name (lowercase, no spaces, max 80 chars). | **Returns:** `Channel` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_rename_channel", {"channel": "general", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `schedule_message` :warning: Schedule a message for future delivery | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to send to. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | Message text (supports Slack mrkdwn formatting). | | `post_at` | `integer` | Yes | Unix timestamp for when to send the message. | | `thread_ts` | `string` | No | Optional parent message timestamp for threading. | **Returns:** `ScheduledMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_schedule_message", {"channel": "general", "text": "Hello!", "post_at": 10, "thread_ts": "your-thread_ts"}) ``` --- ### `search_messages` Search for messages across the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query (supports Slack search modifiers like ``from:@user``, ``in:#channel``, ``has:link``). | | `sort` | `string` | No | Sort field — ``timestamp`` or ``score``. (default: `timestamp`) | | `sort_dir` | `string` | No | Sort direction — ``asc`` or ``desc``. (default: `desc`) | | `count` | `integer` | No | Number of results per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-based). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_search_messages", {"query": "is:unread", "sort": "timestamp", "sort_dir": "desc"}) ``` --- ### `send_message` :warning: Send a message to a Slack channel or thread | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID (e.g. ``C01234ABCDE``) or name. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | Message text (supports Slack mrkdwn formatting). | | `thread_ts` | `string` | No | Optional parent message timestamp to reply in a thread. | | `unfurl_links` | `boolean` | No | Whether to unfurl text-based URLs. (default: `True`) | | `unfurl_media` | `boolean` | No | Whether to unfurl media content. (default: `True`) | **Returns:** `Message` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_send_message", {"channel": "general", "text": "Hello!", "thread_ts": "your-thread_ts", "unfurl_links": True}) ``` --- ### `set_channel_purpose` Set the purpose of a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID. | | `purpose` | `string` | Yes | New purpose string (max 250 characters). | **Returns:** `Channel` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_set_channel_purpose", {"channel": "general", "purpose": "your-purpose"}) ``` --- ### `set_channel_topic` Set the topic of a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID. | | `topic` | `string` | Yes | New topic string (max 250 characters). | **Returns:** `Channel` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_set_channel_topic", {"channel": "general", "topic": "your-topic"}) ``` --- ### `set_presence` Set the bot user's presence status | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `presence` | `string` | Yes | Either ``auto`` or ``away``. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_set_presence", {"presence": "your-presence"}) ``` --- ### `set_status` :warning: Set the authenticated user's status | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `status_text` | `string` | Yes | Status text to display (max 100 characters). | | `status_emoji` | `string` | No | Optional emoji code (e.g. ``:house_with_garden:``). Pass an empty string to clear the emoji. | | `expiration` | `string` | No | Optional Unix timestamp for when the status should expire. Pass ``0`` to keep the status indefinitely. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_set_status", {"status_text": "your-status_text", "status_emoji": "your-status_emoji", "expiration": "your-expiration"}) ``` --- ### `unarchive_channel` Unarchive a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to unarchive. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_unarchive_channel", {"channel": "general"}) ``` --- ### `unpin_message` Unpin a message from a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID. | | `timestamp` | `string` | Yes | Message timestamp (``ts`` field) to unpin. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_unpin_message", {"channel": "general", "timestamp": "your-timestamp"}) ``` --- ### `update_message` :warning: Update an existing message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the message. | | `ts` | `string` | Yes | Timestamp (``ts``) of the message to update. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | New message text (supports Slack mrkdwn formatting). | **Returns:** `Message` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_update_message", {"channel": "general", "ts": "your-ts", "text": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `update_usergroup` :warning: Update an existing user group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `usergroup_id` | `string` | Yes | The user group ID (e.g. ``S01234ABCDE``). | | `name` | `string` | No | Optional new display name. | | `handle` | `string` | No | Optional new mention handle. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional new description. | | `channels` | `string` | No | Optional comma-separated channel IDs to set as default channels. | **Returns:** `SlackUserGroup` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_update_usergroup", {"usergroup_id": "your-usergroup_id", "name": "my-name", "handle": "your-handle"}) ``` --- ### `upload_file` :warning: Upload a file to Slack channels | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channels` | `string` | Yes | Comma-separated channel IDs to share the file in. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | File content as a string. | | `filename` | `string` | Yes | Name for the uploaded file. | | `title` | `string` | No | Optional display title for the file. | **Returns:** `SlackFile` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_upload_file", {"channels": "your-channels", "content": "your-content", "filename": "your-filename", "title": "My Title"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_messages` for filtered queries and `list_bookmarks` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 1 request/second (Tier 2) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`archive_channel`, `create_channel`, `create_usergroup`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses ## Related Connectors - [Gmail](../gmail/) — Email automation - [Discord](../discord/) — Community messaging - [Teams](../teams/) — Microsoft collaboration - [Telegram](../telegram/) — Bot messaging --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

sqs.readme_md: # AWS SQS > Fully managed message queuing service ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Message_Queue-EF4444) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-16-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Message Queue | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/sqs](https://aws.amazon.com/sqs/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/APIReference/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 (Access Key + Secret Key) | | **Rate Limit** | Unlimited (Standard), 300 msg/sec (FIFO) | | **Pricing** | Free tier (1M requests/month), then $0.40/million | --- ## Overview The AWS SQS API provides message queuing operations for decoupling distributed systems. Send, receive, and delete messages. Manage queues, configure dead-letter queues, and build reliable event-driven architectures. ## Use Cases - Microservice decoupling - Task queuing - Event-driven architectures - Batch processing - Dead-letter queue management ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[sqs]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SQS_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["sqs"], credentials={"sqs": "your-token"}) # List SQS queues in the account result = kit.execute("sqs_list_queues", {"prefix": "your-prefix"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["sqs"], credentials={"sqs": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["sqs"], credentials={"sqs": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 (Access Key + Secret Key) 1. Create an account at [AWS SQS](https://aws.amazon.com/sqs/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("sqs_list_queues", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_permission` :warning: Add permission to a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue. | | `label` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the permission being set (max 80 chars, alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores). | | `aws_account_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of AWS account IDs to grant permission to. | | `actions` | `array` | Yes | List of SQS actions to allow (e.g. ``SendMessage``, ``ReceiveMessage``, ``DeleteMessage``, ``ChangeMessageVisibility``, ``GetQueueAttributes``, ``GetQueueUrl``, or ``*``). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_add_permission", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "label": "your-label", "aws_account_ids": "your-aws_account_ids", "actions": "your-actions"}) ``` --- ### `change_message_visibility` Change message visibility timeout | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue. | | `receipt_handle` | `string` | Yes | The receipt handle of the message. | | `timeout` | `integer` | Yes | New visibility timeout in seconds (0-43200). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_change_message_visibility", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "receipt_handle": "your-receipt_handle", "timeout": 10}) ``` --- ### `create_queue` Create a new SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new queue. | | `attributes` | `string` | No | Optional queue attributes (e.g. VisibilityTimeout). | **Returns:** `SQSQueue` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_create_queue", {"queue_name": "your-queue_name", "attributes": "your-attributes"}) ``` --- ### `delete_message` :warning: Delete a message from an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue. | | `receipt_handle` | `string` | Yes | The receipt handle of the message to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_delete_message", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "receipt_handle": "your-receipt_handle"}) ``` --- ### `delete_queue` :warning: Delete an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the queue to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_delete_queue", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url"}) ``` --- ### `get_queue_attributes` Get attributes of an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue. | **Returns:** `SQSQueueAttributes` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_get_queue_attributes", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url"}) ``` --- ### `get_queue_url` Get queue URL by name | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_name` | `string` | Yes | The name of the SQS queue. | **Returns:** `str` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_get_queue_url", {"queue_name": "your-queue_name"}) ``` --- ### `list_dead_letter_queues` List dead letter source queues | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_queue_arn` | `string` | Yes | The ARN of the dead-letter queue to query. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_list_dead_letter_queues", {"source_queue_arn": "your-source_queue_arn"}) ``` --- ### `list_queues` List SQS queues in the account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `prefix` | `string` | No | Filter queues to those whose name starts with this prefix. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SQSQueue]` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_list_queues", {"prefix": "your-prefix"}) ``` --- ### `purge_queue` :warning: Purge all messages from an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the queue to purge. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_purge_queue", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url"}) ``` --- ### `receive_messages` Receive messages from an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue to poll. | | `max_messages` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of messages to receive (1-10). (default: `1`) | | `wait_time` | `string` | No | Long-poll wait time in seconds (0-20). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_receive_messages", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "max_messages": 1, "wait_time": "your-wait_time"}) ``` --- ### `remove_permission` :warning: Remove permission from a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue. | | `label` | `string` | Yes | The identifier of the permission to remove (the label used when calling ``add_permission``). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_remove_permission", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "label": "your-label"}) ``` --- ### `send_message` Send a message to an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the target SQS queue. | | `message_body` | `string` | Yes | The message body string. | | `delay_seconds` | `string` | No | Delay in seconds before the message becomes visible. | **Returns:** `SQSSendResult` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_send_message", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "message_body": "your-message_body", "delay_seconds": "your-delay_seconds"}) ``` --- ### `send_message_batch` Send a batch of messages to an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the target SQS queue. | | `entries` | `array` | Yes | List of message entries, each containing ``Id`` and ``MessageBody``, and optionally ``DelaySeconds``. | **Returns:** `SQSBatchResult` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_send_message_batch", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "entries": "your-entries"}) ``` --- ### `set_queue_attributes` Set attributes on a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the queue. | | `attributes` | `object` | Yes | Dict of attribute names to values. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_set_queue_attributes", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "attributes": "your-attributes"}) ``` --- ### `tag_queue` Add tags to a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the queue. | | `tags` | `object` | Yes | Dict of tag keys to values. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_tag_queue", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use batch operations (`send_message_batch`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls - Rate limit is Unlimited (Standard), 300 msg/sec (FIFO) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_permission`, `delete_message`, `delete_queue`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Rabbitmq](../rabbitmq/) — Message broker --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

stripe.readme_md: # Stripe > Payment processing, subscriptions, and billing ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Finance-6366F1) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-40-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/stripe/stripe-python?style=social)](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-node) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-go) [![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/Java-SDK-ED8B00?logo=github)](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-java) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Stripe Inc. | | **Category** | Finance | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [stripe.com](https://stripe.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.stripe.com](https://docs.stripe.com/api) | | **Auth** | API Key (Secret Key) | | **Rate Limit** | 100 read/sec, 100 write/sec | | **Pricing** | 2.9% + 30c per transaction | --- ## Overview The Stripe API provides a complete payment infrastructure. Process payments, manage subscriptions, handle invoices, issue refunds, and build checkout experiences. Supports cards, bank transfers, and 100+ payment methods worldwide. ## Use Cases - Payment processing - Subscription management - Invoice generation - Marketplace payments - Revenue analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[stripe]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_STRIPE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["stripe"], credentials={"stripe": "your-token"}) # List events from your Stripe account result = kit.execute("stripe_list_events", {"type": "your-type", "limit": 10}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["stripe"], credentials={"stripe": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["stripe"], credentials={"stripe": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key (Secret Key) 1. Dashboard 2. Developers 3. API Keys [Get credentials →](https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("stripe_list_events", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `cancel_payment_intent` Cancel a Stripe PaymentIntent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `payment_intent_id` | `string` | Yes | The PaymentIntent ID to cancel. | **Returns:** `PaymentIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_cancel_payment_intent", {"payment_intent_id": "your-payment_intent_id"}) ``` --- ### `cancel_subscription` :warning: Cancel a subscription | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subscription_id` | `string` | Yes | The subscription ID to cancel. | | `at_period_end` | `boolean` | No | If True, cancel at end of current billing period. If False, cancel immediately. (default: `True`) | **Returns:** `StripeSubscription` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_cancel_subscription", {"subscription_id": "your-subscription_id", "at_period_end": True}) ``` --- ### `capture_payment_intent` :warning: Capture a Stripe PaymentIntent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `payment_intent_id` | `string` | Yes | The PaymentIntent ID to capture. | | `amount_to_capture` | `string` | No | Amount to capture in the smallest currency unit. Omit to capture the full authorized amount. | **Returns:** `PaymentIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_capture_payment_intent", {"payment_intent_id": "your-payment_intent_id", "amount_to_capture": "your-amount_to_capture"}) ``` --- ### `close_dispute` :warning: Close a Stripe dispute | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dispute_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe dispute ID to close. | **Returns:** `StripeDispute` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_close_dispute", {"dispute_id": "your-dispute_id"}) ``` --- ### `confirm_payment_intent` :warning: Confirm a Stripe PaymentIntent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `payment_intent_id` | `string` | Yes | The PaymentIntent ID to confirm. | | `payment_method` | `string` | No | Optional payment method ID to attach before confirming (e.g. ``pm_...``). | **Returns:** `PaymentIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_confirm_payment_intent", {"payment_intent_id": "your-payment_intent_id", "payment_method": "your-payment_method"}) ``` --- ### `create_charge` :warning: Create a charge | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `amount` | `integer` | Yes | Amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents). | | `currency` | `string` | Yes | Three-letter ISO currency code (e.g. ``usd``). | | `customer` | `string` | No | Customer ID to charge. | | `source` | `string` | No | Payment source token or ID. | | `description` | `string` | No | Arbitrary description for the charge. | | `metadata` | `string` | No | Key-value metadata to attach. | **Returns:** `StripeCharge` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_charge", {"amount": 10, "currency": "your-currency", "customer": "your-customer", "source": "your-source"}) ``` --- ### `create_checkout_session` :warning: Create a Stripe Checkout Session | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `line_items` | `array` | Yes | List of line item dicts, each with ``price`` (price ID) and ``quantity``. Example: ``[{"price": "price_xxx", "quantity": 1}]``. | | `mode` | `string` | Yes | Checkout mode: ``payment``, ``subscription``, or ``setup``. | | `success_url` | `string` | Yes | URL to redirect to after successful payment. | | `cancel_url` | `string` | Yes | URL to redirect to if the user cancels. | **Returns:** `StripeCheckoutSession` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_checkout_session", {"line_items": "your-line_items", "mode": "your-mode", "success_url": "your-success_url", "cancel_url": "your-cancel_url"}) ``` --- ### `create_customer` :warning: Create a new Stripe customer | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | No | Customer email address. | | `name` | `string` | No | Customer full name. | | `description` | `string` | No | Arbitrary description for the customer. | | `metadata` | `string` | No | Key-value metadata to attach to the customer. | **Returns:** `StripeCustomer` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_customer", {"email": "user@example.com", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `create_payment_intent` :warning: Create a Stripe PaymentIntent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `amount` | `integer` | Yes | Amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents). | | `currency` | `string` | Yes | Three-letter ISO currency code (e.g. ``usd``). | | `customer` | `string` | No | Optional customer ID to associate with the intent. | | `description` | `string` | No | Arbitrary description for the payment. | **Returns:** `PaymentIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_payment_intent", {"amount": 10, "currency": "your-currency", "customer": "your-customer", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_payout` :warning: Create a payout to your bank account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `amount` | `integer` | Yes | Amount to pay out in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents). | | `currency` | `string` | Yes | Three-letter ISO currency code (e.g. ``usd``). | **Returns:** `StripePayout` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_payout", {"amount": 10, "currency": "your-currency"}) ``` --- ### `create_price` :warning: Create a Stripe price | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product` | `string` | Yes | The product ID to attach this price to. | | `unit_amount` | `integer` | Yes | Price amount in cents. | | `currency` | `string` | Yes | Three-letter ISO currency code (e.g. ``usd``). | | `recurring_interval` | `string` | No | If set, makes this a recurring price. One of ``day``, ``week``, ``month``, or ``year``. | **Returns:** `StripePrice` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_price", {"product": "your-product", "unit_amount": 10, "currency": "your-currency", "recurring_interval": "your-recurring_interval"}) ``` --- ### `create_product` :warning: Create a Stripe product | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Product name. | | `description` | `string` | No | Product description. | | `metadata` | `string` | No | Key-value metadata to attach. | **Returns:** `StripeProduct` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_product", {"name": "my-name", "description": "your-description", "metadata": "your-metadata"}) ``` --- ### `create_setup_intent` Create a Stripe SetupIntent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | No | Optional customer ID to attach the payment method to. | | `payment_method_types` | `string` | No | List of payment method types to accept (e.g. ``["card"]``). Defaults to Stripe's automatic detection if omitted. | **Returns:** `StripeSetupIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_setup_intent", {"customer": "your-customer", "payment_method_types": "your-payment_method_types"}) ``` --- ### `create_subscription` :warning: Create a subscription | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | Yes | The customer ID to subscribe. | | `price` | `string` | Yes | The price ID for the subscription. | | `trial_days` | `string` | No | Number of days for a free trial period. | **Returns:** `StripeSubscription` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_subscription", {"customer": "your-customer", "price": "your-price", "trial_days": "your-trial_days"}) ``` --- ### `delete_customer` :warning: Delete a Stripe customer | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe customer ID (e.g. ``cus_...``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_delete_customer", {"customer_id": "your-customer_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_balance` Retrieve the current Stripe account balance **Returns:** `StripeBalance` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_balance", {}) ``` --- ### `get_charge` Retrieve a single Stripe charge by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `charge_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe charge ID (e.g. ``ch_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeCharge` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_charge", {"charge_id": "your-charge_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_customer` Retrieve a single Stripe customer by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe customer ID (e.g. ``cus_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeCustomer` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_customer", {"customer_id": "your-customer_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_dispute` Retrieve a single Stripe dispute by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dispute_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe dispute ID (e.g. ``dp_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeDispute` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_dispute", {"dispute_id": "your-dispute_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_event` Retrieve a single Stripe event by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe event ID (e.g. ``evt_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_event", {"event_id": "your-event_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_invoice` Retrieve a single Stripe invoice by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `invoice_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe invoice ID (e.g. ``in_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeInvoice` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_invoice", {"invoice_id": "your-invoice_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_payment_intent` Retrieve a single Stripe PaymentIntent by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `payment_intent_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe PaymentIntent ID (e.g. ``pi_...``). | **Returns:** `PaymentIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_payment_intent", {"payment_intent_id": "your-payment_intent_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_payout` Retrieve a single Stripe payout by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `payout_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe payout ID (e.g. ``po_...``). | **Returns:** `StripePayout` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_payout", {"payout_id": "your-payout_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_setup_intent` Retrieve a single Stripe SetupIntent by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `setup_intent_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe SetupIntent ID (e.g. ``seti_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeSetupIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_setup_intent", {"setup_intent_id": "your-setup_intent_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_subscription` Retrieve a single Stripe subscription by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subscription_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe subscription ID (e.g. ``sub_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeSubscription` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_subscription", {"subscription_id": "your-subscription_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_charges` List charges from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | No | Filter charges by customer ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of charges to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Charge ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeCharge]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_charges", {"customer": "your-customer", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_customers` List customers from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of customers to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Customer ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeCustomer]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_customers", {"limit": 10, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_disputes` List disputes from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of disputes to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Dispute ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeDispute]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_disputes", {"limit": 10, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_events` List events from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `type` | `string` | No | Filter by event type (e.g. ``charge.succeeded``, ``invoice.payment_failed``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of events to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Event ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_events", {"type": "your-type", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_invoices` List invoices from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | No | Filter invoices by customer ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of invoices to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Invoice ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeInvoice]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_invoices", {"customer": "your-customer", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_payment_intents` List PaymentIntents from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | No | Filter by customer ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of PaymentIntents to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | PaymentIntent ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PaymentIntent]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_payment_intents", {"customer": "your-customer", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_payment_methods` List payment methods for a customer | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | Yes | The customer ID to list payment methods for. | | `type` | `string` | No | Filter by payment method type (e.g. ``card``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of payment methods to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Payment method ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripePaymentMethod]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_payment_methods", {"customer": "your-customer", "type": "your-type", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_payouts` List payouts from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of payouts to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Payout ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripePayout]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_payouts", {"limit": 10, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_prices` List prices from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product` | `string` | No | Filter prices by product ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of prices to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Price ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripePrice]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_prices", {"product": "your-product", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_products` List products from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of products to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Product ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeProduct]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_products", {"limit": 10, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_refunds` List refunds from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `charge` | `string` | No | Filter refunds by charge ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of refunds to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Refund ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeRefund]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_refunds", {"charge": "your-charge", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_subscriptions` List subscriptions from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | No | Filter by customer ID. | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (e.g. ``active``, ``past_due``, ``canceled``, ``all``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of subscriptions to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Subscription ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeSubscription]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_subscriptions", {"customer": "your-customer", "status": "active"}) ``` --- ### `refund_charge` :warning: Refund a charge | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `charge_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the charge to refund. | | `amount` | `string` | No | Amount to refund in cents. Omit for full refund. | | `reason` | `string` | No | Reason for the refund: ``duplicate``, ``fraudulent``, or ``requested_by_customer``. | **Returns:** `StripeRefund` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_refund_charge", {"charge_id": "your-charge_id", "amount": "your-amount", "reason": "your-reason"}) ``` --- ### `update_customer` :warning: Update a Stripe customer | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe customer ID (e.g. ``cus_...``). | | `email` | `string` | No | New email address for the customer. | | `name` | `string` | No | New name for the customer. | | `description` | `string` | No | New description for the customer. | | `metadata` | `string` | No | Key-value metadata to set on the customer. | **Returns:** `StripeCustomer` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_update_customer", {"customer_id": "your-customer_id", "email": "user@example.com", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `void_invoice` :warning: Void a Stripe invoice | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `invoice_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe invoice ID to void. | **Returns:** `StripeInvoice` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_void_invoice", {"invoice_id": "your-invoice_id"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 100 read/sec, 100 write/sec — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`cancel_subscription`, `capture_payment_intent`, `close_dispute`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses - This connector has 40 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Plaid](../plaid/) — Banking data --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

supabase.readme_md: # Supabase > Open-source Firebase alternative with Postgres ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Database-10B981) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-16-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/supabase/supabase-py?style=social)](https://github.com/supabase/supabase-py) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/supabase/supabase-py) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Supabase Inc. | | **Category** | Database | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [supabase.com](https://supabase.com) | | **API Docs** | [supabase.com](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/api) | | **Auth** | API Key (anon/service_role) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Pro from $25/month | --- ## Overview The Supabase API provides RESTful access to your Postgres database, authentication, and storage. Query tables, manage rows, handle file uploads, and build full-stack applications with Supabase's auto-generated API. ## Use Cases - Database CRUD operations - User authentication - File storage - Real-time subscriptions - Serverless backends ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[supabase]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SUPABASE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["supabase"], credentials={"supabase": "your-token"}) # Get a single record from a Supabase table by ID result = kit.execute("supabase_get_record", {"table": "your-table", "id": "your-id"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["supabase"], credentials={"supabase": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["supabase"], credentials={"supabase": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key (anon/service_role) 1. Create an account at [Supabase](https://supabase.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://supabase.com/dashboard/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("supabase_get_record", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `auth_sign_up` :warning: Sign up a new user via Supabase Auth | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | Yes | The new user's email address. | | `password` | `string` | Yes | The new user's password. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_auth_sign_up", {"email": "user@example.com", "password": "your-password"}) ``` --- ### `call_edge_function` Call a Supabase Edge Function | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | The edge function name. | | `body` | `string` | No | Optional JSON body to send to the function. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_call_edge_function", {"name": "my-name", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `count_exact` Get an exact count of records in a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `filter` | `string` | No | Optional PostgREST filter dict. | **Returns:** `int` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_count_exact", {"table": "your-table", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `count_records` Count records in a table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Name of the table. | | `filter` | `string` | No | PostgREST filter dict (e.g. ``{"status": "eq.active"}``). | **Returns:** `int` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_count_records", {"table": "your-table", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `delete_record` :warning: Delete a record from a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `id` | `string` | Yes | Primary key value of the record to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_delete_record", {"table": "your-table", "id": "your-id"}) ``` --- ### `get_record` Get a single record from a Supabase table by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `id` | `string` | Yes | Primary key value of the record. | **Returns:** `SupabaseRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_get_record", {"table": "your-table", "id": "your-id"}) ``` --- ### `insert_many` :warning: Insert multiple records into a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `records` | `array` | Yes | List of dicts, each mapping column names to values. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_insert_many", {"table": "your-table", "records": "your-records"}) ``` --- ### `insert_record` Insert a record into a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `data` | `object` | Yes | Dict of column-name to value for the new row. | **Returns:** `SupabaseRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_insert_record", {"table": "your-table", "data": "your-data"}) ``` --- ### `list_tables` List tables available in the Supabase project **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_list_tables", {}) ``` --- ### `query_table` Query records from a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `select` | `string` | No | Comma-separated column names (PostgREST select). | | `filter` | `string` | No | Dict of column-name to PostgREST filter expression. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of records to return. (default: `100`) | | `offset` | `integer` | No | Number of records to skip. (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SupabaseRecord]` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_query_table", {"table": "your-table", "select": "your-select", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `rpc` Call a Supabase RPC (stored procedure) | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `function_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the Postgres function. | | `params` | `string` | No | Keyword arguments to pass to the function. | **Returns:** `SupabaseRPCResult` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_rpc", {"function_name": "your-function_name", "params": "your-params"}) ``` --- ### `select_columns` Select specific columns from a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `columns` | `array` | Yes | List of column names to include in the response. | | `filter` | `string` | No | Optional PostgREST filter dict. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of records to return. (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_select_columns", {"table": "your-table", "columns": "your-columns", "filter": "your-filter", "limit": 100}) ``` --- ### `update_record` Update a record in a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `id` | `string` | Yes | Primary key value of the record to update. | | `data` | `object` | Yes | Dict of column-name to new value. | **Returns:** `SupabaseRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_update_record", {"table": "your-table", "id": "your-id", "data": "your-data"}) ``` --- ### `update_records` Update records matching a filter in a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `filter_column` | `string` | Yes | Column to filter on. | | `filter_value` | `string` | Yes | PostgREST filter expression (e.g. ``"eq.active"``). | | `data` | `object` | Yes | Dict of column-name to new value. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_update_records", {"table": "your-table", "filter_column": "your-filter_column", "filter_value": "your-filter_value", "data": "your-data"}) ``` --- ### `upsert_record` Upsert a record into a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `data` | `object` | Yes | Dict of column-name to value. | **Returns:** `SupabaseRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_upsert_record", {"table": "your-table", "data": "your-data"}) ``` --- ### `upsert_records` :warning: Upsert records into a table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Name of the table. | | `records` | `array` | Yes | List of record dicts to upsert. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_upsert_records", {"table": "your-table", "records": "your-records"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by plan — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`auth_sign_up`, `delete_record`, `insert_many`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Airtable](../airtable/) — Spreadsheet database - [Firestore](../firestore/) — Document database - [Mongodb](../mongodb/) — NoSQL database - [Redis](../redis/) — Key-value store --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

teams.readme_md: # Microsoft Teams > Team collaboration, messaging, and meetings ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Communication-3B82F6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-17-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-python?style=social)](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-python) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Microsoft | | **Category** | Communication | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [teams.microsoft.com](https://teams.microsoft.com) | | **API Docs** | [learn.microsoft.com](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/teams-api-overview) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0 (Microsoft Graph) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by endpoint | | **Pricing** | Included with Microsoft 365 | --- ## Overview The Microsoft Teams API (via Microsoft Graph) provides access to teams, channels, messages, and members. Send messages, manage team membership, create channels, and build collaboration workflows for Microsoft 365. ## Use Cases - Team messaging automation - Channel management - Meeting scheduling - Notification bots - Workflow integrations ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[teams]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_TEAMS_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["teams"], credentials={"teams": "your-token"}) # List messages in a Teams channel result = kit.execute("teams_list_messages", {"team_id": "T01234567", "channel_id": "C01234567", "limit": 50, "page_url": "your-page_url"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["teams"], credentials={"teams": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["teams"], credentials={"teams": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 (Microsoft Graph) 1. Create an account at [Microsoft Teams](https://teams.microsoft.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_RegisteredApps) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("teams_list_messages", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_member` :warning: Add a member to a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The Azure AD object ID of the user to add. | | `roles` | `string` | No | Optional list of roles to assign (e.g. ``["owner"]``). Defaults to member if omitted. | **Returns:** `TeamsMember` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_add_member", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "user_id": "user-123", "roles": "your-roles"}) ``` --- ### `create_channel` Create a new channel in a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the new channel. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional description for the channel. | **Returns:** `TeamsChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_create_channel", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `delete_channel` :warning: Delete a channel from a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_delete_channel", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `get_channel` Get a single channel by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel. | **Returns:** `TeamsChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_get_channel", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `get_team` Get a single team by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | **Returns:** `Team` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_get_team", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_user_presence` Get a user's presence status in Teams | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the user. | **Returns:** `TeamsPresence` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_get_user_presence", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_channels` List channels in a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_channels", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_chat_messages` List messages in a Teams chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the chat. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of messages per page (max 50). (default: `50`) | | `page_url` | `string` | No | Full ``@odata.nextLink`` URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[TeamsMessage]` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_chat_messages", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "limit": 50, "page_url": "your-page_url"}) ``` --- ### `list_chats` List chats the user is part of | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `string` | No | Maximum number of chats to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_chats", {"limit": "your-limit"}) ``` --- ### `list_members` List members of a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_members", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_messages` List messages in a Teams channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of messages per page (max 50). (default: `50`) | | `page_url` | `string` | No | Full ``@odata.nextLink`` URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[TeamsMessage]` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_messages", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567", "limit": 50, "page_url": "your-page_url"}) ``` --- ### `list_teams` List teams the user has joined **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_teams", {}) ``` --- ### `remove_member` :warning: Remove a member from a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `member_id` | `string` | Yes | The membership ID of the member to remove (this is the conversation member ID, not the user ID). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_remove_member", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "member_id": "your-member_id"}) ``` --- ### `reply_to_message` :warning: Reply to a message in a Teams channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the message to reply to. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Reply content (HTML supported). | **Returns:** `TeamsMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_reply_to_message", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `send_chat_message` :warning: Send a message to a Teams chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the chat. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Message content (HTML supported). | **Returns:** `TeamsMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_send_chat_message", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `send_message` :warning: Send a message to a Teams channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Message content (HTML supported). | **Returns:** `TeamsMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_send_message", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `update_channel` Update a channel in a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel. | | `display_name` | `string` | No | New display name for the channel. | | `description` | `string` | No | New description for the channel. | **Returns:** `TeamsChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_update_channel", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567", "display_name": "your-display_name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by endpoint — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_member`, `delete_channel`, `remove_member`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Gmail](../gmail/) — Email automation - [Slack](../slack/) — Team messaging - [Discord](../discord/) — Community messaging - [Telegram](../telegram/) — Bot messaging --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*
